author: Antonio Acevedo
title: Wish 0 star was an option!
body:
Wish 0 star was an option!
Car had lots of cosmetic damages along with a bad cigarette smell and squeaky breaks and a radio that didn’t work.
From day 1 the tire pressure light came on and after multiple attempts of filling the light never went away and after 5 days of driving the car started flashing warning lights that oil was low and the car started blowing smoke for the exhaust! After stopping and checking oil level which was extremely low I contact the host and expressed that I was uncomfortable driving the vehicle in that condition.
The host advised me to drive 40 minutes out of the area that I was in and loose time on activities with family.
After arriving the host filled up the oil and said the car was “100% good to go”. I then left and not even 3 minutes of driving the car started to smoke heavily and I returned. The host did a test drive and they confirmed that the car was in no mechanical condition to be driven. I mentioned the host my concerns as this car has multiple bad reviews with oil consumption, tire & brake issues.
The host verbally agreed to refund half my reservation and I got on the line with Turo to find a replacement. After Turo reached out the host stated they would open a claim for damages on the vehicle despite multiple reviews with the same issues and problems.
The host then refused to refund what was agreed upon. All in all this host does not maintain or keep vehicles up to standard mechanically or physically. DO NOT RENT!!!

author: Priyal Parekh
title: I had a very disappointing experience…
body: I had a very disappointing experience with this trip overall. I booked a regular SUV nearly a month in advance, only for it to be cancelled just a few hours before my trip, forcing me to scramble for alternatives through customer support at the last minute. I was given a luxury vehicle as a replacement, which significantly increased my expenses due to premium gas requirements and, later, a very costly tire replacement after a sidewall gash during the trip. To make matters worse, the car was not properly cleaned inside or outside at pickup. The experience with customer support was equally frustrating, as the representative I spoke with was rude, dismissive, and showed very little accountability or empathy for the situation. What should have been a smooth and budgeted trip became unnecessarily stressful and expensive due to circumstances completely outside of my control, and I expected a far better level of professionalism and customer care from Turo.

author: Bri
title: Paid for Enhanced Roadside Assistance and Received Chaos
body:
I rarely leave negative reviews, but my recent experience with Turo was honestly one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve had in a very long time.

I rented a vehicle and paid extra for Enhanced Roadside Assistance specifically for situations like this. During my trip, the front passenger tire suddenly went flat after a short 20-minute drive. I parked the car briefly, came back, and the tire was completely deflated. There was no damage to the rim, no visible puncture, no nail, and even the roadside mechanic later said the tire appeared repairable.

What should have been a straightforward roadside assistance situation turned into an exhausting 18+ hour nightmare filled with misinformation, contradictions, broken promises, and extremely poor communication.

I spent HOURS on the phone with multiple agents. One representative told me I could return the vehicle early and receive a refund or price match for another vehicle despite my reservation being non-refundable. Another representative confirmed the same thing. Later, the “Experience Team” completely contradicted them and told me I would receive no refund at all because my reservation was non-refundable.

Not only was I repeatedly given false information, but every time I spoke to a new person, I had to explain the entire situation all over again because no proper notes were being added to my case.

Roadside assistance itself was completely disorganized:

I was promised a tow truck, but instead a mechanic arrived in a personal vehicle.
The spare tire was low on air, and I was told inflating it required an entirely separate authorization request.
I had to call back AGAIN late at night and wait on hold just to authorize air for the spare tire.
One representative was so confused she thought I was either the host or the roadside provider instead of the guest.
I was promised a tow for 9:00 AM the next morning. Nobody showed up.
After waiting around all morning, I finally called the towing company myself and was told delays were happening because of Eid al-Adha, but nobody from Turo or the towing company had proactively informed me.
Later, I was told the tow would not happen until around 7:00 PM.
Then I found out the vehicle wouldn’t even be brought directly to the host, who was actually willing to repair the car that same day.

Meanwhile, I was still actively paying for a vehicle I could not use.

The most frustrating part is that the host was actually helpful and cooperative throughout this entire situation. The problem was entirely the lack of coordination and professionalism from Turo support and their roadside partners.

If this is what “Enhanced Roadside Assistance” looks like, then it is incredibly disappointing and unprofessional. The amount of time, stress, confusion, and inconvenience this caused was unacceptable.

This experience has seriously damaged my trust in Turo, and I will definitely think twice before ever using the platform again.

author: Seena
title: Unexpected cancellations
body: I've been using this company for several years without issues, as long as I rent short-term. However, I encountered a problem when I rented a car for 50 days long-term. I booked it three months in advance to secure a good price since long-term rentals are usually difficult. Five days before my trip, my reservation was canceled. As expected, I couldn't find any car within the original price range, and I had to pay 90% more for the second booking. I contacted customer service to ask if they could offer a coupon, discount, or credit for the higher cost, but they simply replied that there is nothing they can do. I learned that, unlike with insurance companies, where prices are locked in and cars are always available, I should never rent a long-term drive on Turo again. Also their customer service is awful. they are not helpful at all.

author: clean outlook
title: great experiance gone bad
body:
My experience started off as a 5+ rating, but unfortunately it quickly went sour. At around 5:30 a.m., in a dark airport parking lot, I accidentally parked in the wrong spot. Shortly after, I received a rude and nasty text from the host questioning why I hadn’t followed their parking instructions.
I tried to explain that while they provided instructions, the app itself guided me to a different area, which caused the confusion. Instead of understanding, the host responded in a very condescending manner, saying that if I had “read better,” the situation could have been avoided. They also informed me that I would be responsible for any improper parking fees—which I had no issue accepting, since I acknowledged the mistake.
What bothered me was the tone and attitude. There was no reason to be so rude or dismissive. As someone who works in the hospitality industry, I understand the importance of customer service, and this interaction fell far below acceptable standards.
I will not be using this host again. The conversation is documented in the app’s messaging system, and I would hope it’s reviewed, because this kind of behavior shouldn’t go unaddressed.

title: ETT rentals is one of my go-tos.
body:
ETT rentals is one of my go-tos.
His vehicles are well maintained and always clean inside and out. 
I would recommend his vehicles to anyone renting via Turo.... my daughter has even rented from him as well. 
Communication is easy and effortless with a reply within minutes of inquiries.

author: Orion_7
title: Fine, could have done better.
body:
The car pickup was strange it was just unlocked parallel parked in front of a closed car dealership. 
Also the car was pretty dirty inside, lots of crumbs and bits of dirt and food all over. As well as only having like barely a 1/3 tank of gas upon pickup. 
It was just gross inside and having to put gas in it literally the second time we drove it to get on our trip was annoying. Along with this the pickup instructions just gave us a location and I got like 3 seperate messages about not smoking in the car, but by the time I got to the car I had no instructions for how to get the key, the car was just unlocked sitting on the road. Thankfully the host answered on my first call and let me know the key was just simply sitting on top of the front tire. Not a great experience for pickup overall.  

Communication with the host was good, they answered both my calls.

I was sure that if an issue arose that I could rely on Rob to help. Fortunately all went well. The vehicle was clean, comfortable, well maintained and really fun to drive. author: Laura Parker
title: The process with the owners is…
body: The process with the owners is excellent. The cars have been great. However, the inflexibility Turo shows with regards to minor schedule changes is less so. If you go one minute over a day, you get charged another full day - and they try and blame it on the owners who tell you they have zero control to accept/waive the surcharge. Also, watch your credit card statements: they hit my credit card (no email, no notice, no receipt) for a "change in the protection plan" when all I did was shorten my trip by 60 minutes. I had to contact them to fight it and then they didn't even refund the full amount they charged.

author: Skye Kent
title: I found this to be an easy process
body:
I found this to be an easy process. Our host had very clear instructions and a very clean vehicle. We made arrangements to pick our car up at the airport and then to drop it back off there too...and a shuttle took us to and from both times. 
The car itself was clean, ran well and had plenty of room for all of our stuff. The only thing that was odd was the GPS - I gave her a name but can't repeat it here. Well, and the actual app for Turo has some quirks, but overall everything worked out.
As far as using Turo for a "rental" vehicle, this was an easy process from booking to paying to taking the actual trip. I read through previous user reviews of my host to ensure I was choosing a good one and chose a car that I knew I'd be comfortable driving (because I own the same kind - just a few years older). I'll definitely use this service again!

author: Richard Barnett
title: My original host canceled my vehicle 10…
body:
My original host canceled my vehicle 10 minutes after landing at LAX (from Louisville).
When I called Turo about the problem, the customer service representative was nice and helpful, but he could not get me a replacement EV at anything close to my original price, so I had to settle for a hybrid AND pay $50 more than my original vehicle!
The replacement took two hours to get, leaving us stranded at LAX, and made us late for a dinner reservation with our son.
A last moment cancellation on a customer should never make the customer pay more…Turo or the host should pay the difference!
The second vehicle served us well.

author: Rae's Media Creation
title: I had an extremely frustrating…
body:
I had an extremely frustrating experience with Turo while trying to rent a vehicle for my anniversary. I attempted to rent a vehicle through Turo and selected Affirm as my payment option. Affirm showed that I had a scheduled payment, but Turo showed that I had no upcoming trip.

I contacted Turo support and was told there was no trip planned under my account. The problem is that no one could give me any confirmation number, cancellation number, or clear documentation because they said there was no trip. Meanwhile, Affirm still showed a payment connected to Turo. That left me stuck between Turo and Affirm with no clear answer and no rental.

After that, I still could not rent from Turo at all. I tried using a different card, a different phone number, and even a private browser window, but I still could not complete a booking. Turo could not clearly explain whether my account was restricted, whether there was a verification issue, whether it was connected to the Affirm problem, or what steps I needed to take to fix it.

This was not a casual rental. This was for a special anniversary trip, and the lack of clear communication completely ruined the experience. Turo should not allow a customer to end up with a payment issue through Affirm while showing no actual trip, then fail to explain why the customer can no longer rent.

Very disappointing customer service, poor communication, and no real resolution. I would be very cautious using Turo, especially if you are renting for an important event or using Affirm as a payment option.

author: Richard Zimmerman
title: I have not rented a car in the last 30…
body: I have not rented a car in the last 30 years  . . . HONESTLY!!! Because of my inexperience I rented a Mini Cooper for 25.5 hours costing $187+. What happened: the rental was from 10am until 10am the following day. The car to be picked up and returned to the short term lot at PIE airport, FL. Things got a little tight for the 10am return time. I texted Turo to ask if it was OK to return the car at 11:30am. The response was "that will be fine" and nothing further was advised. Knowing what I know now I honestly have no issue with your rental policies. My issue is that at the time of the request I should have been advised that to gain 1.5 hours I would be charged another day's rental, $93.51. Had I realized the 24 hour policy, I would certainly found a way to return the car by 10am. That is why I am upset with the way you folks do business and will be reluctant to use your company in the future.

author: Kevin Lee
title: Dear Turo Executive Support and…
body:
Dear Turo Executive Support and Customer Experience teams,

I’m requesting an executive review regarding Reservation #57151139 due to what appears to be a contradictory platform workflow and misleading operational guidance that resulted in an improper return charge.

I’ve already gone through standard support channels and received a denial. However, I do not believe the substance of the issue was fully evaluated.

My concern is not primarily the dollar amount. My concern is that the Turo app workflow, host instructions, and operational reality conflicted in a way that created a predictable guest failure scenario.

Summary of concerns:

1. Misleading airport pickup representation BY HOST

The listing and booking flow represented the pickup as Philadelphia International Airport (attached). However, the actual pickup process required transportation to a third-party off-airport parking facility (“The Parking Spot North”), including shuttle logistics, additional wait time, and a non-standard pickup/exit process that was materially different from a typical airport pickup experience that are NOT DISCLOSED prior to airport arrival and vehicle check-in.

As someone who has rented through Turo multiple times in multiple cities, including other airport-adjacent Turo experiences, this setup was significantly less transparent than other airport listings I’ve used (e.g. San Diego Airport utilizing near-airport locations)

2. Contradictory return instructions

The host later messaged that returning the vehicle anywhere other than Parking Spot North would result in fees.

However, during the actual Turo app return workflow, the app presented multiple approved return locations, including airport parking garages. I selected Garage D through the Turo app workflow, and the app allowed me to continue through checkout without warning that this selection would trigger an “improper return” fee.

I reasonably relied on the  platform workflow presented by Turo during checkout.

If the app permitted a prohibited location selection without warning, I do not believe the resulting fee should be imposed on the guest.

3. Inability to recreate app evidence after trip completion

Support denied my appeal largely because I could not provide screenshots of the checkout workflow. Unfortunately, once the trip was completed, I could no longer access or recreate that exact workflow inside the Turo app.

I would ask Turo to independently review:
- the guest checkout workflow presented for this reservation
- the return-location selection options shown during checkout
- whether Garage D was presented as an approved/selectable option
- whether any warning was shown that selecting Garage D would still trigger an improper-return fee

Requested resolution:
- reversal of the improper-return related charges
- internal review of the contradictory guest workflow
- review of whether this host’s airport representation and operational flow align with Turo marketplace standards

I’ve been a repeat Turo customer and would strongly prefer to resolve this professionally and directly with Turo rather than escalate externally.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

305 Auto Rental (Miami) - On TURO

Chevrolet Camaro 2023 - White Convertible

Rented for a week vacation covering all the Florida Keys. The car was spotless and fantastic to drive with top down. Handled very well and fun to drive!!! Limited room for luggage as top folds into 2/3rds of trunk, so use of full back seat for suitcases was necessary. Tight  visibility with back window on Camaros when top is up. Otherwise a fun and fantastic car and the host at 305 Auto Rentals made sure the entire trip was smooth and seamless. Effective and friendly communication. My experience using Turo was quite good. The car booking process was easy and there was a wide selection of vehicles at a wide range of prices. The app was also user-friendly and easy to use.

author: J W
title: I am filing this complaint due to…
body:
I am filing this complaint due to Turo’s failure to resolve a dispute regarding a $50 promotional credit and their failure to uphold a commitment made by their support team.
On May 23, 2026, I canceled a car rental reservation for an upcoming August 2026 trip. Because this was canceled well in advance, there was no financial impact on the host or the platform. However, Turo failed to refund the $50 promotional credit applied to this booking.
When I contacted support, I was informed that the credit was forfeited and that I would essentially have to wait until next year to receive another one—a highly unreasonable response for a loyal customer of five years. I was then given escalation case number 20260523-8Q0TGW and was explicitly promised that a representative would contact me within 24 hours to discuss issuing the $50 as a travel credit.
As of May 25, 2026, no one has reached out to me, and the issue remains unresolved. I am seeking the immediate reinstatement of my $50 promotional credit. I believe that ignoring a customer's escalation case is poor business practice and violates the commitment to service that a company of Turo's size should uphold.

author: Timothee
title: I had what initially seemed like a…
body:
I had what initially seemed like a great experience using Turo—until a dispute arose.

I rented a vehicle from May 18–21, but returned it early on May 20 at 9:09 PM. I parked the vehicle in the same back parking area where I originally picked it up, left the visitor parking pass displayed exactly as it was when I received the vehicle, refilled the gas tank, secured the car, and immediately notified the host through the Turo app. I also provided timestamped photos showing exactly where the vehicle was parked.

The host read my message, thanked me, and even asked me to leave a 5-star review. At no point did they mention any issue with the parking location or ask me to move the vehicle.

The next day, I was hit with an “improper return” charge, including fees and admin costs.

I appealed with Turo, providing screenshots of the in-app messages, acknowledgment from the host, and photos documenting the return. Despite this evidence, Turo denied the appeal.

My issue is less about the amount charged and more about the principle: if a host acknowledges receipt of a properly returned vehicle without objection, then later makes a contradictory claim, what meaningful protection does the guest actually have?

I wanted to like this platform because the convenience is great, but this experience left me with serious concerns about how disputes are evaluated and whether guest-provided evidence is given fair consideration

author: Arvil Webster
title: First time Turo, not impressed.
body: So, i'm new to the platform. This rental was my very first rental through Turo - I typically do the rental counter to be honest, and, if i'm able to get back in that position, I will continue using the rental counter. All was fine and well, I had a plan to use the Turo for gig work as my vehicle was reposessed 2 weeks ago. I get the car and there is ABSOLUTELY something wrong with it. I actually did message the owner and was like hey, i'm not going on any kind of road trip or anything, but is the car ok? It feels like it's having a really hard time shifting. Long story short, I was so uncomfortable driving the car that I did not even do what I was planning on doing with it. The whole week was a wash and I literally lost $200. When I realized what a horrible situation I was in, I, VERY reluctantly, requested an extension for one day so I could do one more gig route. It wasn't worth it. The cost of the extension plus the gas to do the gig work left me at a whopping $0. I could have done more gig work, but you can see through my rental that I literally used almost less than half of the miles allowed. It was a poor experience and i'm reluctant to try it out again. And yes, I gave the host and car 5 stars for everything, because i'm new, and it would have beens uper easy for the host to rate me poorly. I don'tk now that I will use Turo in the future, but, I don't want any kind of negative rating on my name.

author: Angibabii
title: I think Turo should be more aware of…
body:
I think Turo should be more aware of vehicle maintenance, like registration & tags on cars being 3 months behind without current info in the vehicle. Which could cause undo harm on the renter by being pulled over. Small damage, should be noted & captured via photo on the vehicles being rented. All too many times especially with Speedie cars have interior damage or exterior damage, huge dents, & dings, & who wants to pay more than a rental ar company to rent a car that has damage outside? This is often not disclosed & should be immediately either a discount should be applied to the renter if you pull up & upon documentating the vehicle you see damages that wasn't made clear in the advertisement. 
I also think cars should be vacuumed out before presenting to a renter, not all cars have this isste but some. I'm paying often time more than I would at a rental counter, with limited miles of generally 200 miles a day vs unlimited with a rental company.
Considering this cars should be just as clean, & immaculate.

author: George
title: A nightmare with Turo
body:
I will NEVER use Turo again. I gave them one star simply because there is no zero choice.

I purchased the minimum protection plan and was involved in a car accident with my rented car. A car traveling behind me provided me with camera footage that clearly showed I had no liability at all. I reported the damage to the platform and provided the evidence on the same day, but it was only weeks later that they got back to me.

They made no effort at all to recover the costs from the other party’s insurance. In fact, it was only then that I realized their policy seems to be that the guest pays for the damage no matter who is responsible. This is the first time I have heard of such a ridiculous policy.

Their claims support staff were unprofessional, inefficient, and unfriendly. The whole process lasted half a year, and in the end, I paid the full amount of the excess. If this had been handled by a professional insurance company, I am sure the costs could have been covered by the other party’s insurance.

My advice is to use a professional rental company and don’t be tempted to use Turo. It was a total nightmare!!!

Turo decided to cancel my reservation and only give me a refund. I do not feel like this is the best decision Toro part, for I had no idea that my license was suspended until this morning. Furthermore, I have a Cleveland record and it’s almost perfect driving record, but I cannot resolve this issue until Monday when the DMV is open. I did not receive the service product that I paid for, and now that a mishap is taken place beyond my control, or understanding, I am not only without a car that I fully intended to rent out, I am only given half of what I originally paid for. This feels like a huge scam! Furthermore, I will no longer be utilizing Turo for wanting vehicles in the future. This has left a very bad impression and experience that I wouldn’t wish on anyone.

author: Brittany Walsh
title: Turo Denied Fuel Dispute Despite Extensive Evidence
body:
As a first-time Turo user, this experience significantly changed my perception of the platform’s dispute process.

I returned the vehicle after refueling immediately before airport drop-off. When I parked and turned the vehicle off at return, the fuel gauge was showing full, and I messaged the host directly to let them know the vehicle had been returned with a full tank. I also followed all other checkout instructions and prompts provided through the Turo app.

I provided proof of the fuel purchase, Life360 mileage and location tracking showing I only drove approximately 8.5 miles after fueling, MPG calculations for the vehicle, and direct communication confirming the vehicle was returned full.

However, because I did not take a final dashboard fuel-gauge photo, Turo denied my dispute and stated I had no grounds for reimbursement reversal, despite all other supporting evidence provided.

What concerns me most is that the actual reimbursement amount did not reasonably match the amount of fuel potentially used before drop-off. I spent approximately $21 to refill the vehicle from around half a tank to full. Based on the vehicle’s MPG and the approximately 8.5 miles driven after fueling, the actual amount of fuel used before return would realistically have only been worth approximately $1 at most.

Instead, the host later refueled the vehicle approximately 33 miles away from the airport in a different state and submitted a refueling receipt for approximately $14, which I was then charged for along with additional convenience fees and taxes totaling over $27. The host’s own dashboard photo still showed the fuel gauge extremely close to full.

What also concerns me is that at no point during check-in or checkout was I specifically prompted or clearly warned that a timestamped dashboard fuel-gauge photo/video would effectively be the single most important piece of evidence in a fuel dispute. As a first-time user, I reasonably believed fuel receipts, mileage tracking, timestamps, and communication with the host would also be considered valid supporting documentation.

If Turo’s dispute process ultimately depends almost entirely on a final dashboard fuel photo, then the platform should explicitly require or strongly prompt renters to capture and upload these photos during checkout instead of dismissing all other evidence afterward.

My advice to future renters: over-document absolutely everything at return, including timestamped dashboard fuel and mileage photos/videos, even if you already have receipts and trip tracking data.

title: The cars owner quoted me a price
body:
The cars owner quoted me a price, I submitted  my CC account. #s which held the car for the agreed dates. All went well with the experience until the final CC  paid receipt came and an " Airport parking fee" had been added to the negotiated price. The car was no where near an airport!
I contacted the cars owner, he said take it up with the company, TURO, and "good luck". Im in the process of just that and believe I will be credited with the backing of my credit card company and the documented invoice
When i check with TURO idiiot  AI it said somestimes this fee is "accidentally added.". Would you like to speak to my assistant. Im not kidding! 
The important thing is, REVIEW receipts that arrive weeks or months after an agreement of charges. Dispute false charges with both the rip off company, it'screp and your CC bank.
PS .dont review anything until you get ur CC statement or final receipt submitter to the CC bank.
The last word: I win. Turo loses! 
My CC bank refunded the bogus airport parking fee!

Customer service couldn’t do anything without “proof” yet roadside assistance from Turo told me exactly what to do when I called them yet it wasn’t proof enough to get a refund. The only way to get proof was to drive the car, put myself and everyone else in danger and get evidence that it was not drivable. Absolutely no logic whatsoever.

title: The host I rented the car from is a SCAMMER!!
body: The host I rented the car from, is trying to scam me out of money. The host text me 13.5 hours after I returned the vehicle in great condition, the host said I didn’t fill up the gas tank when I returned it, even though he sent me pictures with the gas tank on full, he tried to charge me $28 dollars, after I spoke to the host and told him that was ridiculous, he cancelled the invoice, that was going to charge me $28 dollars for no reason.  Then he said that there was a crack in the windshield, that I never saw the whole time I had the car, which was parked in my garage when I wasn’t using it.  I sent him photos of the car after I returned, and there was no crack in the windshield.  He argued with me, and made a false claim with Turo stating a cracked windshield.  The Host is a true scammer!!!  I disputed the claim, I’m just waiting to hear back from Turo.

author: rich
title: Overall good
body: My experience is always great when the host is very responsive and easy going, and pickup/drop off is super easy. The car has maintained well, no service issues. I have had bad Turo experiences in the past where the car requires maintenance (ie 2 incidents where the brake service lights are on), and am told it's fine by the hosts. They even gave me a 1 star review after I switched vehicles and requested a refund! I do think Turo should penalize these hosts in some ways to prevent this from happening, because not only do I feel pressured to take the car still, but I also am stranded now without a car for a few hours. It has happened to me ~5 times now where the car is improperly serviced and these are times when I think to myself, I should go with Hertz next time. But overall, I enjoy cars and the good times outweigh the negative

author: Charlene R
title: Turo touts having transparency...NOT!
body: Turo touts having transparency with costs, no hidden fees, etc.  I was charged an additional $45 delivery fee that was not on the initial total charges invoice.  I specifically shopped for car with airport delivery and no additional fee was stated.  I was warned about this habit of turo ahead of time.  I have my total charge invoice which is $45 lower.  Turo made a separate charge to my account of $45.  Since they have your card, they charge it.  Their policy states if you dispute charges with credit card company then they can't address it.  I sent email, no reply.  Go in app and get message "you can't go down this road" when looking to submit dispute.  Extremely disappointed and frustrated at being ripped off and don't seem to have means to resolve it with turo.  Hoping they respond to this review.  Aside from this nontransparent charge, rental was okay.  If this doesnt get resolved, will not use turo again due to hidden fee.

author: Anja-Marie Charles
title: Regrettably
body: Rented a vehicle through Turo using the app. The first issue was that the price was different on my phone vs my wife’s phone. That should have been my sign to use another vendor. But I went ahead with the rental and followed the prompts. I updated my driver’s license as prompted and declined the additional coverage since I have insurance already. The following day I received notification that my rental was canceled and I was being refunded minus an $80 fee. When I contacted Turo I was told repeatedly that I did not follow the pre check-in instructions including providing my drivers license. I sent PROOF that my drivers license was indeed updated in the app and only after that did the Agent admit that it was showing as updated. Suddenly, the issue turned into me not declining the additional coverage. I have proof that I declined that as well but after going back and forth with an Agent who herself seemed confused, I asked for the matter to be escalated. A few mins after ending the chat, I get an email saying I would not be refunded and the decision was final. It’s obvious the decision maker didn’t even take the time to fully investigate the issue. I’m frankly disgusted by the entire ordeal and will never use Turo again. Unless you plan on being frustrated to no end, I’d suggest rethinking their app and services.

author: Gio DePalma
title: Shady Business Practices
body: I bought the enhanced insurance because it said specifically it cover what you’re towing, however, when I got two flat tires on my trailer I was told to figure it out on my own and there’s nothing they could do. Because I wasn’t able to get any quick help it added 3 hours to my trip which meant I was forced to return the truck 3 hours late. Due to this 3 hour delay now Turo wants to charge $200+ for being late after I explained what was going on and talked to the host (who was ok with it being late per my messaging with him). They told me to “extend in the app” when I tried it wouldn’t let me extend which I also explained to the agent but that doesn’t matter to them. I tried calling for support but they told me that I had to pay because I was late no matter the reason and no matter the fact the host said it was ok. When I asked to speak to the manager the person the phone with me (who was extremely hard to understand due to a very thick accent) said that they wouldn’t help and won’t speak to me and after arguing with her she said the manager would give me a call later to discuss and this was 2 days ago and I’ve gotten nothing except for Turo’s constant emails demanding their money. The company I work for has used Turo for years and we will never be returning again after this experience.

author: Janice Lowe
title: Turo From Now On!
body: Renting from Turo is a breath of fresh air after having dealt with the regular rental car companies.  Good renters are appreciated and get better deals than irresponsible ones.  When I rented from 'the others' I was forced to handle things like getting a replacement car fob battery and pumping up low tires on Christmas Eve.  When I returned the vehicle I paid an additional $900 US for fees and had to fight for $100 US credit which they only gave me because I kept asking for a credit back.  Turo is amazing because you deal with the actual owner of the car and not someone doing a job because they have to make money.

author: Roger Acord
title: Ryan’s Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe was…
body:
Ryan’s Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe was absolutely outstanding and played a huge part in making our family trip to Leavenworth such a success.
The rental process was incredibly smooth from start to finish. Ryan was communicative, professional, and made pickup and drop-off effortless. The vehicle was exactly as described—clean, well-maintained, and in excellent condition.
The Grand Cherokee 4xe itself was the perfect vehicle for exploring the area. It was comfortable, spacious, and had plenty of room for our family and luggage. The hybrid system was a nice bonus, providing great fuel efficiency without sacrificing power or capability. Whether we were driving through town, heading to trailheads, or exploring the surrounding mountain roads, the Jeep handled everything with ease and confidence.
What really stood out was how worry-free the entire experience was. Having a reliable, comfortable vehicle allowed us to focus on enjoying our vacation, and Leavenworth was even more enjoyable because of it.
We wouldn’t hesitate to rent from Ryan again and would highly recommend both him and this Jeep to anyone visiting the area. Thanks for helping make an already great trip truly exceptional! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

author: Olivia Moore
title: The vehicle had preexisting cosmetic…
body:
The vehicle had preexisting cosmetic damage, including damage to the front passenger-side bumper, which I documented in my pre-trip photos before driving the car that the host never disclosed. I returned the vehicle with a full tank of gas, yet the host still tried charging me an extra $10 for gas after already making over $500 from my trip, which felt extremely petty and unnecessary. I disputed the charge with Turo and was refunded after they confirmed the car was returned full. However, the host then called back in trying to dispute my refund, forcing me to contact Turo a second time to resolve the issue all over again. I also tried reaching out to the host directly before escalating things, but received no response.
The unnecessary charge caused an overdraft on my account and made the entire experience stressful, inconvenient, and very unprofessional. I would never rent from this host again.

body: Overall I had a really good experience. It was easy to schedule, pricing was better than a rental car location, and the pickup and drop off process was easy. My only complaint is that I was charged a large amount for turning in my car late, even though I communicated with the host and they said it was okay. I didn’t realize I needed to submit on the app for an extension and my host didn’t mention it, so I thought I was good, but then I ended up being charged half of what I had already paid for the extra 2 hours. It was frustrating that they wouldn’t refund me when I emailed and let them know the situation.

title: Thieve and elder abuse
body: I booked a vehicle with TURO on January 4th for a trip to Arizoa on May 27th.I had to cancel the booking on May 20th for health reasons.They refused to offer ANY refund and repeatedly violated HIPA laws with request from my doctor foe a written medical reason to cancel.I have rented vehicles from almost every rental agency in the US and this TURO is the worst I hve every encountered.NO CUSTOMER SERVICE and VERY intrusive Questions They charged me $270 and kept the whole amount DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY.I will be filing reports with BBB and consumer protection and any others I can find.I am a senior citizen (78) and live on SS and can not waste $270.

author: Adam Pacheco
title: I brought the vehicle back and turned…
body:
I brought the vehicle back and turned out, There was a little scuff on the corner of the back bumper.
It's not a dent, it's not a scratch.It's a scuff. They are calling this damage. 
Never mind that the vehicle had previously been in an accident and wasn't disclosed. The fender,not fully attached,and hood were obviously not fixed correctly. 
The host, didn't speak English, so no communication occurred when picking up the vehicle.
I had to buy a windshield wiper and windshield wiper fluid just to get the windshield clean enough to see out of. 
The vehicle drove great and we had a great time while having the vehicle. But all this stuff has added up, and now i'm being charged to fix something that can likely be cleaned off the bumper.
I guess we'll see what happens!!

title: Didn’t like having a set time to return
body:
Didn’t like having a set time to return. I rented the car at a daily rate, as a cruise ship passenger I only estimated the times…. Yet I was told that 5 pm was my return time as that is what I estimated. If it’s daily then I should have for 24 hours without fear of being late due to an estimated time of use. That aspect needs to be clarified as renting a car for 5 hours at $400 need not be threatened with a late return fee.

author: Dez L
title: This was my first time using Turo
body:
This was my first time using Turo, and unfortunately it was not a good experience. I went on a trip to Honolulu and needed a rental last minute. I found a vehicle hosted by Aya in Kaneohe, HI. Initially, booking wasn’t an issue. I completed the ID verification and took the required pre-trip photos of the vehicle.

After returning the vehicle, I messaged the host to let her know I had arrived. She read the message but never responded, so I assumed that was all I needed to do. Later on, I received a message regarding the checkout process asking for additional information and post-trip photos after I had already left the location. I was unaware of this requirement, and I believe the checkout protocol should be clearly available and emphasized at the actual return time. Then after I checked out through turo, I’m sure the host got a notification because she finally got back and said to rate her 5 stars, which I did. 

Then, about 24 hours later, the host claimed I caused damage to the vehicle and sent me a photo that appeared edited. I asked for a video of the damage and also asked if I could return to inspect the vehicle myself, but she denied both requests and became defensive. I was simply trying to understand the situation and get clarity. I know exactly how I left the vehicle.

Also, when I picked up the vehicle, it did not look the way it was presented in the listing photos. In the photos, the car appeared very clean, polished, and well maintained on the outside. However, when I arrived, there were already multiple dents and scratches throughout the vehicle. Because of that, if these false damages are now being claimed against me, it’s difficult to believe any money would actually be going toward repairs on a vehicle that already had visible cosmetic issues and clearly needed work done.

To sum it up, be very mindful. I think it’s best going through rentals through the airport.

TURO has not unlisted the vehicle I rented in Kauai a month ago that had bald tires, cracked window shield (reviews dating back 8 months about windshield), bad wipers, almost dead FOB battery and Check engine light coming on. I see multiple reviews after mine stating the same issues as well as the Host Cancelling within 12 hours of guest arrival a couple times. You can still reserve / rent this vehicle.
I won't even get into what they trying to pull from my boyfriends rental last week in New Mexico.                             I am seriously considering cancelling my Non-refundable reservation for CO in August.

author: Stacy Stately
title: Will never use Turo again
body:
I am a new Turo user and was not fully familiar with the extension process. My scheduled return time was 12:00 PM. I contacted the host directly before the end of my trip to request a one-hour extension, and she verbally agreed, telling me that Turo was closed on weekends and that's why the app wouldn't allow an extension. Based on her approval, I returned the vehicle at 1:00 PM.
I completed all return steps — I took photos, locked the vehicle, and secured the key. I was unaware that my checkout photos had not uploaded correctly until after I had already left for the airport. I corrected this as soon as I realized the error.
I acted in good faith throughout this process. I had the host's verbal approval for the one-hour extension, returned the car on time per that agreement, and completed the checkout process to the best of my ability as a first-time user. I requested that the charges be waived through 2 phone calls and 2 different emails with attached photos of the text messages between myself and the owner as well as time-stamped photos of the car when I dropped it off to no avail.  I am very frustrated and will probably never use Turo again.

author: Adrienne DeMarco
title: Turo is a game changer for travel
body: Turo is a game changer for travel.  From booking to return the entire process is seamlessly easy.  I love how simple it is to change search parameters on the app to find exactly what you’re looking for in a vehicle.  The rate transparency is a welcomed change to traditional rental car companies with no added or unexpected fees.  Pick up and return could not be any better or more convenient with instructions right from the host as soon as you land at the airport, such a huge time saver.  Real time communication with hosts is also so refreshing.  I’ll never use another company for rental car needs again.

author: Carlos Javier
title: Extremely disappointed with my recent…
body:
Extremely disappointed with my recent experience with Turo and their claims process.

During my trip, another driver backed into the vehicle, left a note, accepted responsibility, and provided insurance information. I fully cooperated from the beginning and provided everything Turo needed.

What happened next was honestly shocking.

Turo repeatedly told me the third party’s insurance “denied coverage” because the damages were supposedly below the driver’s deductible. Then, almost immediately after emailing me, they charged $500 directly to my credit card before I had any meaningful opportunity to review documents, dispute the determination, or verify the insurance information myself.

Later, the at-fault driver personally contacted Progressive again and was told there may have been a processing error. According to him, Progressive escalated the claim for payment and even stated the deductible should not apply the way Turo described it. The driver provided me with the Progressive representative’s direct contact information and encouraged Turo to contact them directly.

Despite this contradictory information, Turo chose to:

* finalize the matter internally,
* keep the charge on my account,
* and tell ME to personally recover the money from the at-fault driver myself.

That is incredibly unprofessional.

The entire process felt like an abuse of power by a company that knows it can immediately charge customers first and sort things out later. There was no meaningful dispute process, no neutral review, and no real opportunity to resolve conflicting insurance information before funds were removed from my account.

To make matters worse, the actual damage estimate was only around $681 for relatively minor bumper damage. Yet Turo acted as though the matter was completely settled while conflicting information still existed. 

I understand companies have Terms of Service. That is not the issue. The issue is how poorly and aggressively this claim was handled from a customer-service and fairness standpoint.

I expected professionalism and reasonable investigation. Instead, I got automated charges, contradictory explanations, and zero meaningful opportunity to resolve the situation before my card was charged.

Very disappointing experience.

title: Communicating outside of Turo App and Turo Value
body: Well to start, I had a side conversation with the owner, where he reached out to me and asked me to call him to discuss rental. Upon my return he didn’t have a car wash membership anywhere, like most rentals, so I took it upon myself and took the car to get washed. Apparently there were some superficial scratches from the brush on the tailgate and he submitted a damage fee. On top of that, I reserved it for 3pm, ended up not picking it up until 3:40 pm after communicating that and returning it at 3:45, he submitted a late fee. I have rented over 40 times with Turo and not EVER a single complaint. I know in the future to never communicate outside of Turo and that Turo clearly doesn’t value loyalty to allow such foolishness.

author: Jocelyn Angel
title: No longer using Turo outside the country
body: I usually rent through Turo in California. I was traveling through Canada and rented a vehicle as my experience in California has always been great. Typically i always insure the car with the "premium" insurance as that has no out of pocket charge should the vehicle be damaged in any way. When i booked in Canada, there was no premium insurance, only standard. I purchased the standard and unfortunately while driving on the highway, a pebble hit the hood of the car, scratched and dented it through no fault of our own. The damage was minimal that we didn't notice it. The owner noticed it when we returned the vehicle and charged us $100 for repair. Had we had the option for a premier insurance, the owner could have dealt with it on his own with the Turo insurance. Instead i had to have several conversations with the owner via the app while i was still traveling to other destinations with minimal cell phone reception, which caused me a lot of stress while still on my vacation as i felt the host might think i was trying to ignore the conversation. Additionally, there was a disclaimer once the claim was submitted that the host could not charge me more than what my out of pocket amount is. However, i was unable to find on the app what my out of pocket amount is with the standard insurance. Due to all of this, I will no longer use Turo outside of the country again.

title: Went from good to bad!
body: To start, I am new to the turo experience with this being my first rental. Back in March I had to put my truck in the shop, so to deliver supplies to my sights I rented a truck from this company. The initial experience with this company was not too bad.  My first moment happened when the key fob battery died the second day which had the alarm going off as soon as I opened the door and wouldn’t stop even when I started the vehicle. Luckily I had an extra battery from my own fob which was a match, issue solved. It went smooth from there. (Side bar I rented and returned the vehicle with no problem and let the company k no about the key fob battery replacement with good cheer on both ends) I even needed an extra day since my wife went into labor during the rental which the host was very hospitable and granted an extra rental day. I was very pleased and grateful. My unpleasant issue came today May 19th once I realized there was a back charge for a toll ticket that I was unaware of. I rented the vehicle March 18 until the 23rd so me seeing a charge 2 months later was a surprise, again I am new to turo and hadn’t been on the app since the first and only rental. Once I saw the charge taken from my card I redownloaded the app to see what was going on( forgot to mention that I unloaded the app to save memory space on my phone) when I reached out to the company the rudeness and contempt that came across was so distasteful and unprofessional that I had to write a full review of it. Not sure why I was messaged with such disregard and disrespect but professionalism is always a must and anything else is unethical and unexceptable. This experience went from good to bad!

author: Jennifer V.
title: Turo charges unreasonable late fees!
body: Turo charged me an extra $45 for being 4 minutes past the rental period.  I had returned the car and parked it in the designated area just in time.  Yet I was charged this late fee because I still had to take inside and outside pics of the vehicle to prove I had not damaged it.   If you dont do this, you will be liable for any damages that occur to the vehicle between the time you drop it off and the time the vehicle owner returns to it (which could be whenever they feel like it!).  You must check out officially through the Turo app in order to avoid any late charges.  It's not enough to text the vehicle owner that you had returned the vehicle.  In order to officially "check out", you must upload your in and out pics in the Turo app.  That takes time, which is why I was 4 minutes late.  The vehicle owner had no problem with the extra minutes, but Turo has to make their profit.  When I explained the situation to Turo customer service, their response was that I was still 4 minutes late, and "we now consider this case closed."  How's that for customer service?, especially for a new Turo customer like myself.  I, for one, am not impressed.  I'd rather go through a legitimate, established car rental company than deal with one like Turo that hits you with extra steps before officially returning the vehicle and doesn't make any concessions for these types of circumstances.  Best of luck if you use them.  Don't tell me I didnt warn you!

author: A.E.
title: TURO will take care of you
body:
TURO will take care of you! We picked up a tesla at LAX after flying across the country that later started to smell like weed. We didnt notice at first because the owner had a super strong air freshener inside. After we put the freshener in the glove box we immediately started smelling weed. Problem was that it was late and our hotel was an hour away. I contacted TURO from our hotel and told them about the smell and how we wanted to swap the car. They were on our side the whole time. No arguing. 

They offered to book a new vehicle after returning the vehicle (required before they could rebook), but we decided to rebook a car ourselves for the next day so we wouldn't have to wait. It ended up costing an additional 200 over what we originally paid for the original tesla. 

The next day after the drop off of the original car and pickup of the new car from the airport, they refunded the charge for the second car, covering the additional $200. 

Yes, still a bit more hassle than a rental agency but its really the only way to rent a tesla on the west coast at affordable prices. Gas prices are nuts and we drive a lot when visiting. Teslas make sense for us and Turo has been a great rental source for them.

author: Jerry F
title: JerryF
body: First time user. The process was pretty straight forward. SFO is a mad house trying to get to the vehicle. The rate was low and the car was an older model. So not bad in any way just know you get what you pay for. I felt that I got a little taken advantage of on parking I don't know if it Turo or owner. Turo refunded $8 but the price was $37. It seems like the host/owner have a business that just leaves multiple vehicles in the garage for renters. So if it sit in the garage for multiple hours of course it will be higher. I did arrive a the car roughly 30 minutes for my pickup time. They don't give you check in or drop off procedures until minutes before your scheduled times. I turned my vehicle the night before by contacting the host. But I started to get alerts I didn't drop off on time. But it was turned in prior but not officially finished. Would I do it again, probably for personal travel. I did not check with my credit card nor insurance company to see if I was covered for Turo. So cover yourself in case of an emergency.

title: Turo is better than the comeptition
body: Renting through Turo was the ONLY way I was able to get the technologies I needed in my rental. I tried the big competitors, and NONE of them would agree to give me a specific car on a specific date. author: Shamille Lockwood
title: The company needs a feature where you…
body:
The company needs a feature where you can record. I’m currently being accused of a false claim by Vince. I rented his fully loaded all power BMW SUV and soon after I picked it up he sends a message about a seat he had to get fixed prior that cost $2000. This is Vince exact text , “We had a guest break a seat while being impatient, and it cost them $2000 to have it repaired.” I knew no one was getting in the backseat while it was in my possession and I knew everything was power! 4 hours after I return he claims a back seat is broken. Vince exact text, “ Hello, one of the middle seats is stuck as it was moved without using the mechanical buttons. We have tried to recalibrate it yet. It is gonna have to be handled by a professional.
I wanted to let you know as we will have to get an estimate for the repair.” I don’t know what seat was broken prior, if it was ever fixed, or if it’s a different back seat.  While the vehicle was in my possession, no one got in the backseat. Only the front driver seat in front passenger seat was sat in. This is the message he sent 4 First and last time I’ll be using Turo. 
Vince exact text
“There was no broken seat when you picked up the car.  We sent you message telling you to use the mechanical buttons as someone previously broke a seat by not using the buttons to move the chair.  That had been repaired long aho.“

I know everything in Vince fully load SUV BMW was power. I have common sense! I didnt get in the backseat and I wouldn’t forcefully try to pull a seat down. Then Vince sent different angle pics that included others scratches/wear and tear to the interior that I never scene because I didn’t get in the backseat.

author: Crystal Brown
title: Backwards System
body:
I write this hoping my experience will change. I have a gripe to pick with this service. My boyfriend and I booked a trip to Orlando, when reviewing the itinerary Turo pops up as a car rental service. My boyfriend loves cars and super cars, so we check out the site for a week or so, and decide on one car. We put in his info, add the car, and we’re actually charged. Only after the initial charge the site cancels the reservation and says the account is blocked for further review.
Our trip isn’t for a few weeks, imagine if booking a trip that’s for tomorrow, and you need a car, you go through the steps, and your reservation is cancelled for review of the account, how helpful is this service then?
Odd.
So now I have to wait for the funds, we’re looking to rent a premium car, so it’s a decent amount, to return to the card - we’re waiting mostly on principle, not due to lack of funds.
I reach out to customer service and they explain it’s because the account is new.
Okay that’s very fair, especially for a car that’s at least 100k.
My question is, if there’s a need to review any/all new accounts shouldn’t that be something that’s said prior to inputting personal and credit card information.
I’m especially annoyed because the trip isn’t for weeks, any review of the account in theory should take place within that time. Not immediately after divulging personal information.
The system is so backwards that the car owner reached out to share pleasantries, and on my end the reservation is cancelled.

This is extremely annoying, tell me the rules and procedures prior to me giving out my information.

•Returned car early, fully washed, and vacuumed.

The Issue (Suspicious Damage Claim):
•Five hours after return, host submitted a $540.49 damage claim.
•Before/After photos show no discernable difference or damage.

Inconsistent Host Behavior:
•I offered to inspect the car in person in the morning.

author: Daniel
title: Not to be trusted, Avoid, Very poor Experiences
body:
Fat Llama claim all sorts of things - they claim that they are a safe platform, for instance. They also claim that the lender receives 80% of the item hiring fee.

Neither of these things are true. They are lies.

The safe platform issue - twice now I have had someone return an item in a non-functional state, and twice Fat Llama have found a way of dodging any responsibility.

It is a horrible feeling to be handed back an expensive item - that you knew to be working fully (one was brand new and never used, the other was recently serviced). 

On the second point - a rental value that the lender sees as £200, the lender receives £150 for. 

However, the borrower is charged £250. 

It's outrageous. Fat Llama are inserting themselves as a middleman but doing everything in their power to wash their hands if something goes wrong.

They are nothing more than a calendar app. This is the limit of their utility, and borrowers and lenders should both be VERY WARY of doing business on here.
reply:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear about your experiences with items being returned in a faulty state. Every guarantee claim is assessed individually and in most cases the outcome is considered fair and reasonable. We are sorry that this has not been the case for you. If you have not already done so, you are welcome to submit an appeal so our resolutions team can review the decisions again.

Regarding fees, lenders do not receive 80 percent. Our lender commission is currently 25 percent, which is also stated in our FAQs and Terms of Service, and from Monday it will be lowered to 20 percent. If you have seen any information that suggests otherwise, we would appreciate you letting us know so we can review and update it. Borrowers also pay a booking fee and together these fees cover platform costs such as secure payments, platform maintenance and development, customer support and insurance. This is why the borrower total can be higher than the rental price you set.

We understand your frustration and appreciate you taking the time to raise these concerns.

Kind regards,
Fat Llama

author: Spot
title: Great platform until you need customer service.
body:
Great Platform — But Lenders Are Left to Deal With the Mess
I've been a committed Fat Llama user for over 5 years, with more than 1,000 successful rentals under my belt, mostly lending out professional photography equipment. Over that time, I’ve met some genuinely great people, and the platform itself works well — until something goes wrong.
Fat Llama charges a 25% fee to both the lender and the renter. For example, if an item is listed at £100, the renter ends up paying £125, while the lender only receives £75. That’s a significant cut, but I accepted it because the platform advertises a “Lender and Renter Guarantee,” which is supposed to act like insurance for both parties.
However, the reality for lenders is far less reassuring.
If an item is returned damaged, the renter can simply hand it back with no further responsibility — and everything is left for the lender to deal with. You’re the one chasing quotes, filling out forms, waiting for responses, and often dealing with delays that stretch into weeks. During this time, there’s no compensation for lost income, no replacement gear, and no sense of urgency from Fat Llama’s support team.
In one case, I provided a repair quote from a very reputable London repair shop, only to be told I needed to get a second quote because the first wasn’t deemed sufficient. Then they asked for an “expert opinion” to confirm how the damage occurred — even though the damage was extremely obvious. It felt like they were just creating obstacles instead of helping.
For a platform that takes a 50% total cut (25% from both sides), the lack of meaningful support for lenders is deeply frustrating. The so-called “guarantee” offers no real protection, and the time and energy required to chase down even basic resolutions can be exhausting.
Fat Llama has huge potential, and the community is strong — but unless they drastically improve how they support and protect lenders, I can’t recommend it to others in good faith.

"Hopefully the rebrand and merger with Hygglo shakes things up at the top — because something clearly needs to change."
reply:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed review and for being part of the Fat Llama community for so many years. We truly value your contribution and the trust you’ve placed in the platform.

We’re very sorry to hear about your recent experience. We understand how stressful and frustrating it can be when equipment is returned damaged, especially when your income depends on having it available. While our guarantee is in place to protect both lenders and borrowers, we recognise that the claims process can feel lengthy and demanding at times, and that our communication hasn’t always been as fast as it should be.

It has been an especially busy season, which unfortunately has meant longer waiting times than we would like. Please know that every case is equally important to us, and our team is working as swiftly as possible to resolve each one.

Your feedback is important to us, and we are actively working on ways to make this process more efficient and supportive for lenders. The upcoming rebrand is part of a broader effort to improve the experience across the platform. As part of this change, there will also be updates to our fee structure, which we hope will make the platform feel fairer for both lenders and borrowers.

Thank you again for sharing your perspective and for continuing to lend through Fat Llama. Your input helps us identify where change is most needed, and we are committed to improving in these areas.

Kind regards,
Fat Llama

author: Random Account
title: Refusing to compensate lender for lost product
body:
I recently rented a camera body  off of fatllama for the week, had minor shipping delays arriving to me but nothing major, after using the camera I shipped it back using a trusted carrier and called it a day. 

Now, with multiple weeks of deliberation later, fatllama is trying to hold me the renter liable for the package being lost in the mail when it clearly states in their terms and conditions that “ If an item is lost during the rental period due to circumstances beyond your control.” And the package was shipped well within the return window. 

While I feel for the lender who’s gear was missing and would love to help, this is rediculous that they are trying to hold me liable for over 900$ worth of gear along with already charging me 100+$ in fees directly.
reply:
Thank you for your review. We understand how concerning it is when an item goes missing in transit, and we’re sorry for the frustration this has caused.

To protect both lenders and borrowers, returns sent by post need to be supported with proof of postage, such as a receipt or tracking number. Without this evidence we cannot confirm that the item was returned, and our guarantee does not cover items lost in transit where no proof has been provided.

If you are able to share any further details or documentation, we would strongly encourage you to reach back out to our Resolutions team. This will allow us to move things forward as fairly as possible. You can contact them by replying directly to the latest email you received from us.

Kind regards,
Fat Llama

author: Rupert
title: A useful, but expensive service
body:
It's a useful system for enabling people to make use 
of costly, specialist, or obscure things that it'd be a waste, or prohibitively expensive to own.

That said, the platform is extortionate for both renter & rentee, inflating prices to take a 25% fee from both parties.

For instance: An item listed for £50.
The platform takes £25. 
This consists of inflating the price for the rentee by £12.50, meaning the item actually costs them £62.50 and subtracting £12.50 from the renter, who receives £37.50.

This wasn't made clear when setting up an account and I rented a thermal camera for over a year, believing Fat Llama was taking a reasonable £12.50 from me & the people renting it were paying £50, which is what I listed it for.
I was not impressed when I discovered this deception & arguably, profiteering behaviour.

Fat Llama needs competition in the market to bring fees to a more reasonable level.
reply:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We are glad to hear that you find the platform useful, though we also understand your concerns regarding the service fee. 

To clarify, the fees are not hidden as they are outlined in our terms and conditions. These fees contribute to the ongoing development, maintenance, guarantees, secure payments and support that make the platform work. 

We also want to share that some changes are coming later this year which will see fees being lowered. You can find more information about this on fatllama.com.

Kind regards,
Fat Llama
