author: GS
title: Excessive Verification Requirements
body:
UPDATE ON 12-May:
Fat Llama requested more details about my experience, threatening to take my review offline if I don't respond. It feels like they want to make negative reviews disappear. I question whether the same scrutiny applies to 5-star reviews. Despite providing my information as requested, I haven't received a response after two days. Prompt communication and fair treatment of all reviews are crucial. I'm disappointed with Fat Llama's response and lack of communication, raising concerns about their commitment to customer satisfaction and transparency.
------------------------------

I attempted to rent an item on Fat Llama, but unfortunately, the verification process was invasive and required more personal information than necessary. The platform required me to submit my government-issued ID and a live photo, which I understand is for security reasons. However, the platform also requested my social media links to verify my identity.

Despite submitting my Facebook link, it was rejected, and I provided my Instagram link. The platform then required me to approve their follow request to verify my account, which I found excessive. Furthermore, the platform requested a company-issued payslip to be submitted, which revealed where I work and how much I get paid. This requirement was an unnecessary invasion of privacy.

To make matters worse, the platform took my bank details before the complicated verification process. If the platform is going to require such a complex verification process, they should inform customers beforehand, rather than asking for bank details first. This way, individuals who do not want to share their personal information can decide not to use the service without the need to have money and bank details held up.

Fat Llama claims that their unique verification process sets them apart, but in reality, it sets them apart from success. The verification process is excessive and raises questions about how Fat Llama handles and protects customers' personal information. I do not recommend using Fat Llama if you value your privacy.

At last, I was able to rent the same thing from the same owner from a different website and returned the item the way it was given to me. Fat Llama has completely been replaced by its competitor and has no reason to exist in the market.
reply:
Hi there,

Thank you for taking the time to leave you review. We just wanted to note, that we do not have the ability to take down reviews and this was never requested.

In regards to the verification process, we are transparent about how this works on our FAQ's https://fatllama.crisp.help/en/article/how-does-verification-work-1xsutcp/. We also are aware that the verification process can require more than most, but this is because we are not a rental house and do not take deposits. Therefore, we need to protect the platform using our unique verification process.

After reaching out to you we had approved your social media and your account has remained active and ready to rent if you wish. We're sorry that you had this experience, however we hoped we resolved it in a satisfactory way for you.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: L M
title: Great experience - some improvements could be made
body:
I recently joined Fat Llama after stumbling across it searching for pressure washer hire. The prices were significantly cheaper than HSS - £45 vs £7. I was really impressed by the interface and the ease of connecting with others. I hired a washer and a chipper and picked them up next day, the people I rented from were lovely and I got my task done for much less than if I'd purchased the equipment or hired it elsewhere, plus there's the environmental benefit. 

I would have given 5 stars however I think there are a couple of improvements that could be made to the site. One is that I don't use social media for personal safety reasons but social media verification was a prerequisite for hiring, therefore I had to reactivate and post something, even though I'd provided a selfie, my passport and a recent bank statement. I would be happy to provide another bill if that would suffice instead of social media.

Secondly as the site becomes more popular I think there should be increased protection for renters, I returned both items but there was no proof I had done so. Whilst I like the idea of trusting people to be honest, it isn't always the case, obviously Fat Llama agrees given that they require so much proof of ID. Would be good to add some kind of function where either the lender confirms on receipt of return or you can add a photo to show you've returned.

author: Rui Gonçalves
title: From Frustration to Gratitude
body:
I've been using Fat Llama for more than two years and have rented numerous items from their site. For the most part, I've had a great experience with the platform, and the process of renting equipment has been straightforward.

However, recently I had a complicated situation where a renter did not return an item, and the total value of the lost items was £1000. The renter was unresponsive and showed no signs of responsibility for the lost items, so I passed the case on to Fat Llama's Resolutions Team. Since the renter had their own insurance, Fat Llama had to try to get the money from the insurance company first. This made the process slower and more challenging.

After four months and nearly 100 emails, Fat Llama decided to pay me for the amount that the renter owed me. Although they're still trying to get the money from the renter's insurance company, I'm relieved that I don't have to be involved in the case anymore, and I can finally buy replacement items.

Despite the challenging situation, I have to say that I'm impressed with the way Fat Llama handled the case. Their Resolutions Team was responsive and supportive throughout the process, and they ultimately found a way to resolve the issue.

Overall, I still highly recommend Fat Llama for anyone looking to rent equipment. While my situation was challenging, I appreciate that they took responsibility for the renter's actions and made sure that I was compensated for the lost items.

author: Natalie
title: This company has no protection for…
body: This company has no protection for hirers. I was given a faulty camera and lost out on £200 hiring a studio only for the camera to be temperamental. I then contacted the hirer to return the item at the agreed time and he failed to respond. I contacted fat llama about the issue so they could contact him. He responded the next day saying he was in Hull but I could drop it the next morning.  I work in London and was unable to. He said he would charge for the week if I didn't return from it. I sent screenshot trying to arrange drop off to the lender and videos of the camera not turning on. Fat Llama ignored this and said I would be liable if I didn't drive back from London regardless of trying to drop it at the agreed time. I am now left with no money for lunch for two days due to the amount of money wasted on travel and hire. Companies like this exploit people for money. Be very careful and do not use this company.
reply:
Hi Natalie, 

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

After having a look into this case, we've seen that the item was not faulty and the owner helped you to get it working and they also did not charge you anything for returning the item a week late. We worked hard trying to get the best outcome for you and from what we can see this has worked out in the end. 

We're sorry you've had this experience but this is not something which is a regular occurrence on Fat Llama. 

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Matilda
title: Privacy Violating
body:
I have rented out items through Fat Llama for two years now. I am a volunteer and rent out items on behalf of a local charity. 

Pro:
Easy to use.
Easy to get renters.
The customer service team do reply really quickly.
They pay out quickly.

Cons:
Mobile App has many glitches - you can't properly edit a listing on the app.
They have insurance but you have to take a photo of the item each time before you rent it. Which is a huge hassle and you end up with thousands of photos of the same thing. 
The insurance will not pay out if the damage does not affect the usability of the item. So I have had an item deliberately defaced/vandalised and they will do absolutely nothing about it because it 'doesn't affect the functionality'. So I've had an item ruined and they do nothing. 
They take a huge cut of your rentals. 
They harass you to accept a booking within two hours, even if the renter has not messaged you to arrange a collection, even if the renter and you can not actually arrange a meeting. 
If you are messed around by a renter - which you have no control over, and you are forced to cancel the listing, it affects your stats. 
If you don't reply to every message, even when the conversation is over and they've said something like 'okay thanks', it affects your stats. 
Your stats control how often you appear in searches. So when negative things happen out of your control, you are punished for it. 

MAJOR ISSUE
They do not care about your privacy and they text your phone number to all of your renters (strangers) even if you specifically withdraw consent. If you ask them to remove your number you will not be able to log into the platform via a desktop. This leaves you open to harrassment, stalking, any kind of abuse from renters to your personal phone number and Fat Llama will not stop violating your privacy no matter how many times you ask. As it's written in their privacy policy that it is okay to violate you repeatedly, they think it's absolutely perfectly fine to put you at risk.

And for that reason, this is a 2 star company at best.
reply:
Hi Matilda,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We're happy you're continuing to use Fat Llama despite the issues you've raised. Regarding the Resolutions Process, like any company we have a criteria set out when it comes to making payouts for claims, which is accessible here: https://fat-lama-assets.s3.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/lenderguarante.pdf

Whilst we appreciate your feedback on the way in which we calculate owner stats, we have also discussed the owner stats with you a few times and have noted that being between 90-100% most of the time will not effect users decisions to rent with you, as it's still a really great score. 

In regards to the phone number sharing, we share this with renters only when the rental has been confirmed by an owner so that they may contact you to organise the handovers. We have since removed your phone number so this does not happen as a special request from yourself.

If you require any further assistance you can email resolutions@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Tom
title: NO protection as a buyer. Go to rental shops
body:
Up until my recent experience I’d of rated Fat Llama 4/5 stars. Where it falls down is with the lack of flexibility to buyers provided once you go past the 48 hour cooling off period. 
In my example, there was a national train strike in place, meaning I needed to make some amendments to how I could collect and receive the equipment from the lender.
I couldn’t come to an arrangement with the buyer, despite offering to come to their address directly. 
Subsequently as a buyer, you have no protection past the 48 hour cooling off period. I received a partial refund and lost £60. At no point was I made away that I’d receive a partial refund until the money hit my bank. I didn’t receive any notification from Fat Llama either, it was only as I questioned it.

Remember, without lenders Fat Llama is nothing, there would be no business model.
I’ve rented 7 or 8 times from them but unfortunately no more from me. Which is a shame. I’d rather pay more from a rental company knowing that they will look after their customers. I recommend Wex or FOMO rentals in London.
reply:
Hi Tom,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We're sorry you had this experience with this rental. However, we have a cancellation policy in place to protect the owners. This is displayed on our FAQ's here: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/2482856-cancellations-policy

In this case, as this was completely outside of the cancellation policy and owner may have turned down other jobs to fulfil your request, and therefore would be losing out on that money. We did also refund partially to you as a gesture of goodwill, which is not something we always do.

We hope with this knowledge this won't happen again during a rental.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: James Fisher
title: new low bar for customer service set
body:
In an industry where time is of the essence- these guys set a new low bar for service and support. Highly not recommended
1- The have a verification process that had me alternately laughing and crying. What the f#*k is a "payslip" guys? this is an industry of contractors and freelancers, why do i need to show you my confidential business paperwork?
2- While every other rental house in the UK can verify anyone within an hour using their company reg number, a credit rating and and one or two industry references from other rental houses, these guys need two days to read my confidential invoices and tax records.
3- the verification team literally ghosted us multiple times in the days before an important shoot - neither myself nor the lender could get a response for them.
4- they have made themselves uncontactable, no phone number, their help bot only answers pre-formulated FAQ's.
5- when they do decide to email back, its a different person who knows nothing about the case, and asks for documents that you sent through 48 hours ago.
reply:
Hi James,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. We're sorry that this was not experience you were expecting when using us. We do sometimes require further verification for items of a higher value. Such as asking for employment documentation. We of course know that people who use us are self employed and freelancing, so we offer alternative options when providing proof of employment such as invoices etc. We do not check users credit history when they use Fat Llama, which makes our verification system unique and more accessible to users.

After having a look at this particular matter, it looks as if your emails were sent to someone at Fat Llama who no longer works for the company which is where some confusion may have come from. We also sent emails to you without a response, which may have ended up in your spam/junk folder. If there are any issues in future, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and our team will be more than happy to help.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: BK
title: I was probably one of the first 1000…
body:
I was probably one of the first 1000 users in 2016 and had a great experience of renting and lending ont the platform (GoPro and some speakers as late as 2021).

I don't know what has happened since then but I tried to rent a Sony a7iii from someone local for a quick shoot for leisure. The process for reviewing my rental included having my passport, utility bill, pay slips and a phone AND a call asking me WHY I wanted to rent! I haven't faced such due diligence even on my mortgage. This, for a 2.2k equipment for 1 day that I accepted liability for and they considered as 3k. Reality is it's probably 1.6k worth given its all second hand. 

I got declined and limited to 1.5k rental....as someone who could easily buy it new multiple times over, they lost a customer and the lender didn't get a rental which around this area is rare as we live in a village. 

I've had to buy one and will be renting it out on other paltforms now.

Having no presence on Google maps and no direct customer support is telling. I'd be surprised if the platform lasts much longer than a few years.
reply:
Hi BK,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We're sorry you've had this experience on Fat Llama. Just to clarify, the value of the equipment is set by the owner, so their value of their equipment is what we base our thresholds on. We do sometimes require further verification for items of a higher value. Our verification system is what makes Fat Llama unique, and it's what we use to protect our owners on the site. If you would like some more help on how to verify then please feel free to email resolutions@fatllama.com and we will be able to discuss your account further.

We do not have a presence on Google Maps as we're an online company and also a remote working team. We do have a whole dedicated customer service team that works from 8am-10pm to help you with any of your queries too.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Mathew Meyer-White
title: I've taken all of my gear off of Fat Llama
body:
I've taken all of my gear off of Fat Llama after just 5 hires. The website itself is not the issue, although they do take an absolutely shocking amount out of your hire fees - something like 1/5th, which is daylight robbery and, in some cases, makes it a pointless waste of time to let things out at all.

The real problem is the users... The larger agencies are fine to deal with - usually respectful and they have an understanding of hire etiquette, but the young kids who are just messing about are just far more hassle than 15 quid here and there could ever be worth to me.  I've had kit come back missing parts already, kids messaging me at 1.25am in the morning. Everything is last minutes and they then harass you via messaging when they don't know how to use something... It's total waste of my already very busy time. 

Fat Llama could really do with introducing a two tiered user system whereby the corporates or larger established agencies sit on one tier while the student types and ultra low-budget 'filmmakers' sit on another - then let me as the hirer decide which tiers my gear is open to. I'd deal with the pros, but not touch the small-timers.

The pros would happily go through stricter verification processes as well, whereas one of the low-budgeters registered less than an hour before hiring and lost a part of the kit. He could well have used a burner phone or SIM card for this weakest of verifications. It's pointless and I'm allowing hundreds of pounds worth of gear to go out to these people.

It's a good idea., just very VERY poorly executed for professionals.
reply:
Hi Matthew,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We're sorry you've had anything less than an amazing experience whilst using the platform.

As a community based platform, we welcome any users who are looking to rent items and meet our verification process. We also introduce further verification for items of high value which protects you as an owner. Our Owner Guarantee also covers you for anything which is lost, stolen or damaged during the rental, as long as the criteria is met. We welcome open discussions between renters and owners regarding how items are used or tips for first timers on the platform. 

You're also able to edit your notification settings if this is something you'd be interested in - if you just email info@fatllama.com, we'd be more than happy to help.

We hope you feel like you could use the platform again in the future.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team.

author: JG
title: Fat Llama is an organisation with dubious business practices
body: Fat Llama is an organisation with highly dubious business practices. I tried to rent some golf clubs which were not available as they had already been rented. The owner confirmed that they weren’t available. Nevertheless FAT LLAMA immediately debited the rental charge + their exorbitant fee (25%) which is not mentioned anywhere on their site prior to you being billed.. I am still chasing Fat Llama for reimbursement….. I would strongly advise people Not to use this company.
reply:
Hi JG,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we're sorry you had this negative using the platform this time round.

At Fat Llama renters have the ability to message lenders to ask about their items availability before you rent the item, we also allow this feature to make sure the handover can be organised smoothly.

Every time you make a request on the platform, your card is pre-authorised for the amount of the rental. This means that the funds are reserved in "Pending" status in your bank account while the request is pending. Only once the owner accepts the request are the funds debited from your account. Therefore, you would have certainly received a refund.

We're sorry that you feel the fees we charge are high. As we do not take any deposits from our users, our fees ensure the smooth running of the platform, the support we provide in our customer service, verifications, resolutions and security teams and for us to protect our Owners with our Owner backed guarantee. We hope you still would choose to us the platform to save money on renting items.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Rob
title: Terrible verification process, HUGE hidden fees
body: Terrible verification process where the renter is not notified that you’ve put in a request to rent their item until the process has completed, but they take money from your bank, and takes a while to get verified. HUGE fees for lender AND renter, and they try and trick you by hiding the fee cost until the very end. It’s a shame.
reply:
Hi Rob,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

Every time you make a request on the platform, your card is pre-authorised for the amount of the rental. This means that the funds are reserved in "Pending" status in your bank account while the request is pending. Only once the owner accepts the request are the funds debited from your account. 

If you do not complete verification or the owner does not accept the request, the funds are released on our end, and it's just up to your bank to put them back in your available balance. We do try and be as clear as possible regarding the fees when you request to rent, as this is displayed before you pay. For any further information please see our FAQ's: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/

Best regards,

Fat Llama

+/ - Although there were multiple verification checks, phone calls and even had to go through further verification and to pull out an insurance policy for the equipment ( was quite of a surprise as it didn’t state that you had to anywhere to my knowledge) , It is a good platform to rent equipment as the hosts have what you need for the right job so they thrive in that department 

+ The support team was quick and speedy as I requested to rent the items the day prior but it was quite stressful as there was a lot of back and forth. 

+ After I was verified, things were a lot more smoother 

- On my previous account I rent all the time but I never recall going through so many verification checks and I hope I don’t have to do that every time I want to rent again as I will be using the platform frequently for a series I am shooting 

- Would be 5 if the process wasn’t so laborious

author: Customer
title: My second attempt to rent was poor also...
body:
I gave a two or three star review the first time I rented. The rental went fine but I could not get the 'first time discount' to work and the 'contact support' process is terrible.

This time because the value of the item was more, they asked for proof of employment. Because I am 67 and retired the system couldn't cope. I went through various emails with customer support ( again an awful experience with long waits and simply automatic replies etc) I could not make the verification process work. The 'Tax return' option is very difficult to make work because a tax return is generally a 15 page document and no one in their right mind would share that amount of personal information. 
I have no confidence ever to use this service again. Sadly it is a great concept but the management are unable to recognise a legitimate renter and clearly clueless about the verification processes and the negative effect this can have on the brand.
reply:
Hi there,

Thank you for your time to leave this review. We're very sorry you've had this experience using the platform on this occasion. I hope the resolve we came to directly with you, was sufficient for you and that you will have the confidence to use Fat Llama again. 

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Chris Edwards
title: A site designed by and for stupid people.
body: Oh What a terrible site. It is set up for the US market so even the addition of your telephone number is difficult. But this is not the reason for one star: fill in all the details even give your full bank card details AND THEN,,, they demand to have a copy of your driving licence or your passport. Who but a complete idiot would put those images on the net. I could imagine the bank refusing to reimburse you if you have problems as you acted so stupidly giving away such images. I can barely imagine anyone been so silly as to disclose such personal details.
reply:
Hi Chris,

We're sorry you feel this way about Fat Llama. 

We're actually a UK based company which operates in Canada and the US as well. There is an option to change where you are based at the top of the screen on the desktop version of the website.

We do require a fair amount of verification, this is so we can protect our lenders and make sure the community on Fat Llama stays trustworthy. If you have any questions about our verification system, please contact resolutions@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Anup Baria
title: Ripping lender & borrower at the same time
body:
Platform fairly simple to use but their fees are  shocking i.e.

£22 one day camera rental:
Borrower gets £16.50 (£22 -£5.50 service fee)
Lender is being charged £27:50
(£5.50 service fee + £22 rental)

So on a £22 rental for one day, they’re taking £11.92 in deductions in total. I asked the lender to send me a printscreen of their charges. Ripping everyone off. They don’t pay you if something goes wrong, they make the lender pay.

I no longer use this platform.
reply:
Hi Anup,

Thank you for your email. We're sorry you do not feel happy with the fee structure that we've had. Just to clarify, the renter of the item would pay the price of the 1 day rental plus a 25% fee, then the owner of the item gets the day price, minus a 25% fee. 

We've had the structure in place for some time now, and unlike normal rental houses, we do not take any sort of deposits, therefore we charge fees to keep the business running. 

If you would like to discuss this further, we'd be happy to hear your feedback at info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

Payment was received after couple of days of the rental finishing, no problem there.

author: Abdul
title: I had enough and decided to pull my listings from there.
body:
I had to give this a miss. I have a proper b2b rental company for some AV. I decided to put some of the surplus equipment from my main business onto fatllama for the retail users. 

Most of the rentors were good. They arrived on time and the returned on time. But some of the users just really took the mick. Agreeing on a certain time - then when I chase up 15-20 mins after that time, they reply back saying will be there in a couple of hours. 

Bear in mind, fatllama is usualy casual hours, evenings & weekends. Single unit rentals as opposed to multiples I did through my own website. It just wasn't worth it for me. 

I just cancelled the last rentor who took the mick with the pickup timing, delisted all my items and I'm going to stick to the business to business trade. Where I can invoice client if they are late. 

The other issue is that fatllama don't give any documentation or delivery/collection receipt to sign. Customers just walk up, hey/hello, here's the goods. bye. 

When I supply stuff to customers I verify their ID and get them to sign the delivery note. It's all a bit casual. 

The insurance policy for the UK is 2 claims and you're done. You cannot have insurance cover with their hiscox policy if you have had more than 2 claims in the last 5 years. Which is fine. I had 0 claims. But they have some guy on their marketing material claiming he makes £4000 a month from fatllama and used it to buy a house.
reply:
Hi Abdul,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we're sorry to see you go. 

Fat Llama is a community based platform, therefore all organising and arranging for timings is up to the users to do between themselves. We have rules regarding late returns that renters need to adhere to and you're within your right to report other users if they do not adhere to these rules. We also have a rigorous verification system to stop anyone coming through that shouldn't be on the platform. 

If you'd like to find out more about this, then please contact us at resolutions@fatllama.com

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Joe Heffernan
title: Big fees to the lender and renter

author: Mickey LF Lee
title: Highly intrusive verification process. AVOID
body: Given my experience with this company, I would advise all to avoid especially if you are renting for work or production, as they have highly intrusive verification process which is based on transaction values, so it kicks off AFTER you paid for the rental. It takes up to 1hour, or more. They ask for a selfie video, then your ID, proof of address, proof of employment status and income, social media accounts ... only official documents will be accepted. I felt violated having to send them my bank statements and client invoice, but had no choice as I have already paid. In the event of their system being hacked or a data breach to an insider, all of your highly personal data that they process and collect are in jeopardy. From all the companies I had to deal with in my personal or professional capacity - they are the worst in respect of GDPR.
reply:
Hi Mickey,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we're sorry to hear that you've had a negative experience on the platform.

We try our very best to protect lenders and borrowers. If you're renting higher value items than usual, we may ask for extra verification. We try our best to be clear as to what is needed for different thresholds, but if you have any further questions at all or feedback, please email info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Tryss
title: HORRENDOUS - 50% Fees, 25% Refunds, Slow Payments, Punishing Compensation System
body:
DOUBLE DIPPING - Fat Lama charge an appalling inexplicable 50% Total Fee by taking 25% from the lender and 25% from the borrower. That means, if you rent something for £100, they will make £50 profit - that is absolutely OUTRAGEOUS! I don't know of any other service that charges 50% fees.

Fat Lama’s payment system is insultingly slow. In 2021, when online payments are instant, you can wait up to a WEEK to receive your payment from them! There are no payments processed on the weekend, so if your rental ends on Thursday - you're out of luck, and not going to receive your payment until Wednesday of next week!

REFUND process is also a pain, because they make it intentionally difficult and expensive. If you as a lender offer a Refund to the borrower, Fat Lama still charge the borrower 25% Fee! Can you believe that? So the lender makes nothing, the borrower loses money and they make money for NOTHING. This is an absolutely shameless ripoff.

Refunding someone is not quick and simple - it is unnecessarily obtuse. As a lender, you have no power of decision to refund a borrower with a few clicks, if it’s necessary – instead you’ll have to jump through hoops and contact their customer support with an explanation and wait for their gracious approval.

The Insurance and Compensation system is also not on the customer's side. I've had harrowing discussions with other lenders, who had to endure the awful compensation process. If something is lost or stolen, Fat Lama will go out of their way to weasel their way out of payment. And if they do agree to compensate, they will find the cheapest price on the web for your lost item, even if the price is a lot lower than its real market value in your country.

Fat Lama's idea of p2p gear lending is great, but their draconian fees and anti-consumer policies make it a terrible choice for lenders and borrowers.

For now they have a market Monopoly, and that's a terrible thing from consumers' standpoint. But not for long - already, there are decent and consumer friendly alternatives starting to appear here in the UK.
reply:
Hi Tyrss,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We have a cancellation policy in place to protect owners from borrowers cancelling, please see this FAQ for more information: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/2482856-cancellations-policy. We take on board your feedback that this is not something the lender can do from their end once there is an accepted booking, so thank you for this, we will pass this back to our product team.

If you ever have any questions about the Resolutions or Claims process, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we will be happy to ask any questions.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Fergus MacInnes
title: Great but needs better UI for renters/sellers
body:
Fat Llama has been great for me as a renter of my drum kit. I joined just 7 days ago and in that time have made over £100 from several rentals. 

However, I've had two technological problems so far. 

Somehow, I'm able to do more through the app than the website. For example, I find it easy to change availability through the app but can't find where to on the website. Another example is those orange lightning bolt things that show up when searching for products (don't know what they mean, firstly), only they are only there on the app and not on the website. 

My second issue is that as a seller I have no analytics to review my earnings. I need some sort of page where I can view my total revenue ever, for the past week/month/year/5 years etc. It should be similar to google adsense analytics for youtube, and I should be able to compare different items I have listed. It should take into account the value of the item, predict an annual revenue per item depending on its price and past popularity, and show a ratio for each item of it's price to revenue generated per year. 

As a renter/seller, I shouldn't have to add up the numbers/create separate savings accounts just to help view how much fat llama is making me. This should be such an easy fix with an 'analytics' tab under my profile. Please implement this as it really is the only thing that makes fat llama not quite what i want it to be.

EDIT:
Just read that they've had £13M invested into the company - How has this not been implemented yet?!
reply:
Hi Fergus,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We appreciate your feedback and have passed this back to the tech team.

We do in fact have a section where you can see analytics showing earnings, requests, rental acceptance rate and response rate, as well as lifetime earnings. You can view the last 30 days as well as lifetime. You just need to go onto your profile and then click on 'See My Stats'.

If you have any further questions or feedback, please email info@fatllama.com and we will be happy to discuss.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Zach
title: Needed lighting for a project
body:
Needed lighting for a project, signed up and paid for a booking.
I was afterwards ask to verify before the booking could be made, even though I had already paid for said booking. 

I’d submitted all details requested including, drivers licence, bank statements, Facebook, Instagram and Youtube accounts. My verification was rejected. 
After 3 days of communication, I was told they couldn’t verify me because I hadn’t posted on social media in over 6 months and that they will call me to resolve, since then they have gone cold, ignoring my emails and requests for a refund. 

The support links on your website don’t work.

I can’t believe this is a more stringent process than getting a loan, all for a £40 transaction. 

You don’t verify customers at the start because you don’t want to pay the cost of admin before the customer hands over money, yet you take a 50% cut from the lender, your greed knows no bounds.
reply:
Hi Zach,

Thank you for taking the time to leave your review. 

We're very sorry you had this experience with the platform on this occasion. We've realised our error on this occasion as your emails got lost in our system, a very rare occasion and one we've now fixed so this won't happen again.

You would have been fully refunded and we hope that our resolution to you via email is satisfactory and you will use us again in future. 

If you need any further assistance, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we will help you through the process.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

author: Adeola Adeola
title: Avoid! Avoid! Avoid!
body: They have a very poor cancellation policy. It's a very drawn out process where they make it difficult for you to contact anyone (lots of automation, computer-generated messages). As a result it is a frustrating process, in the end they then refuse to give refunds despite it being clear that no rental has taken place. It is not worth the hassle. Do not bother. Save your money because this company is facilitating scammers and people pretending to offer items which they never intend to rent out. Be careful!
reply:
Hi Adeola,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

It looks like this case was resolved in the end and you were given a refund. We're sorry this took longer than usual, this was because we required a case to be open so we could review this fully. You have every right as a borrower to seek compensation if you have not received an item so if you have any issues in future, please don't hesitate to get in touch.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Steven Santa Cruz
title: They double the fees 4 A worse service
body:
Used to kind of work because customer service were responsive and good at actually helping you quickly when issues arose with borrowers.

Now fees are crazy? 25% to both parties and they to justify this by saying they have improved the service... they have not... they have made it much worse. 

Particularly worse is the support for lenders via customer service, the worst part is they have made it impossible to directly reach customer service easily (and no phone option at all). It’s a classic discouragement tactic.  If an issue arises and you do get through to customer service via the live chat (Maybe) then they have no authorisation to do anything for you now.  They will not contact the borrower your having difficulties with anymore.  Instead you have to fill out a form and email them ~ buy the time the problem is dealt with any late return will already be in the the past and the knock on effects to lost business work or rentals will take place with no assistance from Fat Llama except maybe The promise you will be compensated later. 

The result is your forced to negotiate with whomever is refusing to return your kit, just to get it back because you can’t afford to spend two day getting Fat Llama To lift a finger to help 

The app is super buggy now Since they updated. So what I’m am I getting for this nutty 25%?  Much Less than I got before.   So the fee more than doubleS and the service got terrible in return.  I don’t get this business move
reply:
Hi Steven,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, your feedback is valuable to us.

Regarding the website and the app, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users. We have a number of things we are working on as well as fixing bugs and making the user experience smooth and seamless. Now while this is no quick fix, and we greatly appreciate your patience in this time whilst we work on further developments.

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

In regards to reporting a resolutions case, our Resolutions Team work hard on resolving cases and aim to respond as soon as possible to help you with your case.

Your feedback has been taken on board and we hope you continue to use the platform.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Tin Drum
title: Great idea, clunky and inefficient platform
body:
I am a big fan of this service and the idea behind it. However, I firmly believe the platform is clunky and inefficient, with several issues:
- The app itself is buggy and I cannot describe how many times I had the issue of missing the messages or lenders' responses;
- A number of times there were unanaswered messages by lenders - who are active on the platform. Unanswered messages should be part of rating. 
- The additional checks for renting items whose collective value is above a certain threhshold - this is incredibly inefficient. While I respect and agree with the system of additional checks, I experienced being flagged for additional checks for the exact same rental I did in the past, which needed no checks. Additionally, I was asked "what are you going to use the camera for?". Unacceptable. If you make me state my portfolio account and profession when joining your platform, send my passport, payslip etc - then make it a stringetn check in the begining and ensure checks for above £X amount are done in a sensible way later but quickly. 
- Your response rate for issues is simply terrible.
reply:

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback.

Regarding the website and the app, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users. The Fat Llama Team

author: MOS
title: SCAM SCAM SCAM do not use Fat Llama
body: Ita a SCAM. Do NOT use fat Llama. I wanted to rent some scooters but renter cancelled last minute. The money came out of my account and renter said he has cancelled because he said he hasn't received the payment in his bank account. (Policy says seller receives their money within 1 week). That is not my issue as ive paid on my side. On my APP, It says ready to collect. I told Renter that ive paid and showed screenshot of receipt. He confirms that its approved but wants the money cleard in his bank account. Renter is not responding to any of my messages. I've emailed Llama to complain and I'm waiting for a response. I wanted to rent a scooter for me and my son this weekend as we have been looking forward to it for weeks but no scooter and I've lost almost £50. Thanks Llama
reply:
Hi Mos,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We want to try and get to the bottom of this and help you receive your refund. If a lender has cancelled on you then they should cancel from their end, if they do not do this, then you can absolutely get in touch with us and we will refund you fully. 

You should have already received all the funds back in your account but if thats not the case, then please get in touch with us.

Fat Llama will always be happy to help you find replacement items if a lender has cancelled on you.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

Hi Ali,

The Fat Llama Team

body: The concept is brilliant but I have noticed that certain lenders (groups) they have high rating and they all recommend each other and some of them (657 reviews ) rating shows. author: Yoni Cohen
title: Very Useful Platform
body: Experience renting equipment through Fat Llama has always been consistent and very good. Excellent platform to operate, communication with the majority of people is usually good and easy transactions and pick ups make the website very useful!

author: TJ
title: So far so good..
body: So far so good... It's great platform for trying kit or renting when working with clients. But make sure you photograph the equipment on receipt and before returning to protect yourself. There is no system in play that protects the renter, only the hirer. Otherwise, there is an amazing range of kit available.

author: Adrian Dickson
title: Has anyone noticed that every good…
body: Has anyone noticed that every good review on here is invited. When you check other sites its a 50/50 on invited and non invited reviews. Almost like they want to drown out the bad reviews... Like they did with their old website, before their spam reviews got deleted due to breach of the trustpilot rules and they had to rebrand the whole company name and add a second L. From my experience on fatllama, this little observation just summarises the company. Read the non invited reviews, to get an actual image of what you are getting into.
reply:
Hi Adrian,

Thank you for the time to leave this review.

We're sorry that you view Fat Llama in such a negative way. We encourage everyone to read reviews and make their own decisions on using Fat Llama. We also have a huge amount of information about how we work in our FAQ's which you can read here: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/

author: Roobs BL
title: Fat Llama has changed the game
body: Fat Llama has changed the game, for any small budget photo/video work I've done fat llama is usually the first place I look for kit as your always going to find a good price. Just an over all great service !

author: JD
title: Borrowers avoid at all costs!
body: Borrower: A truly terrible experience all round using this website. I spend the best part of 10-12 hours trying to resolve a very simple issue but in the end i was ignored and had to cancel my rental. The issue was verification, i had given them my drivers licence and recorded a video message for them but because i wouldn't set my Facebook account to non-private for them i could not be verified and so could not rent anything. I contacted there woeful customer service via their chat app but the conversation was so slow with them often taking over 20minutes to response i eventually gave up and asked that my request £20 rental was cancelled as it was simply not worth the effort and stress they had made the process. I understand Fat Llama have processes that are necessary to protect lenders but the amount of information they demand from you, in my opinion, is extremely excessive and disproportionate to the task i am trying to complete. Would avoid at all costs if you want a relaxed and stress free renting experience.
reply:
Hi JD,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we are sorry you've had a negative experience when using the platform.

As you mentioned, we need to do certain checks on borrowers to ultimately protect the lenders and their equipment. Social media can be used to help establish someone’s identity and/or work, which helps to improve their Trustworthy Score so that we can verify the transaction.

We're sorry to hear that your query took so long to respond to, we work on responding as quick as possible to our customers but sometimes we do get a bit busy and a further wait will be required. We greatly appreciate your patience at those times.

If you would like to contact us to get your verification completed and for you to rent with us again, we would be very happy to help you with that - just email resolutions@fatllama.com to discuss.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Alexandre Prod'homme
title: Great way to combat consumerism!
body:
Renting stuff and renting gear out is fine, but the app and the website still need more work, it can be extremely frustrating at time. 
One of the most annoying one is the calendar. The user interface is NOT keeping up with it's time. One of it's quirk is that when you want to mark your gear unavailable, you can't select multiple item at once, and it's buggy. Reoccurring comment: everything is quite buggy. 
Or the search engine, for which I still scratch my head.  Also, having to login before even starting to browse every single time is super annoying.
This and having clocks&timers for how fast you answers is very frustrating because surely it affect your search ranking (impossible to know to which extent) but non-professional renters (which also are more flexible and friendly) are usually slower and therefore handicapped by this system. I reckon over a long time, it could really shape fatllama in a way I am not very happy with and make me leave the platform. Already searching yield bad bias for super professional rental company who moved on it...
Despite the generous fees they are taking, I see little to no improvement as well...
But despite all that, the community is quite good, and thanks to the system, you avoid buying gear you don't need, and you rent out the stuff you don't use. Something we can all stand behind.
reply:
Hi Alexandre,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We are really happy that you have successfully rented and lent out items, and that you feel there is a good community. This is something we strive for and we appreciate your business.

Regarding the website, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users. We have a number of things we are working on as well as fixing bugs and making the user experience smooth and seamless. Now this is no quick fix, and we greatly appreciate your patience in this time whilst we work on further developments.

To leave any further feedback please email info@fatllama.com and we will be happy to pass this onto our team.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Rainbow Multimedia
title: My camera is dead and buried
body:
A customer damaged some of the buttons of my Sony A7s II camera body. I reported to fatllama they wanted a video proof of the damaged button working. 
Fatllama told me that before renting I have take a video proof of the item working, as well all the buttons of the item working. Fatllama also needs proof of purchase of the item.

If you have time to make a video of all the buttons of your camera working before renting it every time then it's a good platform for you.

Unlucky, i didn't have a video proof nor did i have a purchase receipt (gift camera). My camera is dead and buried under my bed since then.

thanks fatllama. wish you all the best.
reply:
Hi there, 

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We have a specific Lender Guarantee criteria which needs to be met in order for lenders to be covered fully. This criteria is available on our FAQ's on our website and we encourage all lenders to read this. As with any kind of guarantee there will be criteria that needs to be met. 

We're sorry to hear that on this occasion the criteria was not met and your claim was rejected. We hope in future to be able to assist you further on these types of cases.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: MD KHALED
title: Borrwing is fine... for renters it's a nightmare.
body:
* My camera equipment was booked for a day. Borrower promised to return within 24 hours but he didn't return, instead, he forcefully kept it extra days.
* I reported to fatllama resolution team. They couldn't do anything.
* Item was returned 3 days later. fatllama charged borrower for 2 days rental and didn't compensate, of course, they apologized, lol.
Don't trust fatllama's service or what they publicize.
stay miles away from renting. but for borrowing it's perfect. you can borrow return late or whenever you want. fatllama can't do anything.
reply:
Hi MD,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We are sorry you have had this experience on the platform. The Resolutions team will do their very best to get the best possible outcome for both parties when it comes to resolutions cases. They will hear both sides of a story to make sure a fair resolution is made.

We hope you still feel you can rent your items through the platform and continue to lend through us.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Toby Walker
title: Saved our camping trip
body: We needed a tent urgently as the one we bought hadn't been delivered. We were able to rent a tent near our campsite from a really nice bloke. Really great offering and concept.

author: Alex Grace
title: Honest lender review after over a year
body:
Going to keep it short and will write a proper detailed long review once we fully leave the platform. 

Concept is good, when we joined, the platform was great. Now, the little nuisance type issues are:

- App is buggy, slow and pretty much unusable, somehow overheats even an iphone 11 pro (they will respond by saying that they are working on improvements, but thats a lie, it has been like that for at least a year), 
- If you want to use the platform just stick your pc browser, it only bugs out in search every once in a while and in activity page when you try to move past page 1.
- If you are planning on being a lender, expect your items to depreciate, really quick (deep scratches on cameras, dust in sensors, etc etc) Fatllama wont cover it, its wear and tear (read their T&Cs) . 
- 4 out of 5 borrowers will be late to return the kit, they are only on time to collect. 
- When borrowing, people will be rude, people will pop kit into ubers when they cannot be asked to return themselves and not tell you (uber does not have the appropriate insurance btw), they will lose cables and little accessories (doesn't sound like an issue but when replacements cost £10 and it happens once a week, its just an additional cost that fatllama will do absolutely nothing about).
- If something gets broken, you better have video proof just before the rental, because once again, borrowers will just try to say that it was given to them that way. If you can't prove it, fatllama will not do anything.

Now onto the major issues:
- FEES, they've recently increased their fees to 25% each way to both borrower and lender, so if you list something for £100 per day, you get £75, borrower pays £125 (+ getting their own hire out insurnace) and fatllama make £50. They will respond with a link to a blog explaining why they've increased the fees, but as all lenders will agree, changing a line of code to amend how weekly discounting works, does not warrant a 10% fee increase each way. It would be understandable if they actually added features to the platform or made it easier to contact them (they do the opposite of that one, chatbot does everything possible to get you to email them rather than speak to a human being). 

- As mentioned above, kit gets broken, A LOT

- Stuff gets stolen, also A LOT, I want to bring examples from my own and other lender experiences (we all speak to each other) but would rather stay anonymous until the American big daddy competitor gets here near the end of the year. Not going to call them by their name but its a grid to share of sorts. Not gonna get into it, will probably get the review removed.  

- Insurance for lenders is a 50/50, sometimes you get paid, sometimes you dont. Late return fee payments are just like betting on 0 in a roulette wheel, they will try to charge the borrowers bank account once and then pass it onto their security team, which means you will not see the late return fee money.

Anyway, I got bored of trying to not use examples to stay anonymous, will post a full one once we leave the platform in full.
reply:
Hi Alex,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We are sorry you feel this way about the platform after so long, wanted to address some of the issues you have been facing.

- Regarding the app, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users
- Regarding the fee increases, our intention here is not financial gain, however, in order for the business to continue to evolve, we need to make these changes. As mentioned above, we have a number of things we are working on as well as fixing bugs and making the user experience smooth and seamless. Now this is no quick fix, and we greatly appreciate your patience in this time whilst we work on further developments
- In regards to the Lender Guarantee, we do have certain criteria that needs to be met for a lender to be covered. For example, as you mentioned, taking pre rental videos and post rental videos which you can see all of the criteria in our FAQ's. For every case reported, our Resolutions team will do their best to resolve in the fairest way possible.

We thank you for being a loyal customer and hope we can improve our services to your satisfaction so you stay with us. 

If you have any further feedback or concerns, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we would be happy to discuss.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Ned Collier
title: Really recommend Fat Llama
body: Really recommend Fat Llama. My rental experience was very good and the app made everything simple. The registration process is thorough but I see that as a good thing in terms of safety and security. Isis have one qs I asked them and they responded very quickly. Definitely recommended!

author: Nigel Berry
title: Brilliant
body:
Brilliant, so helpful.
What a great customer service person.
Quick response, felt like a friend was helping me.

author: Louisa Marie Juckes
title: Providing little at a large cost
body:
Decent platform, buggy app, polite yet poor customer service, questionable insurance, ridiculous fees.

I first rented through FatLlama and had a pleasant experience and now rent out camera equipment of my own through it. I have lost many rentals because they require renters to get their own insurance and prove identity etc (fair enough) but it never gets done quick enough. I have had people return equipment late and instead of getting me the money for the extra days they said "well they've returned it so you're okay". Most recently the increase to 25% of both the renter and borrowers fee is insane! I'm the one who spent money on my equipment, has paid insurance on it and is spending the time arranging collection and pickups. Yes, you are a third party providing some security and the website and app (buggy) for us to use but that is not worthy of taking essentially 40% of the money. I have looked for alternatives but they are lucky they don't have competition.
reply:
Hi Louisa,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback. 

We hope you can appreciate the verification process in terms of protecting yourself as a lender. We always strive to get this done as quickly but efficiently as possible. In terms of late fee's, Fat Llama do not guarantee this value but we will pursue them on your behalf, please see this FAQ for more info: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/3415479-report-a-late-return-case

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

We'd like to hear your feedback on the app being buggy, as we always want to improve the experience for the user so please feel free to email resolutions@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Tony Boyle
title: Good concept poorly executed.
body:
It's a good company/idea that's just run by the wrong people.

It's buggy, their insurance is questionable, and support are next to useless on most topics. The ethics of the company are also questionable at best.

After lockdown started I had multiple messages from people trying to rent stuff from me and Fatlama were basically encouraging people to still be out and about renting by not closing/shutting down rentals (as confirmed by support chat). Pure greed during a pandemic.

Fee increase from 15% to 25% per side was a massive no no and a lot of us are looking to move our equipment away with our own rental insurance. If you rent some equipment out for £80 then you only see £60 of that, while the renter is then charged £100. So fatlama making a £40 fee of a £80 rental. Basically they are making 50% off your proposed rental fee which is rediculous.

Buggy, greedy, unethical, but a good concept that would be great in different hands. Fatlama are basically their own worst enemy.
reply:
Hi Tony,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback for us. 

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

As a herd community, we advised extra precautions with rentals to ensure the safety and wellbeing of all our users during lockdown, including non-contact handovers and taking advantage of our partnership with the courier company, Gophr at the time for a discount in getting items delivered. We also reintroduced contactless delivery with our delivery partners at Orkestro. 

In times of crisis it is important to stay connected, look after one another, and prioritise our wellbeing. From spending time in isolation, to re-establishing our work-life balances, we were all adapting to a slightly unknown way of life. Fat Llama wanted to be available for users to rent items that would make  life easier throughout lockdown, such as computer accessories, eco-transportation and games.

If you have any further feedback we would be happy to hear this, please email info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: frank
title: Good service, too high commission…
body: It’s a great service but the commission charges are way too high to the point of greedy. £30 commission on an item where I receive £50????

author: F L
title: Easy and straightforward
body: My last transaction was easy and straightforward (for a same day borrowing). Yes, there is a bit of anxiety about lending out your expensive kit to a complete stranger, but I also know Fat Llama have me covered fully if anything goes wrong (though not experienced this for myself, thankfully!). Would recommend 100%.

author: Jack Peacock
title: A happy leasor
body: I rent out my audio equipment regularly with Fat Llama and the experience has so far always been smooth. The renters have always been considerate with my equipment and the platform functions well.

author: David K Mugo
title: Do not use fat llama for anything…
body:
Do not use fat llama for anything important like weddings, birthdays or video shoots. The company does nothing to ensure that renters have to turn up. Renters can decide to not turn up on the day because they received a better job, and the company will do nothing to help fix any damage a no show can do to your lives. 

If you’re a renter happy days. Accept as many offers as you want and decide the one you want to turn up to. All you’ll have to do is pay the money back and set up a new account.

Never had such a bad experience with any company ever.
reply:
Hi David,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback.

We are sorry that you've had a negative experience on Fat Llama with a particular lender. As a peer-to-peer lead community platform, lenders do have the right to cancel if something comes up or if they are unable to carry out the rental. We understand this is frustrating for you and we will be happy to hear feedback on specific lenders and make sure it's not a common habit. We will also be happy to help you find a replacement item from a different lender, just contact our support team and we will be more than happy to help.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Max Dent
title: Works great
body: Nothing hidden, works as intended.

author: Warren Berchie
title: Be vigilant, reap rewards.
body: As long as you're vigilant and do your research shouldn't have any problems, great sharing community. Saved us 100's.

author: Eddie
title: Zero protection for renter. Definitely think twice before using.
body:
I have been using Fat Llama for a while now. I originally thought it was a great service that allowed you to rent equipment for a cheaper price than the big rental companies. That is no longer the case.

I recently got stuck with a £600 bill for damage caused to an item that got damaged during transportation. Fat Llama offer zero insurance for the borrowers and should an accident occur then the borrower is liable to cover all costs of repair or replacement. 

As they repeatedly reminded me, there is a section in the terms and conditions that states the borrower is liable for all damage etc. But you're unable to use the service without accepting the terms and conditions and does anybody really read the terms and conditions? I will look closer at T&Cs from now on before going ahead and using a company like this.

Rent items on Fat Llama at your own risk and read the T&Cs before you do. Accidents happen and they will not help you in any way should one occur.
reply:
Hi Eddie,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback.

I'm afraid currently, our Guarantee only covers the Lenders. We are sorry that you feel this wasn't transparent enough and we will aim to make this clearer to borrowers in future. We have been working with an insurance company and suggesting this to borrowers for higher value items, but we will take on your feedback and work on ways to make users more aware of this.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Kieran Hanson
title: A good service
body: A good service, rented out dozens of items for almost a year and never had any problems. ID verification seems to work well and all users I have encountered (renting camera/sound kit) have been honest and respectful. Support are able to sort out most technical issues fairly quickly. App continues to be a work in progress and can be frustrating at times, but works well for the most part. Recent hike in fees hasn't been welcome, I hope at least it results in improvements to the app and a drive to increase users.

author: Paul Perelka
title: Amazing website
body: Amazing website. It's been over a year since I've started using Fat Lama and never had any issues! Brilliant idea for a business x

author: OSHO Visuals
title: Great service
body: easy and straight forward

author: Marcus Ronchi
title: Good service / need some updates / high fees.
body:
I had a Good time using Fatllama and even when one of my equipment returned broken the issuance system was quick and easy.

The website is quite slow but the app works well usually. They could give more freedom to the landers regarding to modify a rental, right now you always need to talk with customer service to modify something and that’s annoying, and they could add the option to the borrower choose the time to pick up and return and the option for the lander change this times of necessary. 

Once I have a situation that could have ended bad, I guy with a new account came and send a request, I have accepted trusting on their ID check and it was all set, but on the day of the rental I received a message from them saying that the account was not checked and that the rental was cancelled, it was on the day, my lucky was that the guy would come to pick up hour after I received this email, but he could have come before and them my rental cancelled. It was a huge failure, I didn’t understand why Fatllama allowed a rental be approved before check the account! 


Unfortunately their fees were high already, and now it’s even higher so it’s not worthy work with them anymore.
reply:

In regards to the fee's we're sorry that you feel like it's not worth it, we wanted to share this article with you which will hopefully explain our reasoning behind the fee increase: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

The Fat Llama Team

author: Colin Steele
title: Such a great site
body: Such a great site - being able to get pro quality equipment for low day rental rates. Every owner renting me equipment has been helpful and easy to deal with.

author: Jamie
title: FatLlama is good but fees are brutal
body:
FatLlama is legitimate and works well. Their support is good and I've had no problems with rentals so far. However, their new fee structure is punitive. They charge 25% service fee to you and 25% to the renter. This is huge and definitely puts people off. They used to charge 15% to each which I thought was still high but not so bad. Them making 40% on every rental is crazy and you feel taken advantage of a bit since this has increased. I've personally started looking for alternative options for renting my stuff due to these new fees. 

Another downside is it would be good to have more flexibility in regards to the rental such as editing dates etc... when customers contact you as currently you have to chat to support to do this which although they are very good just seems inefficient. The website is also quite clunky. The app is better but I don't think it has all the features that the site as such as generating discount codes but this could be hidden somewhere in the app. I would also say that I don't actually get a huge number of rentals through them so don't feel they're pushing these fees towards heavy advertising of their service to get more people on the site. It probably is fine in London but outside (I'm still in a major city) not so much. Overall they're a good site and I've had good experiences but just me aware of the fees which now feel punitive, I didn't realise at first they were charging me and the renter, and don't expect huge volumes of rentals outside London. I've had about 3 in 3 months.
reply:

Thanks for taking the time to leave this feedback for us. 

We really appreciate your feedback on the improvements we can make to the app and the website. This is always passed onto our product team, who are constantly working on updates to deploy to make your experience a much smoother one. We are glad you've been enjoying the site so far and hope to see you get more rentals as we grow.

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully answer some of your questions: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

If you have any further feedback please don't hesitate to get in touch.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

title: Great company

author: Anthony Hurren
title: Great Platform
body: Great, effeciant service and user freindly platform

author: C. K.
title: Very Bad Communication & Privacy Concerns
body: I submitted a request to borrow some camera equipment. At first, there were some pretty standard verification documents to provide - driving license, proof of address and a video of yourself. I was happy to comply and quite happy that they complete these checks. What then followed was what has compelled me to leave feedback on my experience. First of all, I was asked to submit a proof of income. I chose to submit a payslip and the instructions were to not blur anything. Quite concerning as this includes my NI number. Secondly, they ask to verify you through social media accounts which is incredibly intrusive for those of us with private accounts (and in my opinion does not prove anything - what do my holiday snaps over the past 10 years prove?). Nonetheless I complied and then had what can only be described as a telephone interview on my rationale for requesting the products. All seemed fine and I thought that would be it: They have my license, current address, employer details & salary (which is more than enough to replace the equipment multiple times over), national insurance number, photos of me and my family and a recorded telephone conversation. That should be enough, right? Wrong. My application was rejected with zero reasoning provided, and my account was deleted and locked. I have emailed the company but no response. I have deep concerns about a company that will ask you to provide so much information and hold it on their files and then delete your ability to log in and become unresponsive.
reply:

In terms of verification, social media can be used to help establish someone’s identity and/or work, which helps to improve their Trustworthy Score so that we can verify the transaction. We might ask you to verify your place of work as well in order to boost your Trustworthy Score.

The Fat Llama Team

author: Kat
title: They have a photo!!!
body:
So this is how it Works. You rent an item  you have chosen from a photo, but  in actual fact the lender posts an item of ‘ similar’ appearance which is also broken. 
The recipient informs Fat Llama it is the wrong item and broken. But they state that can’t be the case as the lender has a photo. ARE YOU GETTING THE PICTURE? 
Fat Llama obviously believe their unscrupulous lender and demand payment for an item never received based on the fact they have a picture!
 
Fat Llama opens the doors to invite immoral, unethical scammers in and not only condone these SCAMs but supports them. 

If you like a bit of dodgy dealing this is the place to be. If you rent from here be ready for false accusations because apparently all lenders are honest people and they have a photo, so why wouldn’t they be?

Be prepared, that nice shiny camera you think you are renting might turn out to be a battered old iron in the box, but the photo shows the camera you always wanted to play with. So be prepared to pay up!

Please avoid this scam and don’t believe the lender reviews.

Additionally keep an eye on Experian once you have shared you precious personal ID.

author: Brendan Smith
title: Don't expect a fast payout.
body:
As someone looking for goods and services to rent this app is amazing. A unique business model that gives you access to some amazing stuff. 

If you're renting your kit out it's less impressive. Some fairly hefty fees and quite frankly abysmal payout times often see a rental submitted, lent and returned before the money hits your account. It's pretty ridiculous that in 2020 a company holds funds for 3-4 days before payment. For something that is supposed to disrupt the norm the banking practices are straight out of 1972.

A nice idea but the execution needs work to make this seamless for both sides of the equation.

*Updated after reply from Fat Llama*

Firstly, absolutely how dare you advertise the details of my transactions on a public forum. You've no right to inform anyone viewing this post as to whether I have made money from rentals or not. You might want to check yourself before you're on the end of a GDPR breach. 

Secondly, if you'd taken the time to comprehend my review you would see that my gripe stems from the fact that the delay is unreasonable, not that it isn't Outlined in a document. 

As I said, this is a great app as a renter. You've access to All sorts of equipment with ease. As someone renting equipment out you are at the whim of the system. I believe that in 2020 payments should be instant like they are for almost absolutely other purchase in the World. 

You've been given three stars because that's what I feel you're worth. I have informed readers of the pros and cons in a respectable manner. 

Please don't come leaping to your own defence with borderline illegal information about my user experience. Either make your workflow better or be happy with 3 star reviews.
reply:
Hi Brendan,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback. We are sorry that you feel this way.

We make it clear in our FAQ's on 'How much does it cost to rent on Fat Llama?' & 'How and when do lenders get paid?' which you can easily find online, that we take a 15% fee from the rental from the lender and the borrower and it takes 3-4 working days for the lender to be paid. We also share this information when you're notified that you've been paid. These are 'working days' so sometimes weekends and bank holidays can effect the payout times. 

Please get in touch with us to share any further feedback at info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reply:
