author: Chuck
title: Beware of the hidden money grabs
body: Beware of the hidden money grabs. Outdoorsy advertises affordable rates, but their checkout process is loaded with undisclosed service fees, ambiguous platform charges, and forced insurance add-ons. They do everything they can to inflate the final price. Very disappointed by the lack of pricing transparency. I had originally booked and was paying $217/day (I calculated this myself by taking the full price I paid and divided over the days). When I added one more day to the trip a week later, they charged me $263 for the extra day. When I contacted the customer service team they couldn’t explain the reason for the increased fees. They said the same generic “taxes, service fees, and insurance” but couldn’t break down or show me how the service fees had increased $46 for adding a single day, even when accounting for the insurance and sales tax. And you can’t pay the host directly because they force you to go through their platform so they can collect the service fees. I reached out to the host directly and he didn’t mention raising his daily rate but said I had to go through the website. I understand the business model but if you want repeat customers you need to earn trust… and Outdoorsy has failed to do that.
reply:
Hi Chuck!

Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective with us. 

Please allow me to clarify that Outdoorsy operates as a peer-to-peer marketplace platform. This means that, aside from our standard 10% service fee, hosts independently determine nightly rates and additional fees associated with their listings. 

While we are unable to authenticate the specific experience described based on the information you provided to this platform, generally speaking, nightly rate fluctuations can sometimes occur due to host-configured pricing settings, such as weekend pricing, holiday rates, seasonal adjustments, or other automated pricing factors.

Transparency is vitally important to us, which is why Outdoorsy provides a full cost breakdown prior to booking confirmation. This includes nightly rates, guest fees, optional host add-ons, insurance protection costs, taxes, applicable service fees, etc., so guests can review the complete pricing details and ensure they are fully informed of the total reservation cost before completing their booking.

We encourage you to contact our support team directly and verify your account information so we may review the reservation details more thoroughly and provide additional clarification specific to your booking.

Thanks again for taking a moment to share your feedback! It's greatly appreciated.
 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Sixto Joost
title: Well, here's what happened to me
body: Well, somebody should have told me how to actually complete the rental. I chose an RV, input my dates, received a cost summary but couldn't find a way to actually make the deal. Tried dealing with the WORST AI experience I've ever had (and AI is ra rarely a good experience but dont get me started). After an eternity of trying to make the deal, I was offered a phone number. I called it and met a nice guy who helped me navigate the peculiar procedure used by this company. I had to "register" but was never told this on the website; I was directed to messaging the owner who never messaged me back. I put my phone number in the message bc I wanted to arrange to SEE the vehicle in person before I committed 4000 dollars. Two things: at this point I was creating a process based on many experiences of buying and renting stuff on the internet and doing it without any direction from Outdoorsy. Well, somehow I failed.  Then, I got an email: "you haven't completed rental...."  Against my better judgment, I took the bait. I was offered AI disguised with a human name. Finally, I know what AI is going to do; it's going to make murderers of us all.  Now, after hours of effort, I still have my money but no RV.  Anyone out there willing to rent an RV to a retired attorney with the highest credit rating available, money to burn,and passionately wanting to experience RVing for a couple of weeks?  I'm your guy.
reply:
Hi there,

I'm very sorry to hear you had trouble completing your reservation and that your experience with our chat service did not provide the level of support you were looking for. While our AI is designed to handle quick requests, it is certainly not meant to replace human support. If you find that the AI is not meeting your needs, simply type "live agent" or "representative" in the chat, and you will be connected to a specialist immediately.

Additionally, I'd like to reassure you that our 24/7 Customer Support number, 877-723-7232, is clearly indicated on every page of our website to ensure you can always reach our live support team directly.

I'd love the opportunity to learn more about your experience; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available here. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" in the subject line, and I'll be more than happy to assist you with your booking personally.

I look forward to connecting with you. Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback.

Outdoorsy Support

author: James Krickeberg
title: Hosts can cancel at last minute without…
body: Hosts can cancel at last minute without penalty leaving buyers in a terrible position. Customer service is an AI bot. Awful. Will never use again after this rental.
reply:
Hi James,

We’re really sorry you had to deal with your original booking being canceled, we know that’s never a great experience and can throw a wrench into your plans. While we do everything we can to prevent cancellations, there are times when something unexpected comes up, like in this case where the host’s tow vehicle needed repairs after a prior rental. We completely understand how frustrating that can feel, especially when you’re counting on everything going smoothly. That’s exactly why we have a team dedicated to helping guests through last-minute changes and getting things back on track as quickly as possible. We also take cancellations seriously on the host side, and they can face impacts to their rating along with potential financial penalties.

That said, we’re really glad you were able to find a replacement so quickly, that’s always a relief in situations like this. As for the chat experience, our AI is there to help with quick questions, but it’s definitely not meant to replace a real person. If it ever feels like it’s not getting you where you need to go, just type “live agent” or “representative” and you’ll be connected right away. You can also call our Customer Support team at 877-723-7232 anytime, we’re here 24/7 and always happy to help.

Thanks so much for taking the time to share your experience with us, we really do appreciate it.

Outdoorsy Support

author: Agustin Pinsak
title: I have been cancelled by the host not…
body:

I lost my rv camping fees. My wife flew from toronto for this trip for her birthday. Such a joke. Stay away from this site. 
The Funny thing is the host messaged me the day before to confirm. Haha. Great service outdoorsy!! Keep it up, 👍

Hi Agustin,

After reviewing the situation, it appears the host canceled due to a stolen battery and delays in securing a replacement. While we recognize the circumstances the host faced, the delayed communication is deeply concerning, and appropriate actions have been taken to address this directly with the host. This should have been communicated much earlier so you had more time to make alternative plans.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this caused. A full refund has been issued to your original form of payment, and I’ll be reaching out directly to address any additional concerns and see how we can better support your future travel plans. Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback with us.

Outdoorsy Support

author: Sarah R
title: We’re considering buying an RV and wanted to try one out
body:
We’re considering buying an RV, and since it’s such an expensive purchase, we wanted to rent one first to see if it’s the right fit. That’s why we chose Outdoorsy.

Everything looked great at first — until I actually clicked on the RV listings. An RV advertised at $90 per night for two nights somehow totaled over $700 at checkout. I was within the delivery range and didn’t select any upgrades, so the massive jump in price made absolutely no sense. The hidden fees are outrageous — unlike anything I’ve seen before. It feels incredibly deceptive.

To make matters worse, the app/website kept glitching throughout the process. Pages froze, totals changed, and it was just a frustrating experience overall. With how much they’re charging customers in fees, you’d think they could at least provide a properly functioning platform.

The whole experience left such a bad impression that we’ll be looking elsewhere to rent an RV. Extremely disappointing
reply:
Hi Sarah,

 Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback. Transparency is very important to us, so we’re sorry to hear the final total was a surprise. 

 We'd like the opportunity to review the listing you were looking at; however, we're unable to find your account with the information provided here. Additionally, we’d love to get more details about your experience with our app and website. Our product development team is always striving to make our platform as efficient as possible, so your feedback is valuable.

Please contact us directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with 'ATTN: DALE' in the subject line so we can address your concerns. We look forward to hearing from you soon!

author: Charese Taylor
title: DO NOT RENT YOUR RIGG WITH OUTDOORSY
body:
I was a host for Outdoorsy for 3.5 years, with a 5 Star rating and used to love providing an experience for renters, with discounts and free add-ons.  I have had a lot inexperienced renters in the past, and most which have damaged my RV.   Outdoorsy agreed to pay for damages and put my RV back to it's original state, but each incident Outdoorsy/Roamly paid less and less of the expenses, and in the end realized that I have had to over $6000 in damages to my RV. Replaced my awning twice, new water heater, new door handles, CO2 unit, table, generator, back support bar, ladder, and the list goes on, due to lack of proof a renter caused the damage.  If I could suggest to anyone, do not rent your RV with Outdoorsy and be weary renter of  all the hidden fees, it does not go to the host, the host only get less than 50% of what you have to pay. 

So when a renter hit a Gator and took off the side of my door, and only paid for the replacement and not the paint, which I had to pay out of pocket.  OR when a renter ran into a tree and took off my awning and damaged the back of the RV and it was said to be  two different claims and Outdoorsy only paid for the awning and not the back of the RV, which I had to pay for.  OR this last time when my RV was hit so hard that the door to the Generator was torn off and messed up my Adjuster Arm and Alignment, relocated my generator, but Outdoorsy would not pay for that either, and I had to come out of pocket once again.  The last time was just the last time, not the many other times.  Like when my renter busted my water heater or another one yanked off my door handle.  I can name plenty more, but for the sake of space I digress. DO NOT RENT YOUR RV WITH OUTDOORSY!!!!
reply:
Hi Charese,

We're sorry to hear that you're dissatisfied with the payment for your claim. Thank you for sharing your experience and allowing us the opportunity to clarify the matter further. Upon closer review of the adjuster's report, it appears the invoice submitted for the supplement request does not provide evidence of impact damage or confirm that the failure resulted from the rental incident.

Insurance coverage is strictly limited to the specific damages that occurred during the booking in question. If you have any further questions or need clarification, please feel free to reach back out to your adjuster directly within the email thread where you've been communicating.

Outdoorsy Support

Outdoorsy Support

author: Bill Moerschfelder
title: Outdoorsy - Beware !!!
body:
I would strongly urge everyone to stay away from this company. THIS COMPANY TOOK $1500 FROM ME.  Read below.  I would give this company ZERO STARS if it was possible. 

Please do yourself a favor and READ COMPLAINTS ABOUT THIS COMPANY ON THE BBB don't just look at their rating. I AM NOT THE ONLY ONE. They get a good rating on BBB because they give a customer service impression they are helping, but nothing get's done.
In my efforts to resolve this problem everyone that I spoke with, in my opinion, seems to WILLFULLY MISUNDERSTAND even simple concepts. In my opinion they seem to GASLIGHT intentionally and never get the problem resolved.

My experience with them was the absolute worst customer experience I have ever had, I am in my 50s. It is NOT the customer service aspect, but COMPLETELY related to the companies business practices. They will be pleasant on the phone, all the while not doing getting any results in assisting with the problem.

This company TOOK a $1500 security deposit, TWICE (it was only supposed to be taken once.) and  ------- they have NOT RETURNED IT TO ME -------   AFTER MONTHS of effort.  They returned one of the $1500 but not the second one. Every time I try to speak to them, even though I am clear about the first one being returned, they seem to have difficulty in understanding that I am wanting the second $1500.  PLEASE NOTE: Them keeping it has nothing to do with any issue related to the RV when I returned it.

I am using the bank's method of dispute, but this is also taking longer than it should and I am in the dark about it's status.  But this is a lot of time / effort on my part as well as the loss of money. DO YOURSELF A FAVOUR DON"T SET YOURSELF UP FOR MONTHS OF PROBLEMS AND PHONE CALLS AND FRUSTRATION.

-----UPDATE -------------------------------------------
Hello, I see that you have responded which is what Outdoorsy does to get a good BBB rating they respond quickly which is nice, however no results.

Would you like to resolve this issue openly on this board ? So people can see ? If I could I would paste screenshots / share with anyone who asks.  I have already sent them to Outdoorsy, yet as seen by the response, they have willfully ignored them again.

First: I did not use a credit card for my security deposit, I used my bank account ( I have explained this). If you looked at my case on your system you would know that.

Second: I have sent proof of screenshots showing my bank account transactions and my bank has confirmed there was no hold, the money was taken perhaps this may be a difference in handling the deposit between a credit card and a bank account:

AUG 22, 2025 - $ 1500 was taken by Outdoorsy
Aug 29, 2025 - $1500 was taken by Outdoorsy
Sept 02, 2025 - $1500 was refunded by Outdoorsy Sept 02, 2025 - $165 was taken by Outdoorsy 
 (the $165 = ancillary fee)

-----UPDATE---- 
There has been no update! lol. See they respond but don't assist.
reply:
Hi Bill,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience and allow us the opportunity to clarify the security deposit authorization hold process, as previously explained by our customer support team.

We can confirm that only one authorization hold in the amount of $1,500 was placed on your credit card. This hold was not a charge. It was an authorization to temporarily reserve funds and, as designed, the same authorization amount was reauthorized seven days later. This does not constitute a second charge, as no charge occurs during an authorization hold unless the host submits a valid claim.

Authorization holds automatically expire if no claims are submitted for damages, ancillary fees, or overages. Because these holds are not charges, the funds do not appear as a credit on your account when released. Instead, the hold simply expires and the funds become available to you again.

In this instance, the host claimed a small portion of the security deposit for ancillary dumping charges, which were disclosed in the listing and agreed to at the time of booking. The remaining balance of your security deposit was released successfully on August 30, 2025.

If you require confirmation of when the authorization fully cleared on your account, we recommend contacting your credit card issuer directly, as processing timelines vary by financial institution. Thanks again for your perspective.

Outdoorsy Support

Donna 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Stephanie Rolfo
title: Outdoorsy claims to "secure a deposit"
body: Outdoorsy claims to "secure a deposit", however they will only "secure" it for 7 days from the time of the credit card hold. However, if the rental or claim goes beyond that 7 day period, then they won't cover it. They never told me this. In fact, when the renter brought back the unit late, not dumped, excessively dirty, damaged, and missing jacks, I placed a claim and was told the 7 days would be pushed out to 28 days so I could assess damage. I assessed damages and then was told they could no longer secure the deposit. This is fraudulent and I hope we can ban together to hold Outdoorsy responsible for breach of contract, negligence, and misrepresentation
reply:
Hi Stephanie,

Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and giving us the chance to clear things up about how our security deposit authorization holds work. The authorization renews itself every seven days by default, unless it’s claimed at the end of the trip or seven days have passed after the trip with no claim. At that point, the hold simply drops off and is released back to the guest.

If a 30-day hold was placed, that timeline overrides the seven-day renewals. In your case, because a chargeback was filed by the guest with their financial institution, the bank automatically declined the reauthorization, which caused the hold to release. That part of the process is fully controlled by the bank, not us.

We truly appreciate you sharing your perspective and giving us the opportunity to clarify this process, as our support team and management reviewed with you earlier.

Outdoorsy Support

author: L. K.
title: Do better and be less greedy
body: The outdoorsy host app has continuously gotten more difficult to use. Pictures can no longer be uploaded, they must be taken at the time of handoff, the messaging functionality is atrocious (difficult to use through the app, I typically have to use a PC to message people back which reduces my response time), and the calendar/editing features are difficult and often not full featured like the web-based site. It is annoying that outdoorsy takes such a large cut for providing very little to hosts. Also, they purchased a large RV park near me that used to allow me to dump my RV there and Outdoorsy no longer allows public dumping (even for a fee), very annoying since it is one of a few places that exist in my area to dump. This company has gotten greedier since I started listing my RV with them and provides very little host benefits.
reply:
Hi Luke,

Thank you so much for sharing your feedback. I'm very sorry to hear you’ve encountered issues with our app. We’ve recently released a newer version, so please update it in the App Store to see if this resolves the problem. Each update includes new features and performance improvements, so hopefully this will enhance your experience. 

To ensure a more secure and transparent handoff and return process, all photos must now be taken live within the app. This update helps confirm that each image accurately reflects the vehicle’s condition in real time at the moment of documentation.

Please allow me to clarify some of the benefits that are included with our service fees. For these fees, we provide both guests and hosts with 24/7 international customer support via chat, email, and phone. In addition, both parties receive the security of knowing that we have dedicated teams handling claims, disputes, breakdowns, roadside assistance, driver verification, platform marketing, access to our Concierge team, insurance coverage, and many more; all at no additional cost to you. 

Thanks again for taking a moment to share your thoughts, and please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team if you have any additional questions or concerns.

author: Nick Mansour
title: Be warned…
body:
Be warned…

They don’t allow bad trip reviews on their site

They also don’t refund rental days if the vehicle breaks down, and tell you you have to look after broken down vehicle 

Don’t be fooled - as shady a way of running a business as it gets

MY RESPONSE TO OUTDOORSY RESPONSE

They say they’ve given me a significant courtesy refund, which was $500. But they charged me a total of $2,600 for days the vehicle was broken down. Don’t be fooled…
reply:
Hi Nick,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear you had a negative experience. We strive incredibly hard to make every guest experience enjoyable, so thank you for taking the time to provide us with your feedback. While we understand that going through the dispute process can be stressful, I’m pleased to see that the Resolution Specialist was able to assist by issuing you a significant courtesy refund directly from Outdoorsy. Thanks again for taking a moment to share your perspective; it's greatly appreciated!

author: Scott Reighard
title: Outdoorsy is a good platform
body: Outdoorsy is a good platform, but I am not happy about their review situation. I search all over the place to try to find a place where I can send my customer a review to fill out, and I can never find it. I would have many more reviews if they were easily available.
reply:
Hi Scott,

Thanks so much for your feedback, and I’m so glad to hear you’re enjoying the platform! I’d like to reassure you that upon return, all of your guests automatically receive a convenient review link within their booking details, making it quick and easy for them to share their experience. You might consider reminding them to click that link at their earliest convenience to let others know how their trip went and to share feedback about your accommodations. Thanks again for taking a moment to share your thoughts! It's greatly appreciated. 

Donna 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Pete from the sticks
title: Superhost Screwed
body: Nearly 5 years on the platform, over 80 rentals with over 4 different units, superhost x 2. I host on multiple sites and have a business structured around these platforms. The host fees are out of control, they take over 20% even if I charge the renter to replace something after the rental or to conduct a service (like a lost fork, or a dump fee, or my service of delivery). But here is the worst, a recent rental had a major blowout when the renter was towing, causing about $1800 in damage. Not only did outdoorsy not accept their "excess supplemental" claim after the renter was denied by their own insurance, nor my damage claim, they also released the security deposit back to the renter. So I am stuck with having to reach out and beg the renter to cover some of the cost. Your trailers are at risk, your business is at risk, don't listen to social media, it is not worth it.
reply:
Hi Pete,

I’m very sorry to hear that your experience with our platform was less than ideal due to a damage claim. Our goal is always to provide a smooth and straightforward claims process, and we truly appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective.

Please allow me to clarify some of the benefits that are included with the service fees. For these fees, we provide both guests and hosts with 24/7 international customer support via chat, email, and phone. In addition, both parties receive the security of knowing that we have dedicated teams handling claims, disputes, breakdowns, roadside assistance, driver verification, platform marketing, access to our Concierge team, and insurance coverage, all at no additional cost to you. Thanks again for taking a moment to share your thoughts, and please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team if you have any additional questions or concerns. 

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

author: Mellissa Edney
title: So far so good
body:
Most of our bookings have come through this platform, so I’ve been putting extra time and care into refining our listing here. The customer service team has been responsive and helpful whenever I’ve had questions, and guests seem to trust the Outdoorsy platform, which gives me confidence as a host.

I do wish the service fees weren’t taken from the delivery and cleaning portions since those are owner-provided services, not part of Outdoorsy’s offering. It would make more sense if fees were only deducted from the booking itself. Still, I understand that the platform provides valuable peace of mind, insurance coverage, and customer support.

Overall, I’m grateful for the opportunity to connect with great renters through this service and plan to continue using it as we grow our own client base.

title: Very good service, but needs a few tweaks to be great.
body: Outdoorsy has a very good platform for helping camper-owners rent out their campers when not in use. The website is great, the app needs work. On the app there is no way to switch from guest to host, which makes the app nearly useless as a host. The only other complaint I have is the paperwork; Outdoorsy requests the host to print out a plethora of documents for both the host and the guest to sign/initial. This should all be done digitally. Printing 20+ pages is a waste of paper and ink for the host, which cuts into the profit margin.

author: geoff hardies
title: Watch out for the fees
body: I hadn’t notice the insurance charge until after signing up.  I attempted to have that removed and at the same time read reviews of the company which prompted me to cancel.  The cancellation fee was 25%.  The rental fee was $340 so to me the fee should be $85, but no they count the prep fee and insurance in that cancellation.  This is not a company I want anything to do with or will recommend.
reply:
Hi Geoff,

Thank you so much for your feedback. Please allow me to clarify that Outdoorsy operates as a peer-to-peer platform. This means that aside from our standard 10% service fee, all other fees are set directly by the host, including their chosen cancellation policy, as we offer three different tiers to suit various preferences.

We also provide three insurance protection packages designed to accommodate different budget and coverage needs, as well as stationary coverage options for rentals that are delivered and not intended to be moved during the trip. Removal of Outdoorsy’s insurance protection after a booking is confirmed is possible, provided the host agrees to accept the guest’s personal carrier in lieu of our protection package.

Thank you again for taking the time to share your perspective; we truly appreciate it.

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

author: Jan Peterson
title: Outdoorsy used to be amazing back when…
body: Outdoorsy used to be amazing back when we started using it. Unfortunately over time, the app has only gotten worse as the customer service phone number and chat link were taken down. Now to have to go to the internet and dig for the customer support number. Hiding your customer service information is a disgrace of a business move. Why?…. The app has glitches when entering information where you can’t even see what you’re typing until you hit enter. Adding photos has become more difficult. It’s like to tried to make the customer experience worse every time you update the app. Stop nickel and diming your tools and customer service and charging more for your services. Go back to actually being available for your customers.
reply:
Hi Jan,

Thank you so much for sharing your feedback, and my apologies for any frustration this has caused; that’s definitely not the experience we want our hosts to have.

I’d like to reassure you that our contact information is still easily accessible through the Chat, Email, or Call link found at the bottom of every page. Once you click the link, simply type “phone number” or “support,” and our contact number will appear; no digging required. My apologies for any confusion this may have caused.

As for the app, we’ve recently released a newer version (5.3.1). Please update your app in the App Store to ensure you have the latest features and performance improvements, which should help enhance your experience.

Lastly, I’d like to clarify some of the benefits included with these fees. They help provide both guests and hosts with 24/7 international customer support via chat, email, and phone. In addition, both parties receive the peace of mind that comes with having dedicated teams for claims, disputes, breakdowns, roadside assistance, driver verification, platform marketing, our Concierge team, and insurance coverage, all at no additional cost to you.

Thank you again for taking the time to share your thoughts, and please don’t hesitate to reach out to our support team if you have any additional questions or concerns.

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

author: Amaya Travel & RV
title: Hosting on Outdoorsy has been a great…
body:
Hosting on Outdoorsy has been a great experience! The platform makes it simple to connect with trustworthy renters, manage bookings, and handle payments smoothly. I’ve especially appreciated how easy it is to communicate with guests and how Outdoorsy’s insurance and support team give me peace of mind every time I rent out my vehicle.
Outdoorsy does an excellent job of creating a reliable, community-focused environment for both hosts and renters. Their customer service is responsive, the app is user-friendly, and the whole process—from listing to payout—is seamless. It’s clear they truly care about both protecting hosts and delivering a great experience for travelers.

title: Happy
body: I liked that all correspondence is through outdoorsy.

author: Katherine  Sanchez
title: I’ve had such an amazing experience…
body:
I’ve had such an amazing experience with Outdoorsy! It’s truly an outstanding platform where I rent out all of my RVs, and I’ve had the pleasure of meeting so many wonderful customers through this site—each of them has taken great care of my RVs and made the experience rewarding and enjoyable.

Outdoorsy makes the entire process simple, professional, and secure, both for owners and renters. It’s an excellent place to list your RV if you’re looking to make extra income while sharing your love for RVing with others.

I’m proud to be a Superhost on Outdoorsy, and I highly recommend them to anyone considering renting out their RV or planning their next adventure.

author: cbinaustomer
title: Outdoorsy's secondary insurance
body:
I just had a renter who had to pay out of pocket for damage to 1 of my levelers. he bought the insurance that was offered on the site but for some reason, his claim was rejected...... Your insurance is considered primary to the outdoorsy secondary insurance and my renter did not want to lose their discount for not having claims for a long time. That's probably why he bought insurance on the outdoorsy site. So now my renter paid for damages out of pocket in addition to the nearly six thousand dollars that he spent for renting my r.V for a month. i am seriously considering not having your insurance as a requirement for my renters. it's ridiculous for them to buy insurance that they don't want to use.....

in response to outdoorsy information.... Yes, the claim was canceled at the request of the renter. you were about ready to make a claim on his primary.Insurance. if you could include that information, there would be a clearer picture of what happened. ok.
reply:
Hi Alexander,

My sincere apologies for the negative experience — that’s certainly not the outcome we want our hosts to have. We work incredibly hard to ensure every customer interaction is a positive one, so thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. Upon review, it appears the claim in question was canceled following your call and email to the claims department, in which you requested closure and advised that the guest would be making payment directly to you and filing a claim with their personal insurance carrier at a later date. Please feel free to follow up directly with the adjuster you’ve been communicating with for any additional claim-related questions or concerns. Thank you again for taking a moment to share your feedback and allowing us the opportunity to provide clarification.

Donna 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Patrick N
title: Renting from outdoorsy will put your safety at risk
body:
Rented a sprinter from a well rated host in oregon. Host was strange, but thorough and seemed to take very good care of the rv.  Only downside that the host pointed out was the older tires with worn tread, but he assured us for our trip that it would be fine. He was getting them replaced next week.  Tires were polished, and pressure was ok. I trusted their opinion of the state of the vehicle.

45mins on the highway, the left rear tire had an extreme tire shoulder wall failure at 60mph on a highway. The whole vehicle swerved, nearly tipped, but I regained control and brought it to the side.  Could have been seriously injured. My family was in the back seat.

Deeper inspection of the tires showed the surviving tires shoulder wall tread was at/below the legal road limit, cracked, and risking further blowout.  The blown tire was completely separated from it's treads. During the separation, the treads damaged the body of the sprinter. I swapped the spare, and took it to a tire center for further evaluation.  Expert there said all tires risked further blowout, they need immediate replacement, but you should be able to get back home if you're careful.

Took detailed photos, documented everything, returned the vehicle cleaned the night of.  Outdoorsy claims rejected coverage of repairs since it was a non collision blowout.  Outdoorsy resolution team sided with the Host.  They claimed that after I accepted the vehicle, tires are the renters responsibility.  None of the photos or expert opinions were considered.  It does not matter if the host was negligent.  So, the host took our 1k security deposit, giving him free tires, while my family's life was put at risk.  He stated he "wouldn't pursue this further", which hurt since he put my family's safety at risk to save money.

I'm being punished since I didn't catch this during inspection. The fact that the host did not provide a road legal vehicle for rental wasn't considered by the dispute resolution team.

Here is what I've learned from discussions with outdoorsy's dispute resolution team:

1. Outdoorsy is not in the business of ensuring a safe rental experience, rather they want to ensure an adequate supply of happy Hosts for rentals.
2. Safety is exclusively up to the Renter to determine during the short inspection period with the Host.
3. The Host is only responsible for clearly visible maintenance or safety concerns. This is in conflict with Outdoorsy own terms of service, but they will side with the Host since it's in their business best interest. Safety responsibility only applies to clearly identifiable issues during inspection. By extension, anything non-visible is the renter's responsibility. 
4. The Host will not be found liable by Outdoorsy for any issue resulting from the lack of required maintenance that can't be obviously found during the short inspection period.  Regardless of required maintenance and responsibilities clearly called out by Outdoorsy's terms of service (> 50% tire tread).
5. Any damages caused by the lack of maintenance during the booking period will be the responsibility of the renter, at the renter's cost to safety and expense, regardless of root cause.

Outdoorsy can and should do better.  

-Patrick N
reply:
Hi Patrick,

Please accept my sincere apologies for your negative experience with our dispute resolution process. This is certainly not the outcome we want our guests to have, and I appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback. At Outdoorsy, safety is paramount, and we are committed to ensuring that every dispute is handled through a fair and impartial resolution process. While I’m pleased that a significant courtesy refund was issued from Outdoorsy to help offset the costs of the RV damage repairs, I also want to reassure you that our policies and procedures are designed with the best interests of both guests and hosts in mind. To help avoid future misunderstandings, we strongly encourage all guests to carefully review our full Terms and Conditions—available on our website, in the rental agreement, and within each Outdoorsy dashboard—prior to confirming a reservation. In addition, guests are strongly encouraged to take detailed pre-trip photos to provide clear documentation. Hosts, meanwhile, are required to maintain and provide a recent safety inspection of their RV. These inspections include critical safety checks such as tire integrity, tread depth, and brake function. Each inspection must be submitted to the resolution specialist and is considered official evidence in the event of a dispute. I can confirm that the inspection report for the RV you rented was utilized to determine the final resolution decision. Together, these steps help ensure a safe, transparent, and fair experience for all. Thank you again for sharing your experience with us.

Donna
Outdoorsy Support

Donna outdoorsy agreed to pay for the extra days need to get the RV back to Oregon so there absolutely should have been no charge to us except for the insurance for the RV at two payments of 39.00 because the RV was in the auto mechanic shop for 4 days and when it came out still did not run well enough to keep up with the flow of traffic.

author: Patrick
title: We booked a rental and had to cancel
body:
We booked an RV rental in New Haven, IN through Outdoorsy in August 2025. While the initial booking process went smoothly, we encountered several concerning issues with the host.
The host repeatedly attempted to establish direct contact outside of the platform and sent unsolicited social media friend requests, which violates Outdoorsy's policies. This made us uncomfortable and raised red flags about professionalism. The day before our scheduled delivery, the host claimed we had selected the incorrect delivery option and suggested we were at fault. He recommended canceling the booking, stating he "should" be able to accept a new reservation. Given the timing and the host's expressed unavailability, we felt we had no choice but to cancel our rental the night before departure.

Fortunately, we were able to secure a rental from an established RV dealer. This experience highlighted the advantages of working with professional dealers who provide proper equipment, safety gear, and reliable service.  This included correct hitching equipment and electrical connections.

While Outdoorsy may work well for some RV owners looking to rent out their vehicles, our experience suggests potential safety and reliability concerns when dealing with individual owners versus established dealers. For peace of mind and safety assurance, we recommend considering professional RV rental companies for your next trip.
reply:
Hi Patrick,

I'm very sorry to hear you had to cancel the booking due to your experience with the host. I'd love the opportunity to investigate your feedback further and address your concerns; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available here. author: Mohamed Hasan
title: I proved I was scammed.. here’s what happened..
body:
I would give it 0. To begin, I was naive and trusting enough to not back myself up by taking photos of my rental.. turns out Outdoorsy’s platform and negligent customer service make it very easy for the Host’s to scam their guests. I didn’t even need photo proof to prove it. 

I rented a camper for one week. Th host claimed the backwater tank was faulty when I noticed the backwater was half full.. I believed her. For perspective, a Thor class C holds 30 gallons of black water.. which means a half full tank is 15 gallons (or about 57 litres). I specifically told my group, no pooping in camper. The public bathroom is just a few dozen steps away. “No poop!”, I said.. well the camper smelt like poop for 6 days and the ride home was like driving through a sewer.

Now there was a bunch of complaints from the host about their peace of junk camper. I broke a lot of stuff apparently. They charged me for breaking a super glued countertop. I don’t know about ya’ll. But a SUPER GLUED repair, is not a repair. Matter a fact it’s not damage. After dozens of back and forth emails, I finally get picture proof of the broken counter. The host legit, ripped it off the countertop and sent that as their claim. I sent them a picture of the camper ad with the super glued camper clear as day right there. These hosts clearly have this counter broken every single rental. Because we’re all stupid right?

A mud flap I damaged from getting into a pot hole, forced the mud flap onto the tire and it scraped down to nothing. These mud flaps are about $30-$40 to repair. The host charged me $487 for it. I finally get a receipt of it from outdoorsy and guess what? It’s a ford mud flap that is nowhere close to the one I damaged. In fact it’s the most fanciest ford mud flap you’ve ever seen. Here’s the kicker.. it’s a mud flap for a ford F150, F250 or F350. Easy scam. The host reams me up the hoo-ha by ordering $437 mud flap off Amazon, proving the cost with an Amazon receipt, and then return it for a full refund. If you subtract $40 from $437 that is $397. That’s an easy profit for a scam. 

Outdoorsy offered me $500 for this experience. Don’t they care that I’m offended by being scammed? Don’t they care how it makes them look when they allow a host to get away with this? Even if they ban the host, did they make me as a customer feel safe, happy and trusting? Absolutely not. I was mad at the host for scamming me. When the Outdoorsy support made me feel like I was cared for, I felt relieved. But now I have a different sentiment about the whole thing.. How can anyone just stand by and allow a customer to get scammed? They let me get scammed. Dissatisfied, disappointed and this isn’t the last place they’ll find me writing a review. 

Update: the responder to this review confirms outdoorsy closed my case without telling me so. They did not care to know if I was satisfied or not. They clearly won’t bother to entertain the evidence I put forward. At this point I’m a nuisance. They refunded me $500 but it doesn’t fix the fact I was ripped off.
reply:
Hi Mohamed,

Thanks for writing a review, and I'm very sorry to hear you had a less-than-perfect experience with our service due to a damage claim. Upon reviewing your account, it appears that you've been in contact with our Resolutions team regarding your dispute, and I verified that it was resolved after a thorough investigation by our Resolution Specialist. I want you to know that we take our customers' feedback seriously, so I'll be reaching out to you to address any additional questions you have regarding the dispute process. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking the time to provide us with your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Kylie Marie
title: I would give zero stars if I could
body:
I would give zero stars if I could. I booked an RV through Outdoorsy six months in advance for Burning Man, which was supposed to be my 30th birthday trip. Just days before departure, the host canceled on me. Outdoorsy’s response? A refund and nothing more.
That does absolutely nothing to fix the fact that, by then, every other affordable option was long gone. After months of planning and thousands of dollars invested in supplies, I had to scramble all day and am now taking a box truck instead of the RV I paid for. The entire experience I planned is ruined.
Outdoorsy loves to market itself as a “trusted” platform, but when hosts cancel, renters are left stranded with no backup options, no protection, and no accountability. If you’re planning a big trip or festival, I urge you to avoid Outdoorsy — they cannot be relied on. I’ll be making sure the Burning Man and festival community knows this too.
reply:
Hi Kylie,

My sincere apologies for the negative experience with our platform due to a canceled booking. This is most definitely not the experience we prefer our guests to have. Although we do everything possible to prevent cancellations, they sometimes happen, especially due to damage from a previous rental. We recognized that this was a pain point for our guests, so we have a team that's dedicated to assisting with last-minute cancellations for bookings that start within a week. Additionally, I'd like to reassure you that we strongly discourage hosts from canceling their guests' bookings. A host's rating can be negatively impacted when this occurs, as well as a financial penalty being imposed. We do our best to find replacement vehicles when cancellations occur within seven days of the departure date; however, it appears there was very limited inventory of available RVs in the area for Burning Man. Please rest assured that I've shared your feedback with our teams to make sure that your voice is heard. Thank you for your feedback!

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Becki Miller
title: OUTDOORSY NIGHTMARE! Stay away from Outdoorsy!!
body:
We're appalled by Outdoorsy’s gross negligence and won't utilize them in the future. Outdoorsy doesn't vet the owners and their vehicles before renting them out. Outdoorsy is grossly negligent, failing to exercise the degree of care a reasonably prudent company would exercise under the same circumstances and this negligence can result in harm, injury and even death. Outdoorsy breached their duty of care that could cause foreseeable damage. We could have been killed as the vehicle rented on Outdoorsy was a death trap. 

To accommodate 8, I rented a luxury sprinter. believing it would be safe and reliable. After driving three hours, we stopped to purchase gas and noticed the front bumper was falling off. It had been secured with duct tape. Then, we noticed steel coming out of the left front tire. Fortunately, we were close to a tire company who said we were minutes away from a blow out which would've caused a major accident. This delay caused us to be 3 hours late for our event.

The longer we drove, the less the brakes worked.  We had to push them farther and farther towards the floor to stop.  The bathroom vent was broken and when it rained, water poured in, ruining our clothes. 

Outdoorsy refused to compensate us in any way for their gross negligence in renting us the unsafe vehicle they advertised as safe for travel.  Our trip was an absolute nightmare!
reply:

I'm terribly sorry to hear you encountered issues with your rented RV. That's certainly not the experience that we want for you with Outdoorsy. Our guests' safety is our top priority, so please rest assured that we've passed your feedback along to our Marketplace Quality team so we can investigate the host's listing and take immediate action. I'd like to reassure you that all listings on our platform are verified and validated by our Trust and Safety team before they're published to ensure that all vehicles are in safe and roadworthy condition, so thank you for bringing this to our attention. author: Sam Morris
title: I would give fair warning to anyone…
body:
I would give fair warning to anyone looking to use this site, 

The company charges renters 25% commission to book on their site, but they withhold payments - their customer service gives you the run around. They also charge around 15% to the customer on top of the 25% commission they are already getting, so making upwards of  35% 

This has red flags all over it, at the time of writing this review we did not recieve payment for a rental that we operated for them perfectly, they kept citing they disconnected the bak account and just need to reconnect it. 

You then recieve notification that it's been successful and payment has been released. 

Then nothing and when you get in touch with customer service, the exact same run around, of we have the check on this with this department, we will get back to you....then nothing.  

I have screenshots of all the conversations and confirmations that everything is ok and then the run around when trying to actually get money out of them. 

To date we have not received payment for the rental - we would never have let our vehicle get booked through this site if we thought we wouldn't get paid for them. 

Do you due diligence before you use this site both as some booking and renting out your RV, there are loads of better options out there such as RVEZY.
reply:

author: Shane McConnell
title: Misleading listing leads to significantly higher rental cost
body:
Hidden and misleading over mileage charges significantly increased the cost of the hire (listed in miles and charged in kilometers resulting in 60% higher charge). Listing listed in US $ even though hire is in Canada so listing appears significantly lower cost until the currency conversion kicks in once the booking is confirmed. 
Navigating the website and the app was difficult if you want to look back at the details of a completed hire. Customer service was ineffective and did not deal with the issue well, instead referring us back to the fine print.

Hi Shane,

I'm sincerely sorry for the confusion with your host's mileage information. Transparency is very important to us, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. Upon reviewing your account, it appears that you initially accessed our US site (www.outdoorsy.com), so the listing price was quoted in USD instead of CAD. Please accept my sincere apologies for any confusion with the currency conversion. I'll personally get in touch with you soon to address your concerns in greater detail. I look forward to connecting with you. Thanks again for the feedback!

Outdoorsy Support

author: Ryan Jensen
title: Horrible experience
body:
This company goes to great lengths to hide crucial information for planning your trip. We booked a trip through this company. Every other site we looked at had a pretty standard .50 mileage overage fee. On the mobile version of the website they do not advertise the mileage overage fee and when we called about it, it was very hard to find even on the PC version of their website. Needless to say we planned a trip that involved 2000 miles over the allowed limit and “our mistake” was to assume the standard going rate for the mileage overage fee. After our credit card declined when we got charged the overage fee we found out that instead of the planned extra $1000 we were being charged $5000. Never would have planned a site seeing trip like this with a $2.50 a mile overage. Absolutely ridiculous.

Edit: Went through booking on my phone again just to be sure I wasn’t providing false information. I was not. There is no way to see the overage charge on a mobile device until after you book. Then if you don’t read the final booking carefully you won’t realize that it is 5 times the going rate until after you are already home and they charge you.

Update #2:
Been getting the run around with Outdoorsy customer service. They are the worst. They keep saying they are not to blame, but obviously they figured out that they were because they changed their website so they can retroactively claim they are not at fault. They want us to deal with the owner of the RV instead of them to negotiate with him when it is their fault. Don’t believe a word of their reply to this post because they are that dishonest.
Update #3
Have continued to get the run around from their customer service. I did not receive any emails from them as they claim. All I can say is they are definitely committed to deception and wish to always push the blame back on the customer. It is obvious they could care less about making the situation ethically right and are really only looking to blame the customer for not combing their site for information that was not there until after being called out on it. Avoid them at all costs.
Update #4
I am a reasonable individual and willing to admit when I make a mistake. My wife misunderstood me when I asked her about getting any response from Outdoorsy in her email and told me that she hadn’t. I guess the canned excuses didn’t make much of an impression on her. Needless to say the customer “support” team is a joke and has no authority to do anything except make up excuses and lame apologies that do not admit any fault. Now we are working with their dispute department which is where you get sent when the customer support gets tired of hearing your complaints over and over. Anyway, as expected the dispute department sent us screenshots of their updated website and asked for proof that they were at fault. Luckily, I had taken a screenshot with my phone before they updated it and sent it to them. It is the original screenshot dated and everything with no attempt to alter it in any way. Their response? Send us a video. Are you freaking kidding me? If I could give negative stars here I would.
reply:
Hi Ryan,

I'm so sorry to hear you had trouble finding the mileage information for your booking. Your time is valuable, so thanks for taking a moment to share your feedback. Transparency is very important to us, so I'd like to reassure you that all booking fees and add-ons are clearly displayed on the site and app. Please allow me to clarify that since we're a peer-to-peer marketplace and all of the RVs are independently owned, add-on fees such as mileage are set by individual hosts and can vary from listing to listing. Please rest assured that we'll review your booking details, and I'll personally be reaching out soon to address your concerns and answer any questions you may have. Once again, thanks for your feedback!

Update:  Hi again! I reached out via email, but didn't receive a response.  Please feel free to respond directly to the emails you've received if you have any additional questions. Thanks again! 

Updated Response: Hi again, Ryan! I can confirm that I've reached out twice to the email associated with your account, and it appears they're being received and read; however, I'd like to share our team experience address as well, so please reach out to me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com, ATTN: Dale in the subject line, and I'll be happy to reshare the information previously provided. Thanks again! 

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Stela
title: Host Never Responded
body:
Host Never Responded — Outdoorsy Had to Cancel My Trip

I booked a rental on Outdoorsy and was excited about the trip July 3 - July 6 2025. But the host never answered my calls or messages, even as the pickup date got closer. Eventually, Outdoorsy canceled the booking themselves because they also couldn’t reach the host.

This left me completely stranded with no time to make other arrangements. It was frustrating and disappointing, especially since I relied on this reservation for travel plans.

Even worse, the platform doesn’t let guests leave reviews if the trip gets canceled, so I can’t warn others about this host’s lack of communication.

I hope Outdoorsy takes this seriously and improves how they handle unresponsive hosts — they should be flagged or removed. People should be able to trust that their bookings are reliable.
In case i cancel they charging my deposit $500 , and the host is protected , nut what about me?????
Outdoorsy is not protecting the other side !!!!
Don’t use them!!!!!





Hi Jaime,

Thank you for your response and for notifying your Marketplace Quality team. However, I need to be direct — this situation caused significant inconvenience and unexpected out-of-pocket expenses for a last-minute booking.

I understand that your team reached out, but given the short timeframe and lack of viable alternatives, I had to secure different accommodations on my own — at a higher cost.

I believe it is fair to request reimbursement or compensation for the additional expenses I incurred as a result of the host’s failure to respond and the cancellation beyond my control. I

author: Darlene
title: If you're a host considering Outdoorsy
body:
If you're a host considering Outdoorsy, proceed with caution. I had an account previously and decided to return—big mistake. I was prepared to resubmit everything from scratch, which would’ve been fine. But because my original email was no longer valid, I couldn’t reset my password or create a new setup with that email. That took a full day to resolve.

Then came the real headache. After spending two hours setting up my listing again, I hit a wall with the W-9 form—it wouldn’t submit. That issue still isn’t fixed after 11 days, despite two emails claiming it was. I’ve spent hours emailing, calling, uploading print screens, and getting nowhere.

Customer service is polite but powerless. You can’t speak to anyone who can actually fix the problem. Everything is “escalated” to the technical or tax team, but no one follows up. Apparently, the tax team doesn’t work weekends… and I'm beginning to wonder if they work during the week either. Customer service reps even admit they’re frustrated by the escalation delays.

Outdoorsy's site claims a 24–48 hour response window, but that seems more like wishful thinking. I haven’t heard a thing in four days.

It’s now June in Maine—peak RV rental season—and I’m losing valuable time and income because of their broken system. I've switched to RVShare out of desperation. I’m not sure if it’s much better, but I can't waste another minute dealing with Outdoorsy's dysfunction.

For a platform that takes a 25% cut, they ought to provide actual support. Sadly, I’ve since found I’m not the only one with these issues—if you look up reviews, you’ll see plenty of similar stories. Outdoorsy really needs to get its act together.
reply:
Hi Darlene,

I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our service due to the delay in resolving the issue with your tax information on your Outdoorsy account. I know how important it is for you to get this resolved immediately, and we definitely don't want you missing out on potential bookings for the peak RV rental season, so I'd like to reassure you that we've followed up with our Tax team and made your case a priority. I'll personally get in touch with you soon regarding this. Thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. 

Dale R.

24/7 Unlimited Roadside Assistance

We'll be there if you need a tow or a jump start, lock your keys in your car, or get a flat tire or breakdown. We provide 24-hour emergency roadside assistance from a network of service providers, covering:

    Towing
    Jump starts
    Mobile tire assistance

author: Sara Jeekao
title: Disappointing Experience with Outdoorsy…
body:
Disappointing Experience with Outdoorsy and PayPal Payment Option.

I rented an RV van through Outdoorsy for a holiday trip and decided to use the 0% interest payment option through PayPal, allowing me to pay off the rental in four installments. To set it up, I linked my credit card to my PayPal account, expecting a smooth transaction. However, I was unexpectedly charged interest on my credit card.

When I contacted Outdoorsy for assistance, I received no support. They simply directed me to PayPal, stating they received the payment from PayPal and that the issue was not theirs to resolve. They also mentioned that other customers had experienced the same issue with this payment offer. If that's the case, why is Outdoorsy still offering a payment option with these known issues?

While I understand that PayPal may be at fault, Outdoorsy is the one offering this payment option on their platform. As such, they should have taken more responsibility in resolving the problem. Instead, they passed me off to PayPal, leaving me frustrated and without any resolution.

Additionally, Outdoorsy should have made it clear on their platform that customers choosing this payment option must first be approved by PayPal. When I selected the payment plan, the process appeared seamless. But when I called PayPal, they asked if I had received an approval email. I had received an email authorizing the payment to Outdoorsy, Inc., but it turns out this was not the approval email they meant.

It’s incredibly disappointing that Outdoorsy, a company that provides services to customers, showed no interest in addressing my concerns once they had received their payment. The lack of customer service and accountability is unacceptable. I will not be using Outdoorsy again and strongly advise others to be cautious when selecting payment options on their platform.
reply:
Hi Sara!

Thanks so much for sharing your feedback with us. Unfortunately, we're unable to locate an Outdoorsy account with the name and details you've provided within your review. Trustpilot reached out to you for additional details as well so that we may do our best to investigate the unusual issues you described, but unfortunately, they didn't receive a response to their request. We'd love the opportunity to investigate this further as we're unable to locate any contact with our support team from you, so please reach out at experience@outdoorsy.com, Attn: Donna.

Donna

title: Better hope your van doesn't get…
body:
Better hope your van doesn't get damaged 

I am a host on Outdoorsy, and am supper disappointed with their insurance team. 

I rented my van out to someone who put a dent in the back door. The van has been in the shop for 2 months, and I am still trying to sort things out with the insurance. 

When a renter rents your van, they have to purchase insurance on the vehicle. That way if something happens, you as a host are 100% covered. IDEALLY.

The initial amount the insurance paid out to me was way too low to cover the cost of repairs. So I kept asking the shop to send supplements for the repair. 2 months of back and forth and slow communication later, I am almost out of the woods, only to find out that the insurance might not cover the whole job!! So, I am potentially looking at paying out of pocket to fix my van that a renter damaged! 

What's wild is they say that it would have been cheaper to replace the door (which I am not sure is true, still looking into it), but the van is converted. If I just slap on a new OEM door, what about all the work that I did - insulation, panelling, finished cedar. For a company that rents out camper vans, you would think this wouldn't even have been a consideration. 

All this to say, if you are a van owner, I would not recommend that you rent your van out through this company based on my experience. I certainly will not be after this. 

I just want my van back--fixed and the way it was before I ever got into this disaster.
reply:
Hi Sheldon,

I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our platform due to a damage claim. That's definitely not the experience we prefer our hosts to have. It is our goal to provide a smooth and easy claims experience, so thank you for taking the time to provide us with your feedback. Although most claims are processed within 7-14 days, there are some cases that do take longer. I'll investigate this further on my end and reach out to you soon to address your concerns. Once again, thanks for your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Anthony Ordonez

author: Jared Mellinger
title: Great experience!
body: Great experience!  I love the on app key exchange but it’s not real clear how to complete the forms during the process.  The forms were there to confirm but not editable.  Other than that all was good.  Chat feature is fantastic when I have questions!

author: David Paget
title: Thank you Ryan
body: Thank you Ryan, Happy New Year.

author: Andrew Moreland
title: Great way to reduce risk.
body: Are you thinking of listing your camper on Outdoorsy? I would recommend the platform. Outdoorsy offers many great things that alleviate risk, from insurance to renters, to advertising your camper, and even a customer service department. The one thing is they take a large cut of the profits, but you have to remind yourself what you are getting from them. I think it is a good platform to rent out your camper and would recommend it.

author: Tri
title: Avoid, avoid, avoid Outdoorsy
body:
I had a HORRIBLE experience with Outdoorsy.  I am writing this 6 months after the experience because every time I thought of them my blood boiled too much to sit and write this. It was such an incredibly frustrating experience. DON'T RENT WITH THIS COMPANY! Choosing them and the specific host was my biggest regret of 2024.  As others have written, their contract denies them of essentially any responsibility and accountability to the renter (and sounds like to the host too), so they just wasted days of our time with vague and unhelpful emails. With their call center clearly in the Philippines, they had polite staff responding to all calls and emails, BUT THEY WERE NOT KNOWLEDGEABLE. Their incompetence wasted a lot of time and added so much stress. They even gave wrong information, providing claims forms to fill out that were nothing to do with what they were asking us to do (albeit giving a deadline to submit them), and wrote an infuriating number of 'apology emails' such as you see following their bad reviews on Trustpilot.  A lot of communications, doing nothing, even when you reach more senior staff. There was also no reasonable protection from their side about the host who was making unreasonable claims to us for $8,900! Owners are required to do a technical checkup of their vehicle every 90 days, and since the damage of the camper on the first day of our rental was mechanical, and then the mechanic who helped us after 2 tires blew out (separate incident) told us the tires should have been changed long before our rental period started, I asked if Outdoorsy had even checked the technical report to ensure that the host had even provided us with a decent vehicle; they admitted they had not and would not check the report. What is the point of requiring this if it's not even part of their process during conflicts between host and renter, at a minimum? I have a lot more to say, but I'm done with Outdoorsy forever. 
[ @ Outdoorsy, as I have said several times this year, please don't send me any more empty apology messages.]

Since then, I've rented from Escape Campervans and the experience was night-and-day; you rent directly from them so you don't risk drama from an individual owner trying to take advantage of you, nor the disclaimers from an intermediary who just takes your money and barely lifts a finger to assist their clients. Their options of vehicles are not as varied obviously, but choosing Outdoorsy and their host just to have a camper with a toilet cost us ENORMOUSLY.
reply:
Hi there,

Thanks for posting a review, and I'm very sorry you had a less-than-perfect experience with our service due to a damage claim. Outdoorsy is an international company that offers RV rentals globally, and we're a proudly diverse organization that holds all our team members to the same standard of excellent service, professionalism, and support. We always aim to deliver a great customer experience, and we regret that we missed the mark. I'd love the opportunity to investigate your feedback further; however, I'm unable to locate your account or booking with the information available here. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" as the subject. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Once again, thanks for your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: m e kemp
title: Be careful when using outdoorsy
body: Be careful when using outdoorsy. They take no responsibility for the company’s they introduce. We booked an rv through outdoorsy with ‘nevadarvrental’ and what a joke they were. They owe us for a ruined holiday so we approached outdoorsy who basically said they were not liable as they are simply a booking platform. So be careful when booking through them as there is no come back and definitely avoid nevadarvrental ! Read the Google reviews
reply:
Hi there,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear your experience with your host didn't match your expectations. That's certainly not the experience we'd prefer our guests to have. We'll be performing a full investigation into what happened here, and I'll get in touch with you soon to gather more information about your experience. We strive incredibly hard to make every guest experience enjoyable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Daniel Cifor
title: I would rate this a zero If was…
body:
I would rate this a zero If was possible. As an owner I had nothing but bad experience with outdoorsy den of thiefs.
DO NOT RENT ON THIS SITE
They will treat you like shit when your rv gets damaged.they do not stand behind the very people that make this business possible.
I have 3 units for rent and I have blocked all of 2025 until they reverse course and start to care for the very people they profit from.
claims department is in the Philippines and for sure they get commission based on denials because it’s impossible to reason with anyone my dog has more sens then their adjusters.
Then you have the customer service people which all they do is waste your precious time by basically pretending to help you knowing they will do nothing and then come back to insult you to settle for $200 of  repairs worth thousands.
For months i get pushed around and when i call there is nobody to talk to and when you complain  some first name guy reaches out with some very professional written excuse and once you reply the first dung head is back harassing you like nothing happened.
Warning:
When you deliver a unit to a guest , the unit is not insured on its way back.
Huge liability if something happens on the way back not to mention they take 25% from your delivery fee as well.
They lure you with income but all they deliver is damage and headache.
Block 2025
reply:

I'm very sorry to hear that your RV sustained some damage during a rental and that you had a poor experience with our claims process. author: Adam Silva
title: Hosting on Outdoorsy is great
body: Outdoorsy is a fantastic platform for both RV owners and renters! As an owner, I love the easy listing process, secure bookings, excellent support, and comprehensive insurance. Renters will appreciate the wide selection of RVs, detailed listings, easy communication, and secure booking process. Whether you're looking to share your RV with others or embark on a memorable adventure, Outdoorsy provides a user-friendly and reliable experience that fosters a true sense of community among RV enthusiasts.

author: Gerianne Street
title: Outdoorsy is the Best
body: Outdoorsy is the best! Neverending customer support and a user friendly website make listing my Winnebago View painless. Outdoorsy is a leader in RV rental sites.

author: Master Disaster
title: Outdoorsy is shady and unscrupulous.
body:
Our RV got wrecked and had $22,000 in damages, the claims process took well over 9 months to get settled and they low balled the estimate  they do not cross check their renting customers insurance like stated they broke their very own policies by allowing someone without RV insurance coverage to rent my RV. The renter's failed to pay anything and moved from their address  to avoid prosecution . Outdoorsy did every snake move in the book and we had to cancel $16,000 worth of reservation bookings due to their lack of timely fix..    don't expect to get your RV fixed  in timely or professional matter if it's damaged.   The claims dept. adjuster low balled the estimates denied the claims . They get a chance to reply to this . I Dont care I got the lawsuit court case documents and text messages with their claims agent texts . And pictures that show how shady customer service really is. We still use this questionable website...
.
