author: NL
title: OUTDOORSY are TERRIBLE
body: I rented an RV through “Outdoorsy” in BC Canada from the 9/2 to 9/19.  The Owner’s  name is Natalie and the Host’s is Jason Bashnick.  The entire experience was terrible.   The RV was ok but in a fairly poor state given it’s only 5 years old.  There were screws popping out of the bodywork here and there, a chipped windscreen, wardrobe door hanging off and every draw was full of the owners clutter leaving nowhere really to put our stuff.  Nonetheless we took the RV and had a great first part of our holiday. Then the nightmare began.   The breaks on the RV failed on Sunday 9/15 at 1pm.   We managed to pull into a gas station and called Outsoorsy for help…..eventually they told us help was on the way….then they called back to say it wasn’t but if we wanted to pay for a mechanic to come out we could? hello???.   They suggest calling the host who basically said not his problem call Outsoorsy……to be fair to Natalie she texted a few times to tell me to try this and try that but nothing corrected the fault.   Outdoorsy continued to be pretty useless by constantly telling us we had help on the way.  Eventually a breakdown truck arrived 27 hours later after we spend the night at the gas station.   The RV was towed back to Vancouver and we had to check into a hotel for the remainder of our trip.   Since then we have been trying,  unsuccessfully, to claim for the loss of the last four days of our vacation and our unintended hotel expenses.   Outdoorsy have been frankly utterly useless.  I would never recommend anyone use this company to book anything.   The owner basically blamed us for destroying the brakes in her vehicle…..I drive heavy goods vehicles regularly so I know that this is not the case.   Even if that were the case they should have insurance to cover that and the renters loss…….but seemingly they don’t want to even discuss a settlement……and as I said OUTDOORSY are useless DO NOT USE THEM. I am not going to have to use lawyers to fight this as I am not going to be rolled over as a matter of Principle.
reply:
Hi there,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear you had a negative experience with your rental. That's certainly not the experience we prefer our guests to have. We'll be performing a full investigation into what happened here, and I'll get in touch with you soon to address your concerns. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Angela Jackson
title: New to hosting on Outdoorsy
body: I am new to hosting on Outdoorsy. Each time I call with a question, I am treated with respect and dignity. No question is too dumb and every representative I’ve dealt with is able to help me.

author: James Kisiel
title: Excellent application
body: I am extremely satisfied with this application. Customer service is top notch. My only suggestion would be to allow host to see reviews on potential renters. Host have the most on the line since campers cost several thousand dollars. It would be helpful to know if the renter has a past of not treating the RVs with respect.

author: Jerry Zaabel
title: Irresponsible Company
body: I have a campground in Northern AZ. at my horse ranch.  A person rented an RV from Outdoorsy and had it delivered to my property for their visit while participating in a horse clinic.  The renter was flying in from out of state.  The RV owner called me several times while driving to my ranch asking for directions.  She was having a hard time with directions and by the tone of her voice was getting more and more angry.  When they finally arrived at the ranch she pushed the entrance button to open the gate. When the gate opened half way.  Her husband who was driving the pick up truck pulling the RV attempted to drive through the partly open gate and struck the gate causing damage to both his vehicle and the gate opener.  The woman (RV Owner) was extremely upset and jumped out of the truck and pushed the gate causing the already damaged opener to total destruction.  The cost to repair was twenty five hundred dollars.  Outdoorsey's insurance refused to pay for the damage saying that type of damage didn't fit their policy.  They went on to say their policy covers Malicious Mischief or vandalism and/or direct collision.  Both of which were the case here.  After several weeks of "investigation"the insurance sent me a denial letter.  I would not do business with this company.  They are irresponsible and I wonder what they would have done if someone was injured.
reply:
Hi Jerry,

Thank you for posting a review, and I'm very sorry to hear that one of our customers caused some damage to your property. Upon review, it appears that you've notified our Claims team regarding the damage, so if you have any questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to reach back out to them directly within the email thread that you've been communicating.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Rylan Anderson
title: Worst experience I’ve ever had in my life
body: Renting a vehicle from outdoorsy was hands down the worst experience ive ever had dealing with a company, ANY COMPANY. Not only was the cost astronomical, they downsized us from a van to an SUV 2 weeks before our trip was supposed tk start, meaning we couldn't fit everyone we had originally planned. The truck broke down on our 2nd day, no refunds were ever provided for the money we had to spend to fix someone else’s vehicle. They booked us for the wrong dates and then used that as an excuse to keep our damage deposit. The owner used foul and harsh language towards me, calling me all kinds of names and awful things because I asked to get reimbursed for the money we had to spend to fix his vehicle that he rented us, not to mention losing out on multiple days of our trip and not being able to do what we wanted to do on the trip in the first place. And then after all that the owner is trying to charge me 1500$ in damages that were already there on the truck when we got it. This entire website is a scam for con artists trying to bleed money out of people and get repairs done on their vehicles for free. No matter what, DO NOT USE THIS SITE! You will regret it. Everyone I know who’s used this site has had the same experience or worse. Rent from literally anyone else and it will be better and cheaper than this.  I tried to apply for a partial refund, got denied. I had to literally go change my banking information to stop them from taking money of out my account without my permission. Sketchiest most awful company ever. Id rather poop in my hands and clap then give these guys another dollar.
reply:
Hi Rylan,

Thanks for your review, and I'm very sorry to hear you had such a negative experience. That's certainly not the outcome we prefer our guests to have. I'll investigate this thoroughly and reach out to gather more information in regard to the details you provided. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Neil Charlton
title: A great partnership!
body: Outdoorsy has allowed me to rent my multiple trailers to customers in a seamless fashion, while helping me to maintain communication and customer service throughout the process!

author: Cat Smith
title: Westfalia campervans
body:
I've enjoyed using this platform to find renters for my campervans. The experience has been very positive, I've had the pleasure of hosting renters from Europe, United States and Canada. 
The payments arrive on time. I feel confident that my Vans are fully insured. 
I do wish the platform was a bit more user friendly. I find it difficult at times to quickly use the platform to find information, such as my reviews and downloads for forms I need like the contract. Don't often know which Outdoorsy site I'm suppose to be using. 
Overall a very good experience so far.

author: Curtis  Scott
title: Outdoorsy makes it simple to rent out…
body: Outdoorsy makes it simple to rent out our travel trailer. Take some pic's, Complete and post the listing and the messages start coming.

author: Bréhier Chloé
title: My trip was a full success
body:
Thanks to Harry my trip was a success ! 
He took away my stress and got me everthing I needed : good tips, good camping Gear, good tire, good communication. First solo trip was lush! Made me want to do it again !

author: Cam Bogan
title: For Outdoorsy C-Suite
body: First of all, full transparency, I have never booked through Outdoorsy. What I have done is researched the internet universe to find an affordable place to live for a bit less than a year, because I am temporarily homeless. I specifically included the term "long-term" in the Google Search along with "RV rentals" and Outdoorsy came up close to the top and stated there on the Results page, "So unless you're looking for a 6 month rental..." , so I decided to see what was available on your site. I used your filter to choose my price range (by the way, you really need to add a Sort option to list RV's from least to most/most to least expensive option, because without it, you're wasting your customer's most important and non-renewable resource, their time) and realized that there was no option for long-term rentals with a reasonable monthly charge option. And thanks to the lack of Sort option for price-grouping, I had to look through 50+ options that were in my area. Since listings are ALL by the night and most for over $100 a night, that would make my legitimately long-term need of a 7 month rental (specifically 11/1-05/31), at $100 a night, $22K. The people that have the kind of money that make this amount seem reasonable, would likely just buy an RV, that way they can use it then put it on here for their entertainment subscription fund. I'm not asking you to stop being a predatory American company, I'm just asking you to be more honest and direct with your offerings. And, if you're already missing me before I sign off, don't despair, I will be doing another one of these reviews  for your unbelievably manipulative process by which you require customers to run around in figurative circles to close their Outdoorsy account. Toodles!
reply:
Hi Cam,
 
I'm so sorry to hear that none of the listings on our marketplace met your needs for long-term accommodations. Your feedback is very important to us, so I want to thank you for taking the time to share it with us and bring this to our attention. Since we're a peer-to-peer marketplace and all of the RVs are listed by independent owners, all fees are stipulated by the hosts. We recognize there's a need for long-term rentals, and we're currently researching ways for hosts and guests to negotiate these rentals in a way that will benefit both parties. I'll be reaching out to you for some additional details to share with our product team. Thanks again, and I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Chris Kutensky
title: I have been a long time host and renter, with Outdoorsy and others
body:
I have been a long time renter and host, for 8yrs with Outdoorsy, as I recall. I'm my experience, they have a fantastic website and process. At days end, they are middle men. I felt that they handled CV19 when business was ten fold, smoothly when they were likely under staffed and many inexperienced renters, we're on the road renting RVs because airplanes were shutdown. I host in CO, a tourist state and if I had 20 Airstreams, I could have rented them all, easily. I can only imagine how hard Outdoorsy and other RV vendors worked to try to keep millions of units and millions of personalities Happy! It's a law of averages, and you'll always have some negatives, but as you can see it's 5% and when I was a kid, 95% was an A+.  Having rented and hosted for years I've learned any negative review is just like anything, it's based on personality and expectations, unless there is a blatant neglect of communication. Otherwise, personality plays a role, and I'm just as guilty of having too high expectations and no different than a dating website, every profile has a certain effort to market, whether price point, or allowing Pets when other do not.
So, I say, always look at the big picture and the % of positive stars as some people post negatively based on emotion, or one-sided points of views, for example, host or renter. I recommend you consider the RVs age first, as age has wear/tear, and match that age to the competitive cost. Photos are great on sewing the design and general unit, but it doesn't show parts and like an aged car, truck, computer, what have you, age plays a role. Personally, I am thankful for Outdoors, introducing me to some fantastic, renters and I truly enjoy RVs and love being a Host, seeing renters come home after their journey and family vacation, all relaxed and happy. You can see the stress that has left their shoulders from PickUp to DropOff. BUT that said, there will always be certain personalities that you just can't live up to their expectations, and as a Host, you just tell them, text me, call me, let me know how I can help. We will handle any issues that comes up, if any, work with RV repair vendors, pay them, and upon drop off, if determined it wasn't Wear/Tear, we have deposits to reimburse us, but we just want you to enjoy, and so does Outdoors. It's a vacation, time to relax and enjoy and avoid making Mountains out of MoleHills as they say, as it's all comes out in the wash.  And over many years, I have issues as both renter and host, always minor but even issues with Brand New trucks or Rvs, it can happen, so keep comms open, that's what I recommend. No one wants a bad vacation for anyone, and that certainly not why Outdoorsy and Hosts option this industry. I saw this as I've had 1 or 2 personalities who tend, finding to complain whether RV, other industries / business they get involved in, they just tend to demand and it's just characteristic, no matter who the vendor may be. In today's world, it's hard to connect with this persona as they communicate one-way, and talk at you, instead of with you. I've seen very very few, but it's exaggeration and characteristic of the submitter, but to submit 1 star, even start to a middleman vendor, seems more emotional, than offering readers the positives. Why blame Outdoorsy unless the entire vacation was unruly managed start to finish, canceled or destroyed, at their direct blame. I just think emotions are not good reviews. With a great of Tv experience, I know some folks get upset about small things where others accept that "life happens". Wear and tear exists when you rent units over 5yrs, it's part of Rv life, there are tons of forums you should look into, per model you wish to rent, as that can help too.  Life is short, enjoy your vacation and just know that the older the "rental unit" it's more likely to have some small glitch occur, and don't try to fix or work on a Hosts Rv, without working with them, as I've seen people break things trying to Google How To, so let your Host handle it. And if an RV repairman need to get involved, hand it off to Host, they need to know who's working on their unit, and they know their Rv best, or either way, let them pay direct, hand it off to them, so you can go back to enjoying your time with family, friends or your journey.
Two way communication is the answer. As long as there is two-way communication, there is no issue that can't be resolved. And no need to blame Outdoorsy or other vendors in general, your our Guest(s). Just work together and you will be in the 95%, I say, from my years in Rv works. Hope this helps!

author: Modoka
title: I was a Host on Outdoorsy
body:
I was a Host on Outdoorsy.  

The hosting process just to get started was aggravating to begin with.  It's like no-one was serious about helping me get started.  You had to send them the same documents multiple times and they just kept passing you to the next customer service representative. The customer service was crap... After 2 or 3 weeks of trying to get set up, I was finally able to.  Skip to my first and only renter..  After their trip, they returned the camper late, and I understand things happen.  However, the Toilet/Tank was full and clogged first off.  I had to pay for the state park to let me empty it.  Next I noticed on the outside of the travel trailer there was duct tape across the arms and the main beam of the awning, Stretching the entire length.   Last, one of my slide outs were stuck in and and wouldn't go back out.  Mind you I emailed outdoorsy the photos plus receipts and I had to hire a mobile RV Mechanic to make the slide out work again.  They did a review and said they can't use the customers deposit for the damages.  (EXCUSE ME!) I understood that's what a deposit is for.  So that if something breaks that the deposit will help pay for the damages.. HUH, I guess not.  Either way Ive emailed back and forth with the piece of crap company and gotten nowhere.  They refuse to take the money out of the customers deposit and not pay me back for the mobile mechanic or the waste fee.  To me it's just the point of it all.  I do not recommend anyone using outdoorsy for your RV rental hosting needs.  BEWARE!!!
reply:
Hi Moises,
 
I'm sorry to hear you had trouble getting your account set up and your RV was damaged during its first booking. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. I understand how frustrating it is to wait for a claim to be finalized. Our claims team strives to finalize all claims within 7-10 business days; however, some take longer to resolve. For the most recent update and most accurate information on your claim, please feel free to reply to your claim adjuster within the email thread that you've been communicating. I'll be reaching out to you personally to offer some insights, as well. Again, my apologies for the negative experience. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Consumer
title: Worst experience and will never use…
body: Worst experience and will never use them again or recommend. The van and owner were great but this company is a nightmare. We had a minor chip in the windshield and just worked directly with the owner to have it fixed and paid them for the repair. Outdoorsy created a claim against me and is holding my deposit even through both the owner and I sent emails saying we don’t want a claim. It’s been months and they won’t release my deposit and I can’t get anyone to resolve the issue just a runaround.
reply:

There was no replacement rental and we had to rent a vehicle that would not fit all our camp gear (coolers, stove, and more)so we were forced to abandon it also lost $400 worth of  groceries that was in the refrigerator. 
Being stranded over night and more than 16 hours of trying to resolve the matter we were just defeated 😞 after we got home we called outdoorsy and they offered us $100 for our next trip with them. Imagine being stranded 900 miles from home in an RV you spent $3000 to rent and when it breaks down they come pick it up and leave you, your family and your gear in some random neighborhood on the side walk and they assure you that you can figure it out!!!

author: kerriecameron
title: At a loss for our holidays because customer service did nothing to help us
body:
After booking an RV we tried contacting the host to set up arrangements.  The host wasn't responding. I emailed host once 3 weeks and a second time one week before reaching out to customer support for help.  

Customer supports response was that they give hosts 24-48 hours to respond because "we understand life can throw your routine off".  I replied with our timeline of trying to contact the host and asked that they help us reach the host. I haven't heard anything back from customer support after this. 

I emailed multiple times and I called multiple times. No one would answer the call, and once I was on hold for 30 minutes (no exaggeration) to only be disconnected after a recording told me no one was available to take my call. 

I reached out on social media with my situation and got a response to call or email customer support for assistance... really?!  That response just added more to my frustration.

I had to cancel my reservation one week before our holidays because of the unresponsive host and lack of support from the folks at outdoorsy.  Thankfully the amount was deposited back into my account.  

Now aside from this frustrating experience with outdoorsy and their host, I am $80 out of pocket for the "service fee" that wasn't refunded... Really?!  What service did I pay for??  A whole lot of wasted time on my end for trying to get service, I should charge outdoorsy for my time, not the other way around. 

But the worst part of all this is my family and I have been looking forward to this, our annual holiday that we work very hard to afford and now we don't know what we're going to do.  We have a campground  booked with nothing to bring with us. So to quote your customer service I guess "life can throw your routine off" but replace "life" with "outdoorsy".

From this experience, my take away is outdoorsy doesn't care. At no point did anyone on your end try to resolve this matter.  You have profited off my service fee and my family is now at a loss on what we will do.  An appropriate action on your part would have been to 1) Respond to our concerns first and foremost 2) Try to resolve our concerns by helping us contact the host 3) If the host remained unresponsive, find another solution if possible.  Would there have been another rental you could have helped us manage? We even would have paid more for a bigger camper if necessary.  But unfortunately none of that happened.

No one wins from this experience! Outdoorsy, sure you made money from my service fee, but you had to refund $800 and our holidays are one week away and we don't know what we're going to do.

Outdoorsy if you do care about the people you are providing services to and making a profit off of, you still have time to make this right for me and my family. I have little hope, but at this time I have little options.
reply:
Hi Kerrie, 
 
I'm so sorry your host was unresponsive and you ended up needing to cancel your reservation. This is most definitely not the intended outcome we'd prefer our guests to have, especially so close to your trip starting. Due to the nature of the cancellation, we've refunded you in full, and our replacement team has worked to locate an alternate RV for your trip. Please feel free to respond directly to their email to let them know how you'd like to proceed. In the meantime, I'll follow up with you personally to ensure we've done everything we can to turn your experience around. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback.
 
Jaime

author: Zach Klimko
title: Giant red flags
body:
Giant red flags. We rented a trailer that had a completely dead battery, and we were given no idea by the owner. 

We provided Outdoorsy time stamped photos of the battery going dead within 45 minutes, even after hours of running a generator to charge it. We lost all our food overnight, we had no heat. We had an incessant beep that kept our dog and family up, and the only way to stop it was by running the generator at all times, which clearly isn't an option in the middle of the night.

After weeks of internal review outdoorsy sent us a photo of their terms of service with yellow highlighting on the line saying "failing to use the vehicle in compliance with all instructions and warnings provided by us and hosts." Not sure how we could have better used a dead battery that was not disclosed. We did buy additional propane as the owner did disclose that he didn't even check if they were filled. And we weren't given a key. 

Hideous experience and clearly Outdoorsy is going to back their owners over the customers. Buyer beware.

author: May L.
title: RENTER BEWARE
body:
Since Outdoorsy refuses to post my negative review on their site I will post it here. 

Backstory - 

Outdoorsy provides a 14 day window to provide a review. I was about to post my review on their site at 11pm on the 14th day and it said I was no longer able to post as they operate in central time and it was already 1am the next day so I missed their window by 1 hour. 

I was then asked if the review was positive as they could manually post a positive review for me but not a negative one. 

Can someone please explain how this is a fair policy that they can manually post a positive review but not a negative one? It’s screams scam all over and fake positive reviews. 

Here is my review of my RV rental experience: 

RENTER BEWARE
As first time RV renters, we were extremely disappointed with our stay and the treatment of our group. We had to depart a day earlier than expected and had called the host to let them know. I asked what we needed to do before leaving and he said just make sure to turn everything off and close the door. They never mentioned anything about cleaning the RV before we left, nor was it mentioned to us when we checked-in. Later that evening, we received a message saying we had left the RV in a ‘state’ and that there were stains on the cushions and blinds and they were charging us $775 for excess cleaning and to replace the items that were supposedly stained. 

We were honestly shocked by this message and offered our apologies and even to come back and clean the RV due to the misunderstanding of what the prep fee covers as we were under the impression it covered the cleaning (it literally states on Outdoorsy that the fee is to cover professional cleaning and required maintenance checks). 

We were told that they were unavailable and had already cleaned half the RV. We explained that we were under the impression the prep fee covered the cleaning but then the host began to scold me telling me they had left cleaning supplies and that I was making excuses as to why it wasn’t cleaned. 

They did not care at all that it was a misunderstanding and that if we had known we were supposed to clean it, we would have done so.

We feel taken advantage of as first time RV renters as there is no way it would cost them $200 of time to clean the RV, $431.15 to replace the dinette cushion with a tiny yogurt drink stain or $144.45 to replace the blinds that we had supposedly stained. 

Hopefully this review can help the next person renting so they do not get scammed like we did. Make sure you read the paperwork before you sign it and do not be too trusting of hosts. Also make sure to take photos and document before and after as we forgot to take photos before we left and therefore had no evidence of our own to dispute their claim.
reply:
Hi May, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a poor experience with our service due to a dispute with your host about an excess cleaning fee and not being able to submit your trip review within the allotted 14-day period. This is most definitely not the intended outcome we'd prefer our guests to have. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback regarding your experience. I've looked into this further on my end and reached out to you personally to discuss it in greater detail. I look forward to connecting with you.

body: Better roadside assistance needed.  I feel they only care about bookings, a typical SaaS.  And how they get away with taking 40% is beyond me. They charge the renter and host both around 20% per booking.  Way to high imho.  Should be more like 15% like airbnb and VRBO.  That's a finder's fee, not 40%.  Competitors are the same.  It must be the insurance companies.
reply:
Hi Gord, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our fee policy and roadside assistance. We've previously discussed your experience with our roadside assistance partner, and I submitted a complaint to them on your behalf. Concerning our service fees, please let me clarify the structure: there's a 20-25% service fee assessed on host payouts, and guests are charged a service fee of 8-10% on bookings made through Outdoorsy. Guests also pay for their insurance packages, which can vary depending upon the level of coverage they choose and the Actual Cash Value of the RV and any additional insurance products they choose, such as Trip Protection or Interior Damage Protection. I know it's important to know what the service fees are used for, so I'll be reaching out to you personally to provide some additional insight about our fee structure. author: Ryan VanSickle
title: I’ve hosted my RV for 2-years on the…
body:
I’ve hosted my RV for 2-years on the Outdoorsy Platform and finally removed myself completely this week. The service/support, App Issues and overall inadequate screening processes are not worth the effort.

To use one example - Outdoorsy “verifies” drivers and renters through a “proprietary process” which it states gives you the piece of mind that a driver is legally able to drive a motor vehicle. One renter ended up causing damage while driving my RV and at the end of the trip the Outdoorsy Team went to file a claim and found he hadn’t had auto insurance in more than 2-years and his driver’s license had been revoked by the state. The claim/repair took several more weeks to be approved and has still yet to be resolved as they worked through how to appropriately handle the situation. I wouldn’t trust someone who shows up to take your vehicle from the App; there are great, honest renters out there but Outdoorsy’s policies don’t allow you to differentiate.

*9/11/24 Update: I’ve been contacted internally by the Outdoorsy Team but can share that there still hasn’t been any progress on my Claim Status. The uninsured driver who damaged my vehicle submitted fake Insurance Information and Outdoorsy is “waiting to hear from them” before they proceed. I’ve left a handful of messages with Progressive to no avail. Be ready for a multi-month repair if you have any damage done to your RV.
reply:
Hi Ryan,

We're sincerely sorry to hear that your RV sustained damage during a rental and that your experience with our service didn't quite match your expectations. We'll be performing a full investigation into your claim, and I'll personally be reaching out to you soon. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Tracy Federwitz
title: This business is fraudulent
body: This business is fraudulent - stay away!  I have been repeatedly charged on multiple occasions by this company without any warning or explanation.  It started well - I rented a camper and they held a $500 security deposit on top of the rental costs.  We returned the camper without any issues.  They released the security deposit.  Then, a month later, they reauthorized the $500 charge without any explanation or warning, even though my rental had long been over.  Then a month after that, Outdoorsy charged my account AGAIN for $341, without any warning or explanation.  My rental had then been over for two months - they are total frauds.  DO NOT WORK WITH OUTDOORSY, as a renter OR host!!!
reply:
Hi Tracy, 
 
I'm so sorry for any confusion concerning the status of your security deposit. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback. Please allow me to clarify our security deposit process. A host has the ability to request the security deposit to be held beyond the usual 7 days if they need more time to get an estimate for something that's been damaged. Once the amount needed is confirmed, the funds are drawn from the held deposit, and the remainder is released back to your payment method. If you disagree with the amount collected by your host from the security deposit, we have a dispute resolution team that's happy to step in and assist in reaching an amicable resolution. I'll be reaching out to you personally to offer assistance. I look forward to connecting with you.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Lynn Van Eaton
title: Look elsewhere
body: As a host with Outdoorsy I’ve found several inconveniences doing business through this company. Navigating their website/app seems convoluted and illogical. I have to click through too many other menu options to get to my calendar to update my availability. The app only allows you to upload up to 5 photos for the pre- and post-trip inspections and also requires printed inspections which take up a lot of time. The company is pretty paper-heavy. Hosts are required to print out the 28-page rental terms & conditions in addition to roughly a dozen pages of pre-arrival checklists, walk-throughs, booking documents, and post-trip inspection FOR EVERY GUEST. These things should all be available through the app but that’s not an option at this point. The company takes 25% of each booking payout, which is the most I’ve seen among the 5+ RV rental platforms I’ve researched. Plus they charge the host a cancellation fee of $50-$100 (after the 2nd cancellation within 6mos) even if the host has to cancel as a result of a renter doing damage to the RV or if a guest bails but doesn’t officially cancel. Why should the company, who suffers no inconvenience, get a cancellation fee? I’m giving them two stars instead of one for the fact that they seem to have a large pool of guests & hosts to connect. However if you’re looking to rent out your RV I’d look elsewhere.
reply:
Hi Lynn, 
 
I'm sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with your hosting experience so far. This is not the experience we'd prefer our hosts to have. I'd like to learn more about the issues you've encountered with our app and website and to address your feedback about our handoff process and and cancellation penalties. I'll be reaching out to you personally to clarify our policies and answer any questions you may have. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback. I look forward to connecting with you.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

So I'm changing my review from 1 to 3 because they should have contacted me when the placed the second security deposit on my credit card to explain why this was happening.

Two days after returning the vehicle despite the owner having signed off 'no damage' Outdoorsy charged my credit card another 2000.

I'm so sorry for any confusion regarding your security deposit and uncertainty about how to contact us for assistance. Your time is valuable, so thanks for taking a moment to share your feedback. The security deposit is a preauthorization that goes on hold 2 days prior to your trip. Depending on your card issuer's policy for holds and the length of your trip, it may release and reauthorize more than once until 7 days after your trip ends. Concerning our contact information, you can reach us 24/7 at 877-723-7232, support@outdoorsy.com, or via the chat bubble on our website or app. I'll be reaching out to you personally to provide additional details about your security deposit. author: Mark Kraus
title: Fraud Company!! Awful to work with but eager to take your money
body:
You noticed I received an update that somebody from Outdoorsy was going to reach out to me.  They did nothing.  This company is a sham.  They deal in bad faith and here is my update...
We have made the decision to discontinue renting our Solitude on Outdoorsy. The reason for this decision stems from an issue regarding a claim exceeding $3,000 that was not honored by Outdoorsy due to a clerical error in our profile setup. 

My plan moving forward involves lodging a formal complaint with Michigan's Insurance Commissioner, utilizing social media platforms to shed light on Outdoorsy's questionable business practices, and pursuing legal action through small claims court. The primary motivation for utilizing a platform like Outdoorsy is the assurance that our assets will be safeguarded. 

This situation is not solely about financial compensation, but rather about upholding principles of fairness and integrity in customer service. It is my objective to raise awareness about the unscrupulous practices of Outdoorsy. Denying a legitimate claim based on a clerical error demonstrates bad faith, and legally, they would not be in a favorable position. 

While pursuing legal action against Outdoorsy may require additional time and resources, it is essential to hold them accountable for their actions and ensure they prioritize the well-being of their customers.


Terrible company to work with.  

Our renter bought insurance and then incurred over $3,000 in damage.  Outdoorsy, did NOT approve claim due to a clerical error on vin#… that we believe happened on their end.  All the pictures and detailed information is proof that it was the RV insured but they looked for every loop hole not to pay.  Their customer service agent let us know that we can always sue them… what a joke of a company.  We did some investigating with other host and we heard that they never approve the insurance… Just a big fraud company taking advantage of hard working people. They were eager to sell the insurance but don’t expect to get what you pay for.  CROOKS!!

author: Stephanie Peoples
title: JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY-READ THE REVIEWS AND THE FINE PRINT
body:
JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY! 
Today is 7/29/24 and I am still dealing with a bogus scam claim from 7/4/24, 25 days later!  I have spent several hours over many many days trying to communicate with Outdoorsy and I deeply regret using them. 

We are experienced campers.  We decided to rent an RV this time to make things easy (ha!).  We paid about $400 (in addition to rental, insurance, and Outdoorsy fees) to have it delivered and set up. During our rental over the 4th of July, the power cord + surge protector failed...resulting in a smoking, melted plug.  We were left with no power, no A/C, and we are in Texas in the middle of Summer. To our surprise, the the owner filed a damage claim against us which resulted in many headaches and countless hours of wasted time-with no real help from Outdoorsy. In fact, whenever I called, I could only speak with someone in the PHILIPPINES-never the same person, and never a last name.  Sometimes someone would respond via email, but would not be available by phone.  There was always a promise to escalate, but no follow-through.

We had actually purchased the stationary insurance (also called excess insurance), which we learned that they defer to YOUR PERSONAL AUTO POLICY FIRST.  When I went back, I eventually found this in the fine print, but had to click through several pages and links to get to-essentially a rabbit hole. After the owner was told by our insurance company that this was not a qualified claim (and subsequently denied), he then pursued it with the Outdoorsy stationary policy-which was also denied. He told our insurance company that he would pursue it through the security deposit. They then emailed to ask us to approve a charge of $400-to pay for their faulty equipment-even after 2 denials stating that we were not responsible for their equipment failing. We declined. We cannot get anyone at Outdoorsy to help us with this, though we have reached out via phone and email multiple times. Today we learned that Outdoorsy has charged our card for ANOTHER $750-the original amount of our deposit.

After another call and another request to escalate-they are telling us that they have released it.  I have yet to confirm that with my CC company, but will need to make sure that all future attempts to charge us will be denied.  

A side note of the stationary/excess policy-they also add an additional charge for roadside assistance-which you should not need if you are not towing the vehicle.  In fact, the policy becomes null and void if you move the camper for any reason on a stationary policy.  

We would never even think of trusting Outdoorsy again-it's really just a platform, taking a cut, and cheaply outsourcing their customer service. 

There is no protection and no one to call who can do anything to help.
reply:
Hi Stephanie,
 
I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our platform due to a damage claim. It's our goal to provide a smooth and easy claims experience, so thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. Transparency is vitally important to us, so we'll be performing a full investigation into what happened here and will be reaching out to you soon to address your concerns. I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Jeremy
title: If you are renting your rig out on Outdoorsy, BE AWARE.
body:
I own a few vehicles that I rent to people on the platform.

Twice in the past 3 months a renter has damaged my rigs and Outdoorsy has stuck me with the repair costs as well as the lost revenue in rentals. 

A rig with a rooftop tent was taken into a parking garage and the tent was torched as well as the crossbars and some damage to my roof. 

A renter took one of my class B rv's an hour up a 1000ft of vertical, on a gutted out forest service road with 8" potholes. They pushed it so hard because they didn't want to get stranded that far out, so they drove my rig till the water pump and transmission gave out. It took me almost 8 hours to find a towing company that was even willing to retrieve the vehicle. 

Both times Outdoorsy said that the damages were not covered. 

Heartbreaking.

The day before I was suppose to go pick up the RV he told me the Rv had been in an accident and he would zelle my deposit back in a week? I was not happy but I waited and never got back my deposit! I issued a complaint with Outdoorsy but all they could do is block him from using Outdoorsy again. Now I have to try and get my money back through my bank. Please be careful and do not zelle or venmo any money to anyone,there are scammers using these websites to make people think they are trustworthy but they are not! They are just another money hungry company that does not care about the customers just their profit.

Outdoorsy Support

author: Wanderlust AV
title: I've rented my vehicles via Outdoorsy…
body:
I've rented my vehicles via Outdoorsy for three years now.  On a recent rental, one of my clients had a minor issue with their rental.  We discussed and decided a small refund would be best suited to resolve the situation.  Outdoorsy instructs you to call them if you would like to refund your client for something other than a cancellation.  When I called I was told that Outdoorsy would keep their 25% cut of the refund.  That's right, Outdoorsy keeps their 25% "host service fee" on the amount of money you REFUND as the host. 

Outdoorsy is not much of a service but provides a great disservice to both the renter, to whom they charge exorbitant fees and force the sale of often redundant insurance products but also the owner, from whom they take a 25% cut, even when the owner didn't ultimately keep those funds.  

I would love to see another business disrupt Outdoorsy and provide a quality, peer to peer, platform for rentals.  Most renters already have adequate auto insurance that covers their rental. Most owners need little more than a platform/market on which to rent their rigs and a payment processing service that allows for security deposits to be held appropriately.   What nobody needs is Outdoorsy in the middle of it all, bilking all parties for every penny they can. 

Follow up to Outdoorsy's response- yes, that's correct, you are keeping 25% of money the owner does not keep.  There is no confusion here nor is anyone implying that an additional fee is charged.

Folks, this is why Outdoorsy releases 100% of your payout after handoff; it ensures they are keeping 25% of that money no matter what happens during or after the rental.  No other platform keeps their fee, particular a 25% one, on money you don't actually net.  In this case I wanted to refund my renter $750.  Trouble is, Outdoorsy only paid me $562.50.  I can't refund money I never received and Outdoorsy refuses to return their $187.50 cut of those funds that, again, are not in my bank account, to the renter.  They refuse to explain what they are keeping that $187.50 for and I don't know a soul who could rationalize why that money wouldn't be refunded to the renter.  

I can't post images here but would love to share a breakdown of a trip with the public to shed light on the exorbitant fees Outdoorsy hits both renter and owner with.  It's classic gig economy corporate shenanigans where a real service/platform is provided but everyone gets ripped off in the process of utilizing it.

The fact that they want a 25% cut of money you don't get paid really says it all though.
reply:
Hi Wanderlust AV, 
 
I'm very sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our fee policies. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback. Please allow me to clarify how fees work in relation to refunds. Outdoorsy assesses a service fee on the booking total for both the host and the guest. These fees are nonrefundable, even if a refund is granted by the host. However, there's no additional fee assessed on refunded amounts. I'll be reaching out to you personally to provide some additional insight and address any other concerns you may have. I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Alexis Drake

author: Melissa Burke
title: NEVER AGAIN!
body:
4.2 stars looks great until you go to the consumer affairs reviews and see that Outdoorsy is rated as a "1" only because there is no option for "0" stars.
I (single female with toddler and two dogs) rented an RV through Outdoorsy and it TURNED OFF on a two lane highway where I had zero cell reception. Luckily, there was a view area to the right and I rolled it in. When a couple pulled in with cell service, the Outdoorsy support rep told me to LEAVE MY DOGS in the RV and get to safety with my child. When I asked her if it would be lifted on a tow truck (wondering how my dogs would handle sliding to the back when it was lifted), she told me yes and still insisted I leave them. The tow truck arrived well after dark and didn't contact me until close to 11pm. Who would have checked on my dogs during those 9 or so hours??? I am still so furious. Thankfully kind strangers gave all of us a ride. We ended up spending 8k on rental cars, hotels and other incidentals while we waited for the RV to get fixed so we could drive it home. After several days, we left without it and left it up to the owners to get it (it's still at a mechanic 7 hours away with most of our stuff). When I told customer support what had happened, they insisted I get some sort of restitution from the owners and did NOTHING for me. W t f did I even buy insurance for if they weren't going to cover anything? They are rude, demeaning, and only out to make money for themselves. They did not care AT ALL that I was stuck on a freeway with a toddler and 2 dogs- they didn't even send a tow for HOURS later! DO NOT USE OUTDOORSY. You will regret it. Trust me.

Thank you for bringing your recent experience to our attention. I want to express my sincere apologies for the issues you faced during your rental. Your feedback is incredibly important to us, and we take your concerns seriously. I will be personally reaching out to you via email to discuss your experience in more detail. We genuinely appreciate your feedback. Once again, I apologize for any inconvenience or frustration you experienced, and I thank you for giving us the opportunity to address your concerns.  
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: T H
title: DO NOT BOOK WITH OUTDOORSY.COM

Dale R.

author: Richard
title: Not relaible.  Cancelled 4 times!
body: The first 3 VR i reserved were not available, and cancelled within 48 hours. I thouhgt I was luckier the fourth time. It was approved and confirmed. And paid. But 1 month later (and one month prior my trip), It was cancelled again. All my camping reservations are already made and I do not have a VR now. I definitely not trust the site anymore. Not reliable. And only generic We are sorry emails.
reply:
Hi Richard,

Please accept my sincere apologies for the less-than-perfect experience with our platform due to canceled bookings. This is certainly not the experience we prefer our guests to have. I'd like to reassure you that we strongly discourage hosts from canceling their guests' bookings. A host's rating can be negatively impacted when this occurs, as well as a financial penalty being imposed. Please rest assured that I've shared your feedback with my team and our host community so we can ensure that our hosts are always keeping their listings and calendars updated. Thank you for bringing this to our attention. No worries, we'll be more than happy to assist you in finding a replacement, so please contact our 24/7 Customer Support team by calling 877-723-7232 at your earliest convenience. We look forward to hearing from you soon.

Dale R.
Customer Support

Jaime

author: Kellie Simone
title: Absolutely WORST vehicle hire company on the…
body: Absolutely WORST vehicle hire company on the internet. TERRIBLE customer support, they demanded I rent with an international drivers licence (which wasn't necessary, in the end I was right and I just used my one from Australia) I was told 6 different things by 6 different customer service reps, all were WRONG. Absolutely terrible experience, NEVER AGAIN.
reply:
Hi Kellie,

Thank you for posting a review, and I'm very sorry to hear you had a less-than-perfect experience with our service and driver verification process on your booking in December last year. That's certainly not the experience that we want for you. Please allow me to clarify that our verification process for international and domestic guests is the same, except that we may require additional documents such as a copy of passport for guests from outside the US and Canada. We go above and beyond in screening all guests by conducting a risk assessment and driver verification to ensure that all members are safe, insured, and qualified to drive. I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience it may have caused you. I'll personally get in touch with you soon with more information about our verification process and to hear your thoughts on what we can do to improve your experience next time.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Dee Wiseman
title: Outdoorsy finally stepped up.
body:
After nearly two months of dispute, Outdoorsy stepped up to the plate and refunded our damage deposit as they agreed we had not caused the damage. 

The owner, Jacob Ries, continued to be difficult so I hope Outdoorsy severed the relationship. We rented a unit thru Outdoorsy with a terrible experience. The first night the electronic panel didn't work and we were without heat. The owner responded in text that this happened from time to time but generally fixed itself... which it never did. Responses from the owner through the remaining 8 days were short and spotty. After returning the unit with no damages we were informed by Outdoorsy that our damage deposit was held due to damages to the panel and to a bug screen. We provided a copy of the owner's text admitting there were failures on the panel and advised that we did not damage the screen. The reponse was that the owner had a week to respond but we didn't hear back from Outdoorsy so after 10 days we checked back in. We were then told they have up to 8 weeks to resolve the matter but have provided no documentation from the owner. A week in Arizona with no heat and air was miserable as temps varied from below freezing to 85° at night.
reply:
Hi Dee, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our service due to the experience on your trip and the dispute process. This is most definitely not the outcome we'd prefer our guests to have. Your time is valuable, and I appreciate you taking a moment to share your feedback. Although our Resolutions team strives to reach an amicable resolution for both parties as quickly as possible, please note that this process can take up to 6-7 weeks to resolve. I'll be investigating this further, and I'll reach out personally to the registered guest on the booking. Thanks again for your feedback.
 
Jaime L
Outdoorsy Support

author: Joseph Teffeau

I'm very sorry to hear that your RV was returned with furniture damage. Upon review, it appears that this is being addressed by our Resolutions team. I'll get in touch with you soon regarding this and thank you for sharing your feedback.

Dale R.

author: Dana Stein
title: Horrible horrible horrible experience…
body:
Horrible horrible horrible experience Outdoorsy customer service! I’m a host on the site. I made a formal complaint on the Better Business Bureau website and found there are 168 other one star reviews. Shame, shame shame Outdoorsy! You need to fire your customer service leadership!! 


Reply to below reply from Outdoorsy….
It’s unconscionable that Outdoorsy is trying to defend their ridiculous dashboard system. I set up a discount code of 10% for Repeat Renters and there is a button where you can turn on the discount code. Of course I think that that means I’m turning on the code for WHEN I GIVE THE CODE TO A GUEST but NO,  instead what happened is their system gave the discount to every single renter and six renters later, I caught it! Meaning I never gave the discount code to the other customers but yet they got a 10% discount on their rental!! And then Outdoorsy tells me it’s my fault because I turned the button on. How is it even logical that I would think that every renter would get a 10% discount if I never gave them the discount code to enter in? It’s so stupid I can’t even imagine how they’re trying to justify this!! Of course the guests never said anything and Outdoorsy blames it on me.
 $409 in lost revenue and they only refunded me $100 after emailing me back-and-forth with robotic replies, I kept asking them for a phone call and it took three emails to finally get someone to call me that was unhelpful and they gave me back $100.  You still owe me $309!
reply:
Hi Dana, 

We're very sorry to hear that you're dissatisfied with the platform. This is definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our hosts to have, so thank you for sharing your feedback with us. After a thorough investigation by multiple supervisors and our engineering department, it was determined you had setup a discount code to automatically apply to bookings during the check-out flow, thus reducing your nightly rate by 10%. Although it was determined to be user error and not a technical glitch, I'm pleased to see our support specialists were able to apply a credit to your Outdoorsy account to offset some of the revenue lost due to the discount code. It appears you have removed the code from your account now which will remove the discount trigger, but please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team if you need any additional assistance. Thanks again for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. 

Dale R. 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Hunter Anstine
title: Hi my name is Hunter Anstine Outdoorsy…
body: Hi my name is Hunter Anstine Outdoorsy is a complete scam. They set up my deal, the renter flaked on me and I was still charged $3,072.19 for a rental I never received. I had to rent a separate vehicle from Enterprise. Don’t trust anything you see about Outdoorsy it’s all clever marketing to cover up scandalous behavior. To be clear Outdoorsy is not hearing me. The owner did not communicate with me appropriately and the did not deliver the vehicle. He told me he was leaving it somewhere unlocked with the keys in the cup holder and a contract to sign. That is sketchy as heck. And after that Outdoorsy took the sketchy owners side. Shame. I was not a cancellation or a no show, the renter was.
reply:
Hi Hunter, 

I'm sorry to hear you had a negative experience due to a no-show at the scheduled, agreed upon delivery location, date, and time. We always strongly encourage guests immediately communicate with the host about any arrival changes or delays so as to avoid the host delivering the RV without the guest being present. Please be advised that all bookings are subject to the host's cancellation policy that was agreed to at the time of booking, per our Terms and Conditions. My apologies again for the negative experience, and thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback. 

"Guest Cancellations and No Shows 

...If the Guest does not show up to or otherwise complete the Key Exchange at the agreed upon rental start time in the Booking, it will be treated as a Guest cancellation and subject to the Host’s cancellation policy."

If you have any further questions or concerns, please feel free to reach back out to our support rep directly within the email thread that you've been communicating.

RESPONSE TO THE UPDATE: We'd be happy to discuss this further, however, you deleted your account from our platform; therefore, due to CCPA  regulations, we are prohibited from contacting you directly. Please call our support team for any additional assistance. Thanks again! 

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: carolyn
title: If there were zero stars
body:
If there were zero stars, that would be appropriate.  Rented an RV. Picked it up and drove 20 miles to learn the leaf spring broke and it could have been catastrophic.  In a nutshell, we got the run around.  My husband and I were both calling different departments and Outdoorsy literally did nothing.  Said they were sending roadside assistance and never did.  We were on the side of the road in the dark for two hours before we left the RV to find a hotel.  Next day we were told to call our own roadside assistance.  They were then going to help us find another camper...nope.  Nothing.  No reimbursment for our hotel because they only do that for trip interuptions.  HUH? We went camping and didnt have a camper anymore.  If that isnt a trip interruption, what is? We spent double what we budgeted bc we had to get a hotel room.  All our camping food spoiled and Outdoorsy did squat.  They did offer ten percent off our next camper.  Why would we rent from them again.

Our acct number was 5258431 Under my husbands name. Jason
reply:
Hi Carolyn,
 
I'm so sorry to hear of the poor experience you had during your trip. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have while on the road, and I absolutely understand your frustration and disappointment. I'd love the opportunity to investigate further and learn more about what happened, but I'm unable to locate your account with the information provided here. Please feel free to email me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "Attn: Jaime" as the subject, and I'll be glad to look into this. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to my attention. I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime 
Outdoorsy Support

author: Ashley
title: Outdoorsy is a purposefully difficult…
body: Outdoorsy is a purposefully difficult service. Luckily I was able to talk w the owner directly for most things. The water tank leaked, there was no heat (in the mountains) and the driving conditions of the vehicle were questionable on the interstate. We drove for 14 hours in a terrible storm and found a small crack in the windshield upon returning home. Thought, no big deal, the insurance paperwork I read said they cover windshield cracks. Owner was very understanding. Since I paid $359 for MANDATORY insurance and then $500 extra for a security deposit, the owner and I figured getting the crack fixed would be a breeze. I have not been able to speak to anyone from the US to help me. I am a firefighter and my husband is a retired NAVY SEAL. We want to do the right thing for the owner and also have this company honor the insurance. They have directed me to a long contract that was “signed” but NOT by us. They’ve also changed the terms of the insurance agreement multiple times. This is fraud. They take good peoples money and then will not do the ethical thing by fixing the crack for the owner. After paying $850 in addition to the rent and fees, this more than covers the owner being able to get his RV repaired and for me to not pay any more money out of pocket. I paid $1500 to rent. $850 for insurance and security deposit. The owner only received $829 for the week rental. This is not morally or ethically acceptable. They do not want to serve their customers, the owners or respect that hard working Americans and veterans do not deserve to be manipulated and abused.
reply:
Hi Ashley,

I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our platform due to a damage claim. It is our goal to provide a smooth and easy claims experience, so thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. Transparency is vitally important to us, so we'll be performing a full investigation into what happened here and will be reaching out to you soon to address your concerns.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

Outdoorsy Support

author: Brad
title: I rented a travel trailer that stated…
body:
I rented a travel trailer that stated was "brand spanking new" that had too much to list here wrong with it. Broken awning, bathroom vent, etc. Through observation, video, and photos this was not new or even close. But what really turned things was me being alerted by someone at an intersection in the midst of returning vehicle that one of the tires was making a "bad sound". My wife and I rolled down windows and could definitely hear what she was saying. Tried to see if I could determine the cause. Thought it might be one tire that looked like missing something. At that time, I messaged owner rep to say something was wrong and asked if I should stop or continue to return. Was told camper had always had an issue with a tire and that I should be fine. A few miles later the tread on the tire separated causing a massive blowout and damage to the undercarriage. Was told in this process that there was no visible evidence of outside damage and tire appeared to be faulty. Also, through this process was told that tires were new by owner (worded in such a fashion as if to release her of any liability because this doesn't happen to new tires, while failing to recognize this is where they happen the most). Was also told that there had been a blowout on the same tire 2 months prior. Was told that is why "I" have insurance and within hours my $500 deductible was taken. I disputed the deductible because I am to be given a safe and reliable vehicle to rent. Was not provided a safe and reliable vehicle, and then when something on the camper failed, the expectation was of me to fix it or pay for it. This would be the final response from my dispute...

Hi Bradley,
 
After an investigation and careful consideration of the evidence provided by both parties, we have decided in your favor.
 
We believe you should be refunded $250 from the security deposit claimed. While we uncovered that the tires were over 5 years old already, we believe you should shoulder $250 since the damage was incurred during your rental. 

And here is my response and reason for 1 star....

    Think I will consult with someone on this. Aside from a contradiction within your response which has to leave someone lying by stating they were outdated tires when I had multiple people state tires were new (with one very important individual stating they were new and faulty), it is the holding the renter accountable due to the actions of an owner that finds this resolution appalling to me.

       If what you state in your resolution is correct, then the lack of action by an owner to provide safe and reliable tires has taken place. If what the owner and her handyman stated (and documented) is correct in stating the tires were newly purchased, it was they who purchased and installed an inferior product and not myself. Had I punctured a tire, went to purchase a new tire on my own, the tire then exploded causing damage, I would most certainly be singing a different tune. But your resolution seems to state that I must pay 250 dollars because someone provided me with an unsafe and unreliable vehicle for rent. It's hard for me to not think of a thousand scenarios at play here that would represent the same type of conclusion. Such as, what if an owner has an engine failing?  Was repaired with substandard or improper parts that result in complete engine failure while on the next rental. Would the responsibility of the renter be to replace the engine? If so, then there are no protections from the owner's doing whatever they want, with WHATEVER they want, because of no consequence. And I have a significant issue with this because the initial response from the owner of my rental was just that. And I quote, "that's why you have insurance".

      As far as I'm concerned, the first and foremost priority from the owner is to provide a safe and reliable vehicle for rental. The fact this did not happen should instantly render any contract null and void and prohibited anyone from obtaining my deposit. I seem to believe most people would agree with me. And what adds insult to injury is that you have a host who is falsely advertising a vehicle that is not as it is portrayed.

     What also bothers me greatly is the fact that I reached out to the owner's representative prior to any damage occurring to state there was an issue. Specifically asked him whether to continue or not. Only to be told they have always had an issue with a tire and I should continue.

     I will say basically the same thing I said to the owner. This is absolutely about principle. I do not believe I should pay 500/250/or even 5 dollars. The responsibility of repairs needed due to faulty parts and equipment provided by an owner should not rest on the renter in any shape or form but that of the owner.
reply:
Hi Brad, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a poor experience on your trip due to a tire blowout and you're dissatisfied with the dispute resolution provided by our Resolutions team. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to our attention. I'll be investigating further on my end, and I'll reach out to you personally. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: jane
title: great platform
body: great platform, but website not user friendly

author: Nigel Bell
title: Abandoned by Outdoorsy
body:
I hired a RV through Outdoorsy through August 2023 (20 nights). They sub-let to another company in the days/week before commencement - being told the campervan I booked (5 months earlier) was no longer available. A 'similar' vehicle was available when I arrived (same price, same conditions).  Wrong.
No stealth camping as few curtains (!), no pillows (!), limited stove or utensils (!), no outdoor seating or  cover (!).  Not what I expected or paid for ....

But worse was the developing problems with the two-year old automatic vehicle (a Mercedes van!).
From Day 1 it had problems - wrong gear, shuddering forward, over-revving - which was increasingly dangerous when driving on US freeways at half the set speed.  Stop-start - stop - shudder - stop - start - stop altogether - so multiple calls to the intermediary company, then Outdoorsy.

Outdoorsy provides intermittent phone calls/call-backs during the afternoon, leading to a request to drive (!) to the nearest specialist repair workshop (30 miles away).  Tried on back-roads .. but same problems and the vehicle again died half way, leaving me at the edge of the road in 108F degree heat in the middle of no-where ... for 15 hours /overnight!   
  
Many Outdoorsy phone calls - no action - before next morning the tow vehicle was sent to the previous (wrong) address!

Eventually, towed away to the repair shop.  Long wait before they diagnosed the problem.  Meantime, Outdoorsy told me I was one my own - they wouldn't supply an alternative vehicle.  I was expected to start all over again with a new hire (whilst marooned in the middle of nowhere!).  
"Not good enough, we have a paid contract" I said - and let me speak to the manager.  It took a week before a manager sent an email back, asking about my problem !!!!   

And fortunately for me, the dealer found and fixed the 'drive' cables/electronics that had been chewed through by 'varmints' prior to my hire.  So after two delayed days of hassle I was able to continue.

LESSONS:  
Outdoorsy would not meet their contractual conditions when it got hard....they made their vehicle replacement problems mine ...
Outdoorsy is better at PR and marketing than delivering ...   
Outdoorsy subsequently made it very difficult to get a refund for the lost two days of travel ... 
Don't believe their promotional spin .. my experience says you're on you're own if there's a vehicle replacement problem.
reply:
Hi Nigel, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a poor experience with your trip and the outcome of your resolution. This is certainly not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. After further review of your account, I can confirm I found no violation of our contractual obligations and your requested refund during the dispute resolution process was transacted back to your payment method on 9/20/23. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share this feedback with us. I'll be reaching out to you directly to discuss this further.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Morten
title: Do not use Outdoorsy!
body:
I would not recommend using this company! Their internal procedures for damage claims are not working properly. 

I was charged for a full windshield replacement more than 6 months after my rental ended. When I returned the vehicle it was with a minor chip in the windshield (which I reported), clearly repairable and part of the windshield protection under my rental agreement. But the repair was not made until 3,5 months after ended rental at which point a full replacement was necessary and which I was billed for. I have been in dialogue with the claims team to explain to them my dissatisfaction with the overall process but they do not seem to care.
We had a good time in the van but will not be using Outdoorsy again unfortunately.
reply:
Hi Morten, 
 
Please accept my sincere apologies for your negative experience with our claims process. This is most definitely not the outcome we'd prefer our guests to have. The details in your review are deeply concerning, so thank you so much for bringing this to my attention. I've investigated further on my end and reached out to you personally via email.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: David H
title: International card fees
body:
The disclosed fees were already quite high, but to then make a separate charge for an undisclosed, 3% foreign transaction fee, felt wrong and odd.
I’d deliberately used a debit card which doesn’t charge foreign transaction fees for the US trip. Nowhere else did I encounter a foreign/international transaction fee.

Update: Your reply seems at odds with the Chase UK terms, which are as follows.

“ You can use your Chase card to pay for things or withdraw cash outside the UK (cash withdrawal limits apply).

We don’t charge fees when you use your card abroad and we'll apply the current Mastercard exchange rate to any purchases you make in the local currency. Local banks may charge their own fees when you use one of their cash machines and you should make sure you understand what these are before making a withdrawal. You can use your numberless card to make free withdrawals at any Chase Bank ATM in the United States.”
As I said, I’ve not experienced these fees before when using this card with other vendors. Are you sure it’s not your card processor fees, which you’re passing on?
reply:
Hi David,

Thank you for posting a review, and I'm sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with the foreign transaction fee. I'd like to reassure you that this fee is not an Outdoorsy fee or charge but a fee stipulated by your card payment processor when using a card issued outside of the country in which you’re renting. Transparency is very important to us, so please allow me to clarify that when processing a reservation charge on our platform, a warning of the foreign transaction fee is displayed before checkout. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact our 24/7 Customer Support team via chat or by calling 877-723-7232.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Erica and Lincoln Faircloth
title: We love the peace of mind we get…
body: We love the peace of mind we get renting our van through Outdoorsy. Knowing my guests and I are 100% covered if anything were to happen makes the process so much more enjoyable. The app and platform for hosting needs improvement but I think Outdoorsy is working on it.

author: Jonathan Griggs
title: Great company and service
body:
The company does a good job of providing a platform to connector renters with rv/trailer owners. The fees are reasonable in comparison to other such sites and the experience is generally more easy going. If I could provide a piece of constructive criticism, as an owner I regularly receive booking requests or messages while I am away from my PC, but the phone app (at least the iPhone app) does not have a "host" mode that allows me to review the booking request and it can be tricky to get the information pulled up on my mobile browser sometimes. 
Additionally, I have found that I cannot rely on the message-to-text capability. I have regularly received inquiries or messages from guests that do not show up as a text message on my phone while others do. It would provide a much better experience if this were more consistent.

author: Umar Chouhdry
title: Beware of Outdoorsy's Misleading…
body:
Beware of Outdoorsy's Misleading Cancellation Policy and Poor Customer Service
I recently had an incredibly disappointing experience with Outdoorsy, a company that seemed promising for RV rentals. My ordeal began when I booked a van for a trip in June, well in advance. Realizing the costs were too steep for my budget 10 days later, I decided to cancel, expecting a reasonable refund considering the trip was nearly 5 months (June 2024) away. To my shock, I discovered that Outdoorsy had a hidden trap in their cancellation policy, costing me over $1000 in cancellation fees, for a service I never utilized.
The van owner, under the guise of convenience, persuaded me to switch from Turo to Outdoorsy, which I suspect was a maneuver to exploit Outdoorsy's cancellation policy for his financial gain. Outdoorsy's policy, which mercilessly penalizes customers for cancellations made outside a narrow 48-hour window post-booking, is both unfair and predatory, especially when cancellations are made well in advance of the booking date.
Their defense? A rigid adherence to a policy that favors hosts at the expense of customers, trapping hard-earned money with no service provided. The fact that this trip was months away and the rushed booking process facilitated by the van owner makes this policy even more egregious.
Outdoorsy's lack of transparency and customer empathy has left me feeling cheated and disillusioned. Their policies do not protect the interests of the customer but serve to enrich themselves and their hosts at our expense. I caution anyone considering using Outdoorsy for their RV rental needs to think twice. You may find yourself, like me, out of pocket for services you never received, entangled in a web of unfair policies designed to profit from your change of plans.
Final Rating: 1/5 Stars
Avoid Outdoorsy if you value your money and seek a fair, customer-centric rental experience. There are other platforms out there that respect and protect their customers' interests far better.
reply:
Hi Umar, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had to cancel your booking and that you're dissatisfied with the host's cancellation policy and our customer support. I've thoroughly investigated the booking in question and your recent interactions with our support team, and I'll be reaching out to you directly. Your time is valuable, so I appreciate you sharing your feedback and bringing this to my attention. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support
