author: Laurie Aaron-Chodoronek
title: Our family of 6 got scammed by Outdoorsy!!
body: Our family of 6 is planning a 10 day road trip this summer and wanted one vehicle comfortable for us all. I found the perfect vehicle on Outdoorsy, absolutely stunning! We were all so excited. The booking was glitchy and instead of charging a deposit with the reservation I was charged the full $1,300 for the trip that doesn't start for 5 months! I contacted customer service, they fixed it and charged me $757 in Feb. for a Jul. trip, balance later. I never received a confirmation from the vehicle owner, just from Outdoorsy. A few weeks later my daughter inquired about vehicle features and found out the vehicle was sold a year and a half ago and the owner can't get Outdoorsy to remove it. I contacted customer service to cancel and refund all my money. I was told they won't refund my money unless the vehicle owner verifies my claim. Now the vehicle owner has become unresponsive too. If you try to cancel the reservation on Outdoorsy they keep a service charge of $145. It's a total mess and Outdoorsy accepts no responsibility in validating a reservation, or vehicle availability or promptly refunding my money. For a finish, I'm now disputing the charge with my credit card company under fraud protection. Outdoorsy took my money, won't refund it, for a vehicle that isn't available. These people are scammers! Watch out!
reply:
Hi Laurie, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear your host has sold their van and failed to remove their listing from Outdoorsy, causing you to be able to instant book the reservation. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. Upon review, I see that you submitted previous feedback and are currently working with a team member to resolve this. Rest assured, we want to support you in resolving this matter as quickly as possible. My teammate Dale is investigating this thoroughly and will be reaching back out to you regarding the next steps. Please bear in mind that if you submit a dispute with your bank, it could take up to 90 days to be resolved, and we won't be able to process a refund on our end when there's an open chargeback. We appreciate your patience and your feedback. Please watch your email for a response from Dale, as we hope to resolve this as soon as possible.
 
Jaime 
Outdoorsy Support

author: christie
title: Avoid at all costs!!
body:
Edited to add that following my review outdoorsy have finally sent us on the 'invoices' from the owner which is a screen grab from Amazon and a shopping cart for another website! The owner hasn't even ordered the parts they've charged us for and outdoorsy have still taken the money from us!



Steer clear!! We visited LA and hired an RV through outdoorsy for ten nights to see some sights and enjoy a family holiday. 
On delivery of the RV the owners told us they'd been up all night trying to fix the slide out after the family that rented it and finished with it the night before our trip has broken it and we need to be extra careful with it as it was not fully fixed, handed us a screwdriver and sent us on our way. Should have run a mile then! 
First night we struggled with the slide out and the over cab bunk wouldn't work, asked owners for help and they said they'd work it out and let us know the morning after!! So 3 beds down and no where for the kids to sleep we finally found a you tube video on how to over ride the system and bring the beds down.
Long story short we had several issues with the slide out and the owners advise was to not use it, but since we'd rented the RV as a family of 6 we needed the slide out working in order to have enough space to sleep. Ended up having to stay in one place so that the owners who were exceptionally rude and aggressive over the phone could come meet us to try fix the issue, they managed a quick fix but told us we'd only be able to perform one more slide action and so we couldn't visit anywhere else in it and had to drive in one from San Fran to LA. During one of our drives an awning over one slide out actually came loose and on advice from the owners my husband and sons had to climb up on to the roof of the RV to remove it!! We stayed in touch with outdoorsy via email during the trip so that they were aware of the issues we were having and they continually reassured us that they would help us claim compensation on return to the UK. 
The owners also advised us that the TVs in the RV didn't work without dvds (despite advertising that it had an outdoors TV entertainment system and two indoors) so we never used the TVs.

When we delivered the RV back to the owner she herself didn't turn up and instead sent her husband who rushed us out saying he was late for a school play (they chose the collection time) he wouldn't do a walk round but verbally confirmed happy with the RV and zoomed off within five minutes of us arriving back. 
On return to the UK they've claimed $1300 of our deposit and have only started 'TV and slide out topper' as the reason. We've asked them to confirm what was wrong and what they mean by that but got the response 'tv and slide out topper' 

We've gone through outdoorsy internal review and they took over a month to decide that they don't know who's at fault and cannot provide us with evidence or proof of even a basic description of what they've claimed the money for as they say the money was taken by the host and not outdoorsy (shows as outdoorsy on our statement) 

They do not want to know. I'm not sure how money can be taken without any proof as to what for or why. 

Were assuming the slide out topper means the awning they actually told us to remove and was also missing from the other side of the RV so I'm certain somewhere another family have been charged for that and it's never been fitted or replaced. 

I've warned outdoorsy that this owner needs removing from the platform but as long as it's making them money they don't seem to care. 

Do not book with this company please save yourself the hassle. A month on from our holiday we're still chasing our money back. 

When the initial claim was made on our deposit outdoorsy told us it was just a hold whilst the internal investigation was ongoing and that it wouldn't actually be taken but a few days later it left our account.

Dishonest, unreliable and unhelpful.
reply:
Hi Christie, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a less-than perfect experience with our service due to issues while on your trip resulting in a dispute. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. I've investigated further on my end and have reached out to you directly to discuss this in greater detail. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to my attention.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

On the road last summer, a small rock hit the windshield and made a tiny crack on the windshield. We immediately reported it to the owner and Outdoorsy. 2 weeks later, we got our full deposit back. Then, despite their windshield policy coverage, Outdoorsy charged us $900, 4 months (!) after the rental, for a full windshield replacement. author: Aaron
title: Beware
body:
From the very beginning the people we were dealing with were less than up front about various maintenance issues.  They knew exactly how many miles we were traveling and that we were using the unit to move our 97 year old mother, who had just had a stroke.

Basically there were several things that would fail, cause further damage and even perhaps cause injury that we had to fix.  When we returned the unit the owners claimed there was damage from a slide out unit that, to our knowledge, was working when we dropped the unit off.  We just walked away from the $1500 deposit and then, after dragging things on for a month they want more money.  I billed them for the work we had to perform and hope they just leave it at that.

The impression I got from the process is that leaving things that will cause further damage is a strategy to have an excuse to grab the deposit from unsuspecting renters.  At this point it feels like they are trying to pick us up by our feet to shake all the change out of our pockets. We have already paid thousands of dollars and the maintenance of the vehicle should not be our problem.      

Just beware, I can't speak about all the owners, but these seem to want to take advantage of us.  The extra insurance we purchased has proven unresponsive and the Outdoorsy customer support is offering to try to bring resolution, so will see if this can end any time this year...

Update: The interior damage insurance we purchased did come through, but it took quite a while.  To their credit Outdoorsy did give us a token credit to express good will and acknowledge our inconvenience with the issues we experienced.  Maybe through the process the owners, who are young, will learn about maintaining their equipment.  

Just beware, don't make assumptions and ask questions about the condition of the equipment.  According to the Outdoorsy policy, if the owners disclose a problem and you accept the rental, you become responsible for the problem and any further damage it may cause while in your possession.  If you experience issues with the equipment that require attention, if the owners are not cooperative, you are stuck on the side of the road and may be financially responsible for getting repairs done.
reply:
Hi Aaron, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had these issues with your recent booking. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. I've researched this thoroughly on my end, and I've reached out to you directly with some additional information.
 
Jaime L.
Outdoorsy Support

They referred us to an arbitrator in the us. We did not get back our money because in fact it is our duty to prove the problem but since we’re not longer in Canada, that was almost impossible.
Be very, very careful and only rent RV‘s that have good reviews and the owner seems good! And if you pickup the rental, do not sign anything if you’re not really sure about the van!

I'm terribly sorry to hear that you had such a negative experience on your trip and that it ended with a dispute. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. Unfortunately, once a guest declines our internal dispute resolution offer and opts for arbitration instead, we're unable to reopen it on our end. All FairClaims arbitration decisions are final and legally binding. Again, I'm very sorry that the outcome was not what you were hoping for. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

I had an issue with damaging the awning, I reported the claim, the forced me to submit it to my insurance company (state farm) and then when state farm would not pay, they took me to court.  The $455.60 is a total rip off and they have no intention of covering anything with that money they are charging you.  In the end the claim was paid by state farm and my auto insurance weny up significantly.  I would NEVER use this service again, it is a total rip-off!

author: Brendan Forrest
title: Absolute NIGHTMARE Story
body:
Hired an RV out of LA by Eugene. A day before hire ended, the RV broke down in 50 degree C heat. And the Host actions were appalling, Constantly blaming us and making us stay with the RV even though the location of brake down was not safe. We purchased the highest level of insurance through outdoorsy, and at this point found out that the insurance means nothing. The is no "Concierge" service as stated. They call a tow truck for you and thats about it. No alternate hotel room or ride. The host then took us to disputes in house at outdoorsy and they were completely toothless, resolved nothing and only Liaised between us and the Host. They then sent us to Fair clams where the host sued us for $23,000 in damages allegedly cause by us. Long story short, he lost and now won't give us our deposit back when he needs no evidence of wrong doing to claim against his customers. 

In summation. Insurance means nothing, 
when on the road no one is obligated to assist you and if something goes wrong they pass you further down the line till they can wipe their hands of you. Spend the extra money and use a reputable business that actually maintain their vehicles.
reply:
Hi Brendan, 
 
I'm terribly sorry to hear your trip ended with a breakdown dispute, and that you're dissatisfied with our resolution process. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. I'll be looking into this further and reach out to you directly to discuss this in greater detail. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

title: Questionable practices
body: 16 hours of driving from Florida to upper Michigan I received a text from outdoorsy stating I hadn't filled out application properly and my camper may not be available.  I missed the drivers license part, as I was not driving rv and it was being delivered and picked up. So I had to stop, take pictures of myself and forward them to outdoorsy or my camper wouldn't be available.  After explaining to customer service that I paid for delivery and pickup they still insisted this process be completed.  Reservation was 2 months prior so any issues could have easily been rectified weeks prior. Already had their funds, so this made no sense whatsoever
reply:
Hi Mark,
 
I'm so sorry to hear that you're dissatisfied with our ID verification requirements. This step is required for all reservations booked through Outdoorsy, whether the RV will be driven or not. Our insurance provider requires each customer to verify their ID to ensure that the RV is protected in the event that there's any type of damage during the rental period. Thank you so much for sharing your experience, and I'll be reaching out to you directly to further discuss your feedback.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: BRANIN BOYACK
title: App works well
body: App works well I just wish it let you click to open more of the info

author: Stephen Rideout
title: Outdoorsy is the real deal
body:
Outdoorsy is the real deal, completely walks both renters and owners thru the process and provides valuable tools along the way to protect both parties.  I am very happy utilizing Outdoorsy for my RV adventures and rentals!
Stephen

author: Justin Holland
title: Too Greedy
body:
Outdoorsy takes too much of a cut from the owner.  They say 25%, but we just did a rental where our client paid $1500 and we only collected $850.  That’s too expensive for the client and not enough money for the folks with all the skin in the game.  
Also, the app is a little clunky sometimes.  Especially when doing a key exchange with a renter, and it won’t load photos, or won’t process requests.
reply:
Hi Justin,
 
I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our service fees and are having some issues with our app. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our hosts to have. I've reached out to you directly to provide some additional information about the fee structure and to learn more about the issues you're having with the key exchange in the app. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Wendy Zawada Straub
title: Small Damage Very Costly
body:
My husband ran over a semi-truck re-tread tire remnant on a remote highway where we could not dodge to avoid the piece of tire. There was some damage to the motorhome and it has been a nightmare to repair. Of course the repair was beyond the $1000 deductible for the insurance I purchased with the rental. Have never seen an estimate for the repairs so hard to understand this. 
There is literally 30 pages of fine print YOU MUST READ ALL AND UNDERSTAND COMPLETELY. I will never use this service again.
reply:
Hi Wendy, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear the RV sustained some damage while on your trip. This is most definitely not the outcome we'd prefer our guests to have. I know the claims process can be a bit difficult to understand, and there's a lot of information to sift through. Our claims team strives to process all damage claims within 7-10 business days, but sometimes it can take a bit longer. I've emailed you directly with some additional information, but for the most recent update on your claim, please feel free to contact your claims rep within the email thread you're currently communicating. Thank you so much for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Jon Clapper
title: Owners beware
body: I have 3 RVs that I rent and I use 4 different platforms to assist in renting to dozens and dozens of people each year. It's a retirement side hustle that I enjoy. Outdoorsy is by far the absolute worst. I had damage done to a unit in May and I still have not been reimbursed by Outdoorsy insurance for the repairs necessary. For months I have been getting the run around from their claims and customer service depts. I'm beginning to think that are going broke and hanging onto every dollar they can get their hands on. It's a shame and owners beware!
reply:
Hi Jon, 
 
Thanks for your feedback, and I sincerely apologize for the delay in processing your claim. Although most claims can be resolved within 7-14 days, some cases take longer. Please rest assured that we'll share your feedback with our Claims team and follow up with them. We'll contact you soon to provide you with an update. 
 
Dale R. 
Outdoorsy Support

title: Fake ads?
body:
Tried to book a campervan rental in California for April. Gave my credit card info and DL info. No reply from owner and no reply to follow-up email. Are the camper van postings legitimate??? Please don't waste my time with fake ads.

I'm so sorry to hear your preferred host has been unresponsive. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. I want to assure you that you won't be charged for a reservation until a host confirms your booking. I've reached out to you directly to provide some insight on the booking process that may help you in successfully booking your trip. Thanks so much for taking the time to share your feedback with us.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Jonathan Maynard
title: I’ve left Outdoorsy due to their bad policy
body:
I rented out my RV through Outdoorsy. Most of the time things went great. Recently I had a person reserve my RV, and I have a strict cancellation policy so that our rental income is maintained if someone decides they want to cancel. 

Several days before the scheduled pickup, they were deemed “disqualified” for rental by Outdoorsy. 

The renter refused to cancel, and long story short, Outdoorsy says that it’s not the renters fault and not Outdoorsy’s fault. So, I am the one that loses out. 

Outdoorsy should require people to qualify BEFORE they are allowed to rent. However, they don’t because it would put a roadblock to people renting, and that would not benefit them. 

So, because they choose greed over relationships, I have left their platform. I have also convinced 7 people to do the same as Outdoorsy is only interested in protecting themselves and not the people that invest in RVs that even make their platform possible. 

I will make more money off platform, and will be protected by other insurance that allows rentals. Plus, I don’t have to worry about policies that only protect them. 

I gave them a chance to make this right, and they refused. They cost themselves tens of thousands of dollars by not holding my renter to their commitment.
reply:
Hi Jonathan,

Thanks for sharing your feedback, and I'm sincerely sorry that your experience with our service didn't match your expectations. I'll investigate to see what happened here and will get in touch with you soon to address your concerns.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Jose Robles
title: Good communication and quick response, and a suggestion
body: Overall great communication and good options. Something to consider for the traveler or event the owner is to see if Outdoorsy could offer travel insurance for RV rentals that are outside of the range of the owner. Considering an RV service call is anywhere from $150-200 for the call I would have gladly paid $100 plus deductible for peace of mind if anything happens out in the road. I know it's not a thing now, but maybe it could be? Maybe offer it as a membership like triple A but for unseen repairs. Food for thought.

Dale R.

author: D. Mia
title: Outdoorsy's insurance is a scam.
body: Outdoorsy's insurance is at best misleading, at worst a scam.  When you read the details, their $1,000,000 insurance coverage claim is for DAMAGE to an RV, not Liability Coverage like normal.  You'll never need the $1,000,000 Damage coverage because no one is renting a $1,000,000 RV.  Their liability coverage is actually just the legal minimum, often just $20,000 or so, which is nothing for liability coverage.  And it's overpriced.
reply:
Hi there, 

I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our protection packages. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. Please allow me to clarify the insurance a bit more. We partner with Assurant Mutual, and all protection packages will vary depending on the Class, year, make, and model of the RV, and those rates are set at industry standards based on the Actual Cash Value of the RV provided to us by the host. This coverage is required for RVs that are being delivered as well. Similar to an auto rental, RVs must have this episodic coverage to protect both parties should any vandalism, theft, acts of nature, etc. occur while the RV is in your possession.

For more information about our coverages, please visit https://www.outdoorsy.com/help/owners-protection-packages.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Nancy Pietroski
title: JUST MAKE SURE YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A…
body:
JUST MAKE SURE YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A CLAIM!
JUST MAKE SURE YOU LIST ALL VERIFIED DRIVERS!
Our hosts did not fulfill their commitment to check that my husband and I were listed with Outdoorsy as verified drivers (my husband was not, and he did ALL of the driving). The Outdoorsy website is clunky to use and adding a verified driver is opaque, so I missed this when I made the reservation online. A stone hit the windshield of the Sprinter we rented and the windshield had to be replaced by the owner when we got back. I am not sure why the hosts are complaining on these reviews that they had to file a claim, because our host made us as the renters file the claim. Outdoorsy/Roamly immediately denied it because my husband was not listed as a verified driver (again, the hosts should have checked this before the keys were handed to my husband, a "non-verified" driver). The toll free number for Outdoorsy has customer service people who are trying to help, but it was IMPOSSIBLE to get in touch with a live person in the CLAIMS department. They charged our credit card on file for the replacement windshield ($1600!!) - even though our card was on file with them, they did NOT GIVE US A HEADS UP that the charge would appear. They claimed they would help us work with our insurance company to reclaim some of the money and BUT THEY DIDN'T. It took WEEKS for them to send me the copy of the invoice to send to my insurance company. They may be fine to rent through IF YOU DON'T HAVE TO FILE A CLAIM, but if you do, get ready to invest alot of time into an excruciating process.
reply:
Hi Nancy,

I'm very sorry to hear there was damage to your rented RV and that you had a less-than-perfect experience with our claims process. Upon reviewing your account, it appears that you were verified as an insured driver; however, there is no other driver/s included in your booking. Please allow me to clarify that the primary guest who registers and agrees to the Outdoorsy Terms and Conditions is ultimately responsible for any additional drivers insured through Outdoorsy’s policy. I'll get in touch with you regarding this to provide you with more information and to ensure that all drivers are covered in your future bookings. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: AC
title: Horrible Host Experience
body:
This is a review from a host experience. First of all, I will say that Outdoorsy's response team is extremely fast and I've never had an issue with connecting with support via chat or phone instantly. The representatives have always been kind and worked within their capacity.

However, I do have a serious issue with how much Outdoorsy makes out of their hosts. They take an average of 25% (including minute processing fees afterwards, which in my opinion.. if you're taking 20% and above at least don't charge processing fees for minute fees out or principle). 

To me, this is an absurd amount. Much more than other rental platforms. How much Outdoorsy takes decreases if you make more money with them. Which to me is also odd, because as a rental listing company, you'd imagine that they want to play power in numbers so the more hosts you have, the more the company makes. However, they just charge a lot. At the end of the day, unlike AirBnb... the host has to deal with all the crap that comes with owning a car.. maintenance, fixing, and even paperwork... It's a tech company but they still make users use paperwork as well as sign online which makes 0 sense to me. I never remember signing something on paper on AirBnB. This is especially atrocious because owning a house also comes with all these troublesome maintenance and cleaning, however a house increases in value while a car decreases. 

Having this in mind, I would imagine at least Outdoorsy would match AirBnb's take from their hosts.

Also, I recently had an incident where it was horribly managed and we are now left to fix the damages on our rig without even being able to take from the renter's deposit as they are denying the damages while Departure / Return photos clearly show what happened during the trip.
reply:
Hi Alexandra, 

Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding our service fees and your recent claim experience. It's important to us that your voice is heard, so I'll be looking into this further on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly with some additional information. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: tim griffin
title: Review
body: Overall I like your host support center. Everyone I spoke to was very helpful. However, your service to my last renters questions were poor. I almost lost them as a renter due to your lack of communication with them. You didn’t respond to them when they asked questions and apparently lost emails containing his partners driver license which he had to send again. I hope you improve your response time to potential guests so that hosts don’t lose potential renters.
reply:
Hi Tim,

I'm so sorry to hear your guests had such a poor experience trying to communicate with our support team. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our customers to have. I'll be reviewing their contact history thoroughly to find out more about what happened, and I'll reach out to you directly for further discussion. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to my attention.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Joel Hobdy
title: Great platform for RV rentals and more
body: Great platform for RV rentals and more. I highly recommend

author: Vantastic Voyage Campervan
title: As a host
body:
As a host, the real benefit is the insurance blanket outdoorsy has for host/owners. BUT. Once the owner has to file a claim the process takes weeks, if not months, and outdoorsy takes weeks to answer emails and they have never called to make sure everything is ok, or what the process will be. 
I filed a claim two months ago, I am still waiting for the internal outdoorsy adjuster to provide their findings regarding what they will cover.  I paid out of pocket to have this fixed since the reviews matter. Outdoorsy takes 27% of the rental $$$.. Maybe they can upgrade their staff and improve the claim process since owners like myself are the way they make $$$.
reply:
Hi Vantastic Voyage Campervan,

Thank you for posting a review, and my sincere apologies for the delay in resolving your claim. While most claims are processed within 7-14 days, in some cases it may take longer. Please rest assured that I'll share your feedback with our Claims team, and we'll get in touch with you soon to provide you with an update.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Josh
title: CLAIMS DEPARTMENT IS TERRIBLE TO WORK WITH
body:
If you are an owner thinking about renting on Outdoorsy be wary. Their claims department is TERRIBLE to deal with. 
Our RV was recovered after being stolen on an Outdoorsy trip. 24 days later it is just sitting at the place it was towed to without an estimate for repairs. The claims team is horrible to deal with and will not respond to any questions you have.
reply:
Hi Josh,

I'm so sorry to hear that your RV was stolen and recovered with damage, and you've had a poor experience with your claim. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our hosts to have. Your review is deeply concerning, so I'll be investigating thoroughly on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to my attention.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Christine Dozier
title: I’m not happy with the length of time…
body: I’m not happy with the length of time claims are taking and the running around and excess time to manage claims since you changed to offering excess only insurance policies that have to be denied by the renters auto insurance before the insurance purchased through outdoorsy kicks in.    I received a denial due to mechanical breakdown and since it broke down in AZ and I am in MO, when I picked up the RV in AZ after work was completed the windshield was cracked, a tire was damaged, a hubcap was missing, the hitch mounted bike rack was missing and other exterior and interior damage and I can’t get an answer as to how to get this taken care of.  We already went through fair claims and was told it is an insurance issue but I don’t have the renters auto insurance to make a claim. I have emailed multiple times per week since June and still no resolution!  Now the renter and I are being told we have to go through fair claims again.
reply:
Hi Christine,

I'm very sorry to hear your RV sustained damage and that you're dissatisfied with our claims process. We'll investigate this thoroughly to see what happened here and will get in touch with you soon. Thank you for sharing your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

title: filling claim with outdoorsy is complete nightmare.

Unless I specify called to get an answer, I was looking for I never was reached out to without me making it happened. and to later find out all information given to me over the phone was false.
Very disappointed as they claim their high service fees are apparently to go to situations like this.

I'm very sorry to hear your RV sustained damage and my sincere apologies for the less-than-perfect experience with our claims process. It is our goal to provide a smooth and easy claims experience, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. Please rest assured that I'll pass your feedback along to our Claims team. Dale R.

author: Kimberly
title: I have had an outstanding claim for 4 months.
body:
I have had an outstanding claim since June with interior and exterior damage that has truly been a terrible experience with Outdoorsy and their 3rd party Insurance company Roamly.

I have been nothing but patient trying to get responses every time I call they tell me someone will reach out. :(

It’s very frustrating with out of pocket fees plus the hefty fees we pay to use the platform to provide insurance.

Our RV was out of commission for almost a month during prime camping season. And we’ve had to take it into service three times trying to get all issues repaired from one renter.

We are so thankful that all of our other renters have been absolutely amazing and they make this so enjoyable but not sure we would recommend using this platform at this time.  

I will be sure to update this review if we ever see a resolution/response.

Hi Kimberly,

author: Sarah Purington
title: Best Customer Support - how unusual!
body:
I have finished my 3rd rental recently (as a host), and I could not be more pleased. I have needed to utilize Outdoorsy Support numerous times as I was learning to navigate their site, app, financial questions, and how to properly prepare for renting my RV out - probably between 10-15 times. Each time, I felt that I was talking to a real person who attentively listened to my problem and found a solution promptly.
Every single time, the person I was communicating with found a solution - without having to transfer me, put me on a significant hold, or otherwise make me jump through hoops. Outdoorsy has some of the best support I have ever experienced, both on the phone and on their Chat.

I had a lot of worries when I first endeavored to list my RV on a rental platform, and subject it to the treatment of complete strangers. But I have been pleasantly surprised at my experience thus far - my renters have been excellent communicators, attentive listeners when I walk them through my rv, and have evidently treated my RV as if it was their own, prized possession. 

I highly recommend Outdoorsy as a trusted platform to offer personal RVs for rent. As a host, I find their services to be well worth the commission. I sincerely hope they maintain their best quality, which certainly gives them an extreme edge in the market - which is their customer support.

Jaime

author: Dr Faustus
title: Nightmare registering driving license
body:
If you try to register tour driving license and you're unlucky, the automatic system will direct you to manual registration. This resulted in my case in an endless e-mail thread with different adresses , robots and people, long phonecalls woth the customer service, wich has no direct acess to your profile or documents. They will make you shooting selfies with your passport in your hand and send you from a to b, trying to catch 22.
It is more than a week since i started the process and in one week from now I reserved a car...
I rent cars regularly for business and leisure.
This is the most difficult und user unfriendly process I ever experienced.
reply:
Hi Dr. Faustus,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear you had a less-than-perfect experience with our driver verification process. This is certainly not the experience we prefer our guests to have. I'd love the opportunity to see what happened here and ensure that you're all set on your upcoming booking; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" in the subject line. I look forward to connecting with you soon. Thanks for your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: SG
title: It was great while it lasted, but they don't care about your rig
body: I listed my van originally at $175. After some renters & reviews I increased my rate to be more comparable to similar rigs. I increased it to $200 which showed on my end. But apparently there was a glitch that kept it on the site as $175. That was brought to my attention by a renter (when I said $200 but the site said $175). So essentially outdoorsy said we can fix it now, but we don't care you lost out on hundreds of dollars at this point. Then because if the glitch these renters canceled the rental losing me an additional $780. I'll keep my future rentals to not impact booked renters, but once I honor those i'll be leaving the site. Outdoorsy doesn't care about you or your rig at all. As long as they take their cut, which is sizable. Also if you need to charge someone for issues out of YOUR security deposit, they charge a 3% fee. I won't be using their platform moving forward.
reply:

I'm so terribly sorry to hear you've had this experience. This is most definitely not the outcome we'd prefer our hosts to have. The percentage we get from your bookings enables us to keep doing the things we do to help you get your RV rented safely, quickly, and securely. These include things like marketing costs, technology infrastructure, trust and safety systems, payment processing, 24/7 customer support, etc. Please rest assured that I'll share your feedback with our teams. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.

author: Scott Federman
title: Not good for RV owners
body: The app does not work well. They give my location without someone renting. Customer service can take a long time and often they do not understand me. It seems to be a language barrier. Worst of all is that I canceled a renter the day before his rental and Outdoorsy placed a negative review on me. There were circumstances that caused the cancelation. As a business partner I was surprised that they choose to give me a bad review. I called them to discuss it and was told that it is automatically generated by the computer, and they cannot do anything about it. This might be the beginning of AI taking over. They also charge a service fee to the owner to collect fees like fuel and things that are missing. I recommend using a different platform to rent out an RV.
reply:
Hi Scott,

I'm sorry to hear your experience with our service didn't match your expectations. This is certainly not the experience we prefer our members to have. Please allow me to clarify that an automated review noting a cancellation will not affect the host's overall rating. A host can respond to the review if they wish to clarify the cancellation. I'll personally be reaching out to you soon to provide you with more information regarding our cancellation policy and to learn more about your experience with our mobile app. Thank you for sharing your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

The UI / UX is fundamentally flawed. Want to just see how much money you are taking in as rental versus add ons? Good luck. Want to find your latest booking quickly? Good luck. Want to be able to login without having to two factor authenticate every hour or so? No chance.

The support has been decent but holy crap are you paying for it.

author: Shane H
title: Don't rent from Outdoorsy
body: Don't rent from Outdoorsy.  The trailer we rented had nothing but issues and broke down during our trip due to a mechanical failure, putting our safety at risk.  Outdoorsy sided with the host for damages.  We had to go through Fairclaims (small claims) to prove and win our dispute.  We chose not to write a host review pending the outcome of the dispute. By the time the dispute was resolved it was too late to submit a review.  Customer support said they would submit the review on our behalf but after discussing it with their "offline team" decided not to submit a negative review that might cost them and the host revenue.  It's not worth renting vehicles that you dont know the maintenance history of or the fact that Outdoorsy sides with the hosts and won't allow submitting of negative reviews. Don't risk your safety by renting from this company.  This company values profit over the safety of its customers.
reply:
Hi Shane,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear you had a less-than-perfect experience with your rented RV. That's definitely not the experience we prefer our guests to have. Our guest's safety and comfort are our top priority, and I'd like to reassure you that we have processes in place to ensure that our hosts are providing well-maintained vehicles. Although the option to leave a trip review is only available within 14 days after the booking is completed, please rest assured that I'll share your feedback with our Marketplace Quality team so we can review the host's listing and ensure that we're always providing the best product possible. Additionally, we'll investigate to see what happened to your dispute and will get in touch with you soon to address your concerns. Thank you for taking a moment to let us know about your experience.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

title: Great platform to rent, but has it's flaws.
body: While I love how easy it is to book my RV for others to rent, as well as renting RV's myself, the only big complaint I have is the Outdoorsy fees.  They are huge fees that do not justify what they are needed for.  The fee should be less than 3% in my opinion as you are only needing it to keep the website up and running and provide the partnership with Roamly or other insurance carriers.  The huge fee's being passed on to the renter is not justified and has discouraged people from renting out their RV's on Outdoorsy.  I know of a few that have decided to rent it out themselves because of these fees.  I have considered doing the same but I'm hoping Outdoorsy will listen to us and lower their fees and stop taking so much from us.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Hans Royal
title: Absolutely horrible- READ MY REVIEW AND BEWARE
body:
Absolutely horrible.   Someone did significant damage to my RV, AND Here's why Outdoorsy denied my claim, and I WON'T BE REIMBURSED BY THE RENTER FOR OVER $5,500 WORTH OF DAMAGE"

I even used Outdoorsy's service to review the listing when I made it, and they LOVED THE LANGUAGE I USED. THEY EVEN RECOMMENDED IT!!!! 

"Hi there,

I did review all of the information you have sent in with two supervisors.

After reviewing the evidence, I do see that there was a discussion in regards to the road. Thank you for that. I do notice that you have a subtitle on your Outdoorsy page that says ‘go where no RV’s can go’” It could suggest “off roading inadvertently.

Unfortunately, at this time, the denial will stand.
I do apologize for any inconvenience this may cause." 

SO APPARENTLY, RENTERS ARE ALLOWED TO TAKE MY RV OFFROAD BECAUSE THIS WAS INADVERTANTLY IMPLIED. TOUGH LUCK!!!!

DO NOT WORK WITH OUTDOORSY.  THEIR POLICIES PUT YOU AT 100% RISK OF A RENTER DESTROYING YOUR VEHICLE ON A TECHNICALITY.

INSANE POLICY!!!!
reply:
Hi Hans,

I'm so sorry to hear this has been your experience. I'll be reviewing this on my end and reaching out to you directly. For further discussion regarding your damage claim, you'll need to continue to reach out to your adjuster in the email thread that you're currently communicating. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Nick Howell

author: Jeff
title: Hidden fees to hosts are not transparent
body:
1) Platform fee increased from 20% to 25% for hosts.
2) Misc host fees are not transparent.  Inexplicable difference between expected payout and payout received on two of my reservations.
reply:
Hi Jeff,

I'm very sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our fee policy. We strive to keep our fees as low as possible while remaining competitive within the market. The percentage we get from your bookings enables us to keep doing the things we do to help you get your RV rented safely, quickly, and securely. These include things like marketing costs, technology infrastructure, trust and safety systems, payment processing, 24/7 customer support, etc. Please rest assured that I'll pass your feedback along to my team to make sure that your voice is heard, and we'll get in touch with you soon to address your concerns about your payout. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Paul and Elle Wilcox
title: Horrible customer service
body: Horrible customer service. Now they have increased their charges to 25% and downgraded their customer service exponentially. Their app is also unreliable making it difficult to complete walk throughs properly so I have to make sure to print everything out for renters. Unprofessional way of doing business. If you are an rv owner I would establish myself on another platform that cares.

Hi Paul & Ellie,

We're very sorry you're dissatisfied with our fees and your experience with our service didn't match your expectations. We understand how important it is for our hosts to be able to streamline the key exchange process, so making all our forms available digitally is definitely on our radar. Although we don't have an exact date for when this feature will roll out, please rest assured our product development team is working on this for you. Please rest assured that I'll share your feedback with our teams. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Erica  Horn
title: Convenient but takes too big of a cut
body: Convenient but takes too much of the earning. Given that the renter pays a LOT extra for insurance on top of the rental, I think the portion that Outdoorsy takes from the nightly rate total is too high.
reply:
Hi Erica, 

I'm sorry to hear you feel that our fees are too high. We strive to keep our fees as low as possible while remaining competitive within the market. The percentage we get from each booking enables us to maintain and operate a safe, high-quality platform that protects you, your vehicle, and your guests. That includes costs related to marketing, technology infrastructure, trust and safety systems, payment processing, 24/7 customer support, etc. We also provide both parties with the security of knowing that we have dedicated teams to service their claims, disputes, breakdowns, roadside assistance, driver verification, and our new Concierge Program. We've also recently added more resources for our hosts, such as the option to attend a free Listing Optimization webinar with our Customer Success Managers, a new Host Handbook, and lots of new updates we're launching this year to enhance the user experience for our hosts and guests. Please rest assured that I'll share your feedback with my team. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: WILSON
title: Great Concept! Not fair to owners
body: The platform and concept is a really great idea, however the owner experience is quite lacking.  Outdoorsy takes a heavy cut on top of their own processing fees so at the end of the day the owner earns a small fraction, despite them being the product provider.  Additionally, all the extra costs and insurance aren't marketed up front so many renters are driven away once they see the actual cost vs the marketed daily rate. Lastly, the app is buggy and errors out a lot.

author: Giorgio Navarini
title: Horrible Horrible Horrible
body:
I've been hosting on Outdoorsy for about 4 months now. I could not be more displeased with this website. Here are my complaints:
(1) the app is constantly signing me out and my cohost and asking for a special pass key.... ergo it wastes a lot of my time.
(2) my car was recently damaged and I thought Outdoorsy would be as helpful as possible considering their VERY high premiums for hosts and clients. They told me to contact the clients insurance company which created a circular disaster of about 12 phone calls none of which resulting in my damages getting fixed or insurance money... Following this, I had to settle with my client directly. I told my client to Venmo me the damages which Outdoorsy jumped on immediately and threatened to cancel my account if I did not get reimbursed through the app.... Not only that they wanted to charge me a fee for the transfer of money for my damaged vehicle. 
(3) Fast forward two weeks they ACTUALLY SUSPEND my account because I told the client I was only taking bookings through Turo as Outdoorsy needs to fix their glitches on the website and app; also I'm still waiting on damages reimbursement from Outdoorsy. (based on how much money outdoorsy brings in this should not be a difficult process).   
(4) Just to give you context of how much Outdoorsy charges a client and how much they pay a host: my last potential client was going to pay $1330 for the reservation out of which I was only going to get $770... That's an enormous premium for low quality performance. 

AS OF NOW, I'M STILL WAITING TO RECEIVE MONEY FOR DAMAGES. AND they have the audacity to suspend my account for just telling a client I'm only taking bookings through another app. There must be some law against this.
reply:
Hi Giorgio,

I'm so sorry to hear you've had a poor hosting experience due to app issues, a damage claim, and the suspension of your account. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our hosts to have. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share this feedback and bring this to my attention. I'll be investigating this thoroughly on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly to discuss this further. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Otis
title: Cool experience for renter, but not worth hosting.
body: Cool experience for renters, but not for owners. Their fees are exorbitant. We recently rented our camper for $500 for a 5 day period (our total including cleaning and generator rental). By the time the renter got to checkout, it was $700 (required insurance, taxes and fees.) At this point Outdoorsy has jacked up our rental fee by easily 30-40%. Of course that's not enough...they take 30% of our rental fee too. By the time it's all over, we saw basically see 55-60% of what the renter paid, which is frankly ridiculous given the amount of time required for hand offs and the fact that we own the infrastructure. These added fees seem to be killing our occupancy too... we're one of the lowest cost, and one of the only vintage campers in our area, and despite excellent reviews, we've seen a huge slow-down this summer.
reply:
Hi Otis,

Please allow me to clarify that our host fee is only 20% to 25%. We do our best to keep our fees to a minimum while remaining competitive within the market. The percentage we get from each booking enables us to keep doing the things we do to help you get your RV rented safely, quickly, and securely. These include things like marketing costs, technology infrastructure, trust and safety systems, payment processing, 24/7 customer support, etc. I'll get in touch with you soon to discuss our fee policy in full detail and answer any questions you may have.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Ray McDonough
title: Tried to help family that had a last…
body:
Tried to help family that had a last minute request because their trailer rental was cancelled without notice.
They tried to book thru outdoorsy but outdoorsy refused last minute rentals. They were a couple with small child at a campsite without any sleeping arrangements.
They pleaded with us to help and was forced to help off-site as site would not work for same day rentals.
Now outdoorsy sends me a threatening letter and suspends me from site.
Would not recommend a company that deals in this manner without giving options of their own for same day rentals.
reply:
Hi Ray,

I'm terribly sorry to hear you encountered this issue when trying to approve a same-day booking request. It's certainly not our general intent to prevent guests from booking last minute; your listing settings dictate whether a guest can book a same-day reservation. With regard to account suspension, our Terms of Service stipulate that all requests that originate on the platform must be transacted on the platform. Our support team is always available to help when you need assistance with your listing or bookings. I'll be investigating this further on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly to discuss this in greater detail. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback and bring this to my attention.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: FL from SC PA
title: Watch your reviews and communicate quickly if there is an error.
body: UPDATE:  As can happen with technology - a problem can arise that was unintended and that appears to be what happened to me.  For that reason, I am amending my comments because while the system is flawed - the customer service team responded professionally, acknowledged a system flaw, and confirmed that it shouldn't have happened - which is GREAT.  Mistakes happen, and that is understandable. I feel confident that my issue was a glitch and for that reason, and the great communication with Outoorsey, I am revising my star rating from 1 to 4.  PRIOR REVIEW:  I saw an "automated review" on my account today that said, "Faith Lucchesi canceled reservation two days before our trip. Automated review."  While true in part, the guest initiated the cancellation, and to be kind, I AGREED TO REFUND ALL FEES that would have been due because of the cancellation policy. By agreeing to assist a renter, Outdoorsy's automated system told all other renters that I canceled someone's trip without the added fact that I did it at their request and agreed to refund all monies despite the cancellation policy. When I saw the review, many months after the harm was done, I called to discuss the matter, and the review was removed, but the damage was done. I was seriously misportrayed by the platform for more than six months, without my knowledge -and it was THEIR automatic response to the situation that published the review. NOT the guest, not me as host. And it was a lie as stated. The corporate decision to punish hosts who help guests is a shockingly myopic business decision and others should know this when reading reviews or considering becoming a host.
reply:
Hi Faith,

I'm so sorry to hear you were penalized due to a mutual cancellation. This is most definitely not what should've happened, and I'll be investigating on my end to find out why this occurred. We absolutely don't intend to punish hosts for providing their guests with a full refund, so your review is quite concerning to me. Your time is valuable, so thank you so much for sharing your feedback and bringing this to my attention. I'll be reaching out to you directly to provide insight on how this unfortunate situation may be prevented in the future. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Curt  Marcott
title: Process made simple by not worrying on the front end.
body:
It makes the process very easy when Outdoorsy handles all the financial and contractual requirements to safely rent your RV.  It’s hard enough managing the care, setup, delivery and pickup of the RV.  There’s also the personal side of the delivery and pickup with meeting all the people I host.
Being able to not have to worry on the front end makes it much more simple to manage the back end of the experience.

author: Rebecca May
title: Very helpful staff
body: Very helpful staff. They are very prompt in calling me when I request a call for assistance.

author: Ashley Cooper
title: Great until something goes wrong
body: Our RV was rented through outdoorsy and the renter drove it until it overheated and left them stranded.  Before this happened I reported to the renter and outdoorsy the fact that the renter was continuing to drive it after I told him to stop at a repair shop.  The vehicle is still in another state and outdoorsy has not helped at all in recovering my vehicle.  They stated the renter is responsible.  The renter has refused to return the vehicle or pay for needed repairs.  I'm going to be stuck paying for all of this with no support from the company that takes 30% of all profit on rentals.

I'm so sorry to hear that your RV sustained some damage and you've had a poor experience with our claims process. I absolutely understand how frustrated you must be, so thank you for taking a moment to provide your feedback and bring this to my attention. While most claims are processed within 7 days, in some cases it may take longer. I'll be investigating further on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you again for making me aware of the issues you're experiencing. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

title: Great site for Camper and Rv rentals…

author: Lou Lindsey
title: Constant cancellations
body:
In May we needed an RV dropped at the NASCAR races in Ft Worth. We originally went to RVShare. We were cancelled on 4 times - the last one was 1 week before the race!!!  We had already bought our tickets. We had already paid for our Parking Spot our camping spot. I was furious!!  So then we went to Outdoorsy - 2 people cancelled on us and then We got one that he said I’m so sorry y’all went through all this, don’t worry I will not be canceling on you. I got you. I’ve never dropped an RV at the Texas Motor Speedway, but don’t worry I got it. Well on Sunday night he was going through the rules to bring the RV in and I don’t know what happened but he said he just couldn’t do it. And he canceled on us!!!  We had four days before we were leaving for the races. 

So then I found Triple w Rentals. Jaden had called me when we had already gotten the guy at Outdoorsy so I told him we didn’t need him. He said if you do, here’s my cell phone number. Call me. Well lo and behold The guy canceled on us so I called him back. Jaden called me back right away. He could not have been nicer. He was so. understanding of what we went through and how stressed I was. He was doing everything he could to let us know that we had nothing to worry about. They had an RV left that they could bring to the track. It wasn’t the best one they had but it fit our four grandsons, my husband and I. They got there on Friday morning. Got everything set up. They were so kind. Randy was so nice and did not get bothered at all with my many many texts back-and-forth. 

Every time I got a call or a text from them, my heart just sank I was immediately thinking they’re gonna cancel on this too. But they did not - they were so extremely helpful so extremely accommodating. 

Whenever we need an RV again, we are definitely coming back to Triple W. They will go anywhere and bring an RV to wherever you need it to be. If there’s any problems at all, they are on it. They will get it fixed. Things happen they just do, but they were just so kind and accommodating. I cannot stress enough how wonderful this company is. With the other two places you are dependent on independent owners of these RV’s and they can back out in a second as we found out over and over and over again.  And we get charged for booking with them!    Yes they charged as for the privilege of being canceled on.😳. 

But with Triple W they own so many RVs. They have plenty for everyone and you are not dependent on a personal owner. They have some beautiful, beautiful campers and RVs. Tell them Miss Lou sent you. ❤️🙏🏼🦋

author: Martin Dolan
title: Inferior parts, unmaintained, unsafe RV suspension (snapped leaf spring); ZERO financial recovery for parts and labor
body:
I rented an RV from an owner in IL who placed his previously damaged RV into RVShare inventory.  One of his leaf springs (single axle) (4 layers thick) previously failed and was replaced with a more robust 3 layer spring (of which the owner denied, "that he bought the 2022 RV brand new).  Upon rental, the other, original spring failed on me, the 4th and longest spring snapped, shredding the tire in the wheel well.  I immediately called Customer Service because I was in a remote area, on the Emergency shoulder of the road, just inside the main gate of a military compound.  Since the base is on a higher threat level of protection they supplied the approved towing company and mechanics who are cleared to tow me to my designated spot and make repairs once parts are secured (new pair of matching springs and tires); to keep all receipts (parts and labor) for reimbursement.  I did so.  All parts were purchased, trained mechanics replaced both springs, mounted, balanced and torqued both tires along w/ removing all "old" grease (lost its viscosity) with fresh grease.  The best tire was used to replace the older spare.

Upon inspection, it was noticed that the failed spring was missing the whole 3rd layer on one side so there was insufficient support for the 4th layer, hence the failure.  The proof was provided in close up imagery.

After several days I further noticed water on the floor, that there was a leaky kitchen hot water line, mold everywhere under the cabinet.  I purchased plumbers teflon tape and made the correct repairs (it was never sealed properly, no tape previously existed).  I was forced to make another repair and clean up the mold so I wouldn't get sick.  Even though I was apparently breathing this for over a week.  All photos were provided and Customer Service contacted.

All receipts, all photos (35ish) were supplied to RVShare along with up to date calls to their Customer Service.  Always stating at the end of conversation, we're here to help...

After returning the RV (cleaned, systems flushed) and accepted by the owner)), I filed my request for reimbursement and was Denied because I signed a Departure Form.  "Everything appeared" to be fine, as if it's reasonable for a renter to crawl under the RV and approve the quality of the parts...  RVShare stated they have zero responsibility in this matter.

I was further informed that I can file for labor expenses, but not parts.  I denied this "offer" because it was less than the total, but primarily because, why am I responsible for maintaining and paying for repairs for something I don't even own?  All for the privilege of renting from RVShare?

Then told that I can open a Dispute (a long and time-consuming process) only to be Denied again, stating that all expenses I incurred are "outside the platform" and I need to seek remedy from the owner.  

I did so, and he states that this should be covered by RVShare or under the insurance policy I paid for.  All of the burden placed on me to have remedy.  Both avenues I requested restitution from and was further Denied, RVShare has no responsibility, seek remedy from the owner.

I shared with RVShare that I think their platform was hijacked and hoodwinked by this owner by them knowingly placing their unsafe and negligently maintained RV into their inventory, jeopardizing their reputation.  Apparently they don't care because they don't stand behind the product they promote.  This should make everyone question what their (RVShares) Participant RV Owners Terms and Conditions Contract states regarding providing a safe RV into their inventory in the first place.

Bottom Line: DO NOT RENT THROUGH RVSHARE.  If you have a problem, they simply say, you signed the Departure Form, accepted the RV "as is".  As if we as renters once again are crawling under the RV to check for an unsafe, inferior suspension system, leaky, moldy faucets, etc.  btw; water doesn't accumulate unless the sink is turned on for a a period of time.  Which is not done when you "test" whether you have running water when you pick up the RV on day one.

This is a RENTER BEWARE Review.  Many owners place their RV's into RVShare inventory to offset their monthly offsite storage fees without paying the necessary costs of continued maintenance.

RVShare does not share ANY responsibility and hides behind legal jargon for their protection, instead of doing what's right.  Their temporary "Insurance" is also apparently worthless.

The only way this will improve is through a loss in rental revenue because there's a boycott; we simply go elsewhere.  Talk is cheap, "we're here for you, we understand and sympathize with your situation, blah, blah, hiding behind some clause within a "Departure Form".  Since it's their Form, the T&C's (Terms & Conditions) are always in favor of the "entity" that provides it...

author: Tony Barrett
title: The experience with the rv owner was…
body:
The experience with the rv owner was great but rv share insurance is terrible.
After taking out several hundreds of dollars in insurance coverage they want me to use my personal auto insurance after I had a small claim.

author: Mary Locke
title: This might be the biggest scam going…
body: This might be the biggest scam going today. Not only did this place cancel our reservation 10 days before our trip. They also screwed us on the insurance and are now randomly charging our credit card that I cannot get off my trip Rental agreement. Not only do they not listen they are condescending and ridiculous on the phone. The owner has been awesome and he’s complaining about them as well and saying how shady they actually are. We so so regret contact this company using this company and we haven’t even gone on our trip yet. Do not I repeat do not ever ever ever rent from RV share. It’s a scam.

author: Rob T
title: RV Share and Owner Unresponsive
body: I sent a note to the owner I rented from about the height of the rv to plan my trip. After 5 days they haven’t replied. I have attempt to contact rv share to ask for help, but the customer service chat goes blank after waiting for 20 or 30 minutes to be connect to a live agent. The phone number is impossible. Not sure if I got scammed at this point.

author: K.B.
title: I made a reservation and they can't verify my ID
body: I made a reservation and was charged for the reservation. After the reservation was made I received notice that I needed to verify my ID. I didn't do it right away. I unfortunately had my wallet stolen a few days later. I tried to use a different government issued ID but was told only a drivers license was acceptable. They said only a drivers license would work for verification and that if I couldn't verify my ID to the reservation would be cancelled and I would be charged a cancellation fee. If a drivers license is required to book, why was I allowed to book without and have my CC charged?

author: Bridget King
title: Kyle was very professional and kind
body: Kyle was very professional and kind.  She answered my questions about payment.  This is my first time experiencing RV Share and I really appreciated the availability and the great response.  Kyle made my experience very comfortable for me.  Thanks
reply: Thank you, Bridget! Come back soon.

author: Lenny Sabia
title: Poor customer communication for owner…
body:
Thanks for the reply, I would not wasted anymore time with RVshare, clueless and horrible customer service.

Poor customer communication for owner of the RV, even worse from the incompetent staff at RVShare, worse experience!  Stay away from RV share

Also, the renter Carie as mentioned above was no help with the situation, RVshare, processed my cc for insurance then cancel my reservation.

body: Kyle M was so helpful and engaging. She is a wonderful asset to RVshare.

body: Unhappy with the RV rental I got through this website as well as the dispute handling by RVshare later.
