author: Erika Peacock
title: We loved our experience
body: We loved our experience! It was a bit windy up in Payson but we had a warm place to escape to after a day of fishing! Comfy bed and everything worked perfectly! Will definitely rent again from Lance! He made it all so easy. Professional and experienced. Thanks again!

author: Milo
title: Easy checkout
body: Easy checkout, updated, well-maintained R-Pod. Great communication.

author: Rebekka Golden
title: Beware this company
body: Beware this company! We rented an RV from them and were very upfront about 1 minor scratch that occurred on the first day my husband was driving the RV. We were expecting to lose our $1500 security deposit but were FLOORED when they proceeded to charge our card for double that amount to cover "repairs."  The company refused to share any repair estimate with us and charged our card without our authorization. They are not upfront in their policies and do not care about their renters.
reply: Hello Rebekka, we’re sorry to hear about your experience. Damage charges are based on a full review of the reported damage and associated costs. We’d like to look into this further for you. Please reach out to us directly or respond to any existing claim communication so our team can review the details with you.

author: Douglas
title: I booked an rv on the web site and it got canceled by rv share with no reason.
body:
I booked an rv on the web site. Communicated with steven the owner. I liked the trailer and so i submitted a booking request. Steven quickly accepted. Went to pay and during the process i chose the insurance package that worked for us. 
Clicked pay and everything was good until a email came back stating rv share needed more information. Then minutes later another email came through stating reservation was canceled. So reason just canceled.
Called customer service and they knew nothing as to why reservation was canceled.  Blamed it on the insurance company. 
I have a clean drivers record.  No felony or misdemeanor convictions ever. Only court record is of a divorce in 1989. 
I went to log back into my account to relay that the rental was canceled but rv share blocked my account. Not sure why. So i called back to cutomer service. They said they were told to block my account. Lol. Asked why and the person said he did not know why. 
I asked for a supervor manager to call me on monday. Hey said he woukd put in the request. I doubt they will call. This company seems disorganized and not cutomer friendly. 
I reserved a 5th wheel from Outdoorsy and everything went smooth as silk. Makes me wonder why Outdoorsy can rent me an RV but rv share cant. Lol.
My recommendation is to not rent from rv share.

author: Kara Diffley
title: RVshare does not care for the RV owners!
body:
Ive been renting out my van on RVshare for a year now. I just listed a new vehicle, a RV, this week and the insurance got approved as of Monday. Last night on Friday I got a reservation request for that new vehicle for the day of. I talked to the renter and approved the reservation, not knowing the RV was blocked from insurance coverage due to needing a document signed that I have never seen. RVshare never emailed me or warned me of this missing document before approving the reservation. I called RVshare immediately for assistance and I could not get the form signed in time for the reservation so I had to cancel the reservation that I made just 15 minutes before and they forced me to pay $165 to cancel the reservation that shouldn’t of been available in the first place. RVshare obviously does not care for the RV owners that use their platform and make them money. I will no longer be renting out my vehicles on RVshare from now on. 

The next Monday, I did receive an email from RVshare retracting the cancellation fee so I do appreciate that. I hope they can prevent this from happening to anyone else in the future.
reply: Hello Kara, we’re sorry for the frustration this caused. This isn’t the experience we want for our owners. To activate coverage, we do need certain documents (like registration and/or a signed consignment form), and it looks like these were still pending at the time of booking. That said, we understand how this led to a difficult situation with a same-day reservation. Our team has reached out. Please follow up via email so we can finalize this with you. We appreciate your feedback and your time hosting on RVshare.

author: Distressed Tired Family
title: $375 prep fee for a dirty RV and no support from the company
body: We were charged a $375 prep fee by the owner. Upon pickup, it was impossible to see how disgusting the RV really was. Urine around the base of the toilet. Something nasty on the shower floor (under a rug at pickup). Fridge and pans with deep dirt and/or mold. Because the individual owner was talking so poorly of other renters, we didn't want to raise concerns until after the trip. We spent hours cleaning before we could even load things. I talked to a person at customer service, and she seemed disgusted. She said it would be wise to wait until the return form had been fully completed. We did. We provided dated evidence of all the things, and we received nothing back stating we agreed to it at pick-up state. They offered us a discount code. I can honestly say we will never use this company again and will go with a company that doesn't have individual owners playing games.
reply: Hi, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Randy Mckissick
title: We took a trip up to Oklahoma from…
body: We took a trip up to Oklahoma from Texas to Cherokee Landing State Park at Lake Tenkiller. Not only was the park and the weather perfect for the weekend, but our RV was the icing on the cake! Our hosts, Haley and Chad have got this RV hosting thing figured out! Our RV was the most comfortable we've ever stayed in. Well maintained and immaculately clean, we didn't need Anything! They thought of Everything! We will definitely go thru them again! Booking was so easy, but the communication was Top Notch, we had questions....They had quick answers
reply: Thank you, Randy! We love to hear this!

author: Danielle
title: Easy to work with
body: Easy to work with. Delivered when promised.

author: Diana Pflug
title: VERY DECEPTIVE and charges will be significantly more than what is shown at confirmation.
body: RV Share is very deceptive and not up front about what the total cost amount is. Basically whatever the rental cost is, double it to equal the "required insurance" fees and service fees. This is not disclosed in the upfront amount but said in a very nondescript way at the bottom of the reservation confirmation email that unless this "required" insurance is paid, in a separate charge, the reservation will be canceled. These extra fees are charged whether you're having the RV delivered and parked (not driving it) or driving the wheels off of it. But wait, that's not all......when we decided to cancel because the amount it costs to rent a camper is the same as renting an entire brand new 4 bedroom lake house on the very same lake, you won't get a full refund even though we made the reservation 3 weeks ago for 6 MONTHS from now. They will keep the service fees for all your time, effort and frustration that they have caused you when they nearly double what you thought was the total cost was going to be. Thumbs down. Do not recommend. Way too many additional and required charges to sleep in a camper for a few nights. You can literally get a 3 bedroom ocean front condo on a beach or 4  bedroom lake house for what this website wants to sneak in as "required". Very disappointed. Would never recommend even to someone I don't like that much.
reply: Hello Diana, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

author: Jake
title: Verify all measurements
body: For the most part, everything went well with the rv rental.  The only issue was the height measurement of the toy-hauler loading door.  The website description/measurement was actually one inch higher than the actual door header height.  One inch may not seem like a lot but it does matter if you are loading an off road vehicle.  Friendly suggestion is to verify all measurements before paying a deposit, as I could not get a refund, even though the website measurement was incorrect.
reply: Hello Jake, we’re sorry about the discrepancy in the door height. While RV owners are responsible for listing details, we understand how important accurate measurements are, especially in situations like yours. We’ll share this feedback with our team to help improve accuracy moving forward.

author: Phill
title: Sharing my great experience with Kyle. Ambasador of the RVShare brand.
body:
I would like to share my great experience with Kyle with the RVShare leadership team. 

This is our first time renting a travel trailer and I had questions after I booked the reservation. Kyle did an outstanding job patiently walking me through the answers to all my questions and details of the program in a way that was easy for me to understand. 

Your decision to keep a direct phone number and ability to talk to a real person is a company differentiator. Keep that as part of your brand promise. 

I will most likely rent again from RVShare as a result of my positive experience with Kyle.  Hopefully when it's time we can work with Kyle again. Kyle is an ambassador of the RVShare brand. 

Thank you Kyle. 
Phill
reply: Awesome to hear, Phill! Thank you!

author: Ken
title: Trailer Experience
body:
Trailer Experience. 4*
RV Share 1*

I am not happy with how RV share handled our claim for the damage. They get a 1 Star. The claim was put in and I requested an itemized list of what was damaged and how I was charged for it. I was told that they only provide that to the owner. A little frustrating to be paying money for something without know what and why. The owner was nice enough to share the information with me. The summary only gave a price for parts and for labor, no detailed list on how they got that amount. Not a good system for openness about the money I am charged. I will look for other option for renting an RV rather than using RV share.

The RV and communication was good. Had a trailer driver side blowout on the return trip and found that the lug nut wrench was too flimsy to change a tire. Noted that 5 months earlier a renter had the same issue on the other side, not sure about the tires. The owners did mention the possibility of using a drill but did not have one to use. Luckily a Good Samaritan stopped to assist, otherwise we would have been there for a while awaiting assistance from roadside assistance.

I was able to pull the trailer with my 4runner, which is what I wanted. A little crowded my family of 5, but that is what I get for wanting to pull with my vehicle. The outside fridge and the freezer worked great. The inside fridge was not cool enough and I should have adjusted the setting but didn't. The AC worked well.
reply: Hello Ken, we apologize for the experience you faced with the damage claim. We can certainly put you back in contact with your adjuster if needed. Please give us a call at 1-888-482-0234 for further review.

author: HAvenue Productions
title: RV Owners Stay AWAY
body:
We were owners of an RV listed on RVshare. The first year was great, until a renter wrecked our RV and caused $18,000 in damage. Renters are required to purchase insurance through RVshare when they rent, which is supposed to give owners peace of mind. Our renters did purchase insurance, but it came with a $4,000 deductible. The renters then canceled their credit card and have been impossible to get ahold of.

RVshare told us it was up to us to collect the deductible from the renters, and they offered no help at all. We are now without our RV to rent for income or to use ourselves, and we are stuck dealing with the financial loss.

Based on our experience, we do not believe RVshare is a trustworthy business. Owners should be aware of the risks before listing their RV.

If you want, I can also rewrite it to sound more formal, more aggressive, or more like a short review.

author: Mary McIntyre
title: Terrible policies
body: Terrible policies.  Rented an RV for Easter weekend months in advance and was contacted the day before to let me know that the RV had a mechanical issue and could not be rented.  This isn’t RV Share’s fault but when they refunded me for the rental, they kept $25 for a non-refundable screening fee and refused to refund it.
reply:
Hello Mary, we’re very sorry your trip was impacted, especially after planning so far in advance.

While the cancellation was due to a mechanical issue outside of our control, we understand your frustration with the screening fee. Please give us a call with your reservation details, and we’d be happy to take another look and see how we can help.

author: Ron Stephenson
title: RV Share Customer Service is a JOKE
body: The guy we rented the RV from was terrific, but the company itself is a joke. We paid $1000 for insurance and roadside assistance, and ended up needing roadside assistance. We called and asked for a tow to be dispatched at 7 pm. We were obviously talking to a call service in another country. We ended up had paying an extra $135 for the tow because it would be over 100 miles. The next morning we called twice to inquire about the ETA of the tow,and both times were told 45 minutes. We finally got the name of the towing company and called them ourselves and found out they had never been dispatched. The tow arrived in 30 minutes, the guy was able to get us started again and on our way. I doubt very much we will ever get reimbursed for the extra money we paid out. We will never use RV share again.
reply: Hello Ron, we’re really sorry to hear about your experience—this is not the level of service we aim to provide, especially in a situation where you needed timely roadside assistance. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Chris
title: Booked an RV for delivery within the…
body: Booked an RV for delivery within the given radius for the area I searched for. Owner of RV said he was unable to bring the RV to the requested campground even though it was within the radius.  The kicker is it auto booked the RV and charged my card so when I went to cancel the RV because of the owners fault they wanted to charge me a cancellation fee.  I contacted RVshare and they did not care it was not my fault and would not give me back the fee. RVshare only cares about money and will not take care of customers that have an issue. Avoid at all cost, much better trust worthy companys out there.
reply: Hi Chris, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Valued Customer
title: Neil B was so helpful
body: Neil B was so helpful. I am older and don't have a computer. Unfortunately I had to cancel and he was very understanding of my situation. He is an excellent employee

author: Jennifer Green
title: RV Share made finding an RV that was in…
body:
RV Share made finding an RV that was in great shape, with a nice, responsible landlord/host to rent it from easy. 
This was our first RV experience, we rented an RV to stay at the Sherwood Forest Faire campgrounds two nights, and it went very well. Jesse our host was organized and patient, and his RV was clean, well maintained, well stocked, and Jesse delivered/picked it up from the campgrounds for a fee. 
And once I saw how much effort and time was involved for delivery and set up, I knew those fees were well worth it! I would use RV Share again, and I would rent again from Jesse in the future!
reply: Thank you, Jennifer. Come back soon!

author: stephanie creel
title: Joy was very helpful !!
body: Joy was very helpful !!! Thank you !

author: Jennifer Fullen
title: Joy was an absolute joy to have been…
body:
Joy was an absolute joy to have been connected with, great customer service rep, 
very polite and got the issue resolved in no time. 
Thank you Joy!
reply: We appreciate your kind words, Jennifer.

title: Joy C was such a help and a pleasure to…

title: Incremental fees!
body: The camper itself was outstanding along with the owner.   It’s the hidden fees that makes no sense to rent from RVshare.  Disappointed that the fees were close to the rental amount.
reply: Hello, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

author: Tracey Lynch Lincoln
title: Reimbursement nightmare
body: As an owner of an RV who rented through RV share they are absolutely horrible when there is damage done to your unit and you need to get reimbursement! We had a renter total our RV in RV share has been battling us for almost a year now trying to give us $40,000 less than the RV is worth. We even were willing to meet in the middle of that 40,000, got a lawyer and they are still standing firm.  Their customer service is horrible, they take forever to get back to you, and do not back the owners of the RV at all. They also sent us a 1099 for almost triple what they actually paid us which we have proof of and had to fight with them to get that corrected. If you own an RV and I thinking about renting it out use a different platform.!!!!!
reply: Hi Tracey, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Soph OHara
title: Do NOT list your camper or camper van on RVShare!!
body:
RVShare as a van owner has proven to be a complete nightmare. 

I had my camper van listed for 8 months - with zero interest - while outdoorsy and RVezy gave me consistent listings. 

I finally had someone book through there and after my. An had been listed for 8 months with ZERO communication - it was after my van was booked that they sent me an email with a 7 day deadline to provide proof of a recall repair. 

They have no clue how anything works. First - they only sent an email (I get 309 emails a day). They have my number to text. 

Second - they gave me 7 days to provide proof of a repair. My van has been in the shop since DECEMBER 1st!! I’m still waiting on parts and approvals for the work. 

They can’t even tell me SPECIFICALLY the work they are requiring proof of. When I pushed on that - then they wanted the list of maintenance items I was having them work on. 

I’ve had them completely remove my RV from their site. 

I recommend everyone do the same. They are scammers. 

Oh yeah. They were hacked and I received dozens of emails of scammers. Took them for ever to plug the leak.  Then their customer service folks all deny it. 

Check out Reddit
reply: Hi Soph, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Reasonable Shopper
title: very disappointed
body: I rented a stationary rv.  The owner told me I would not have to pay the insurance fee.  He was wrong.  RVshare would not honor the owner's committment and I ended up cancelling and having to pay the $135.15 service fee anyway, since insurance through RVshare is MANDATORY under ANY circumstances.
reply: We want to make sure you are still protected even if you are not driving the unit. We require insurance for stationary rentals because accidents still can happen when the RV doesn't move and RVs are expensive assets. Our protection plans will cover the unit in case of theft, vandalism, fire, flood, lightning, explosions, earthquakes, storms, hail, acts of God, etc.

author: Paul Black
title: Hidden fees and dishonest practices
body: Tried to reserve an RV through their website, but the process was misleading. After submitting my reservation, I was told it needed approval, then prompted to pay for additional insurance that wasn’t included in the original quote. By the time I called to ask about the unexpected charge, they said the reservation had been approved and it was “past 24 hours,” so canceling cost me a $65 fee—even though approval happened almost a full day after I booked, giving me no real chance to clarify anything. Canceled because I didn’t trust the hidden fees. Still fighting to get my $65 back.
reply: Hello Paul, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

author: Keith Brand
title: Complete B.S!!!   Owner cancelled, not refunding
body:
Complete B.S!!!

We booked in Oct 25 for a Feb 2026 trip.   This was going to be our first RV experience, and was very much looking forward to.

4 weeks before our trip we got a message from the RV owner the camper was damaged and will be out of commission for our dates.   Super bummed but I get it, this is not where i have the issue.

RV share provided refunds.....but here is the issue.....they WILL NOT REFUND the screening fee.  

I completely understand if WE cancelled the reservation we wouldn't get it back.   But 'WE' didn't cancel the reservation.  If nothing else the OWNER should absorb the charge.   

We tried to reason w RV share but they won't budge.   So now I need to find a hotel, on top of the charge from RV share.

For $26 they lost a customer for life. So stupid.

EDIT;   I see RV shares response....but we called the customer support number many times and was told very directly 'will not refund'.
reply: Hi Keith, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Nancy Bargen
title: RVshare has been hacked several times
body: RVshare has been hacked several times. They continue to deny that they have been hacked. We have had to change our bank account to make sure that it is secure. So, if you make waves you will be kicked from their site. I would love to provide the exact date but they kicked me from the site because I requested follow-up and insurances that this will not happened again. It happened 3 times in 8 week time frame.

author: Dawn Palazzo
title: On December 26th
body: On December 26th, an RV was to be delivered to my home at 12 PM. At 12:50 we contacted the owner to see if he was lost. We received no response so called RV share which told us our reservation was canceled at 12:55 PM. Five minutes after we contacted the owner. They tried to tell us the rv was damaged but was a little coincidental that the RV was not reported damaged and trip was not cancelled until an hour after arrival time and after we tried to contact owner. They took our deposit and promised they would get us a new RV to our home that day. They have a worry, free guarantee which is absolutely a joke. They said they called around to find RVs that were available and sent us a list of four. They said they personally called each owner to ensure they were able to accommodate us. When we contacted the owners, they were not available. The only one available was one without heat. I contacted them at least 10 times that day to tell them about the urgency of the situation. Told me several times a manager will call me with no response and no call back. We are out our money for 7 tickets to Buffalo Bills football game, $350 for  an RV spot at the stadium, supplies and above all a Christmas gift for my kids that they were looking forward to. RV share is not reliable and customer service is terrible. A Christmas present that was supposed to be memorable turned into a nightmare . Please don’t use this company .

author: Tim Williston
title: I would NOT recommend this company if…
body: I would NOT recommend this company if you are a citizen of any country that is not US. -- My wife and I are Canadians. Our Canadian international Drivers  License's were approved by RVshare Insurance. We made our down payment to secure our reservation which RVshare processed. We made many non refundable reservations at campgrounds & attractions in Colorado. 3 days before our final payment payment was due, RVshare told us that we required a US drivers license. We tried to explain that as Canadian citizens, it was impossible to get approved for a US drivers license,, to no avail. We lost several hundred dollars for these non-refundable reservations. We again tried to explain this to RVshare who basically said "to bad-go away"' so our RV reservation was cancelled by RV share with no compensation for our $$losses.

author: Meg Meg
title: Worst rental experience of my life
body: Worst rental experience of my life and I have rented 30 plus times.They bait and switch you. They get all your data, then start telling you there are more expenses and fees, more documents to fill out, more pictures to send. We spent almost 30 hours trying to get it finalized. They already had are credit card and personal data. The cancellation fees are almost the entire cost, so I kept trying to give them what they wanted. You can't use a computer or android phone to send them data. After almost 10 phone calls including 2 to a supervisor and about 20 emails and texts. They then wanted more photos saying are faces weren't clear. These are full facials and ID's sent to Asia, but they refuse to say where. Kept doing it, but it wasn't worth it. Granted the couple who owned the RV were great, the RV was not clean and the stove and awning didn't work. Would never recommend anyone use RVshare. And we are in the US. Their HQ's is an office in a strip mall in Akton OH. Not a real US company and who know what they do with your data. I got a new credit card after the trip.

author: Jack A Lope
title: Broken RV, no compensation for it
body: Basically, someone can rent you a broken RV and you get fully charged for it with no recourse other than to sue the owner directly. This platform does not protect its renters, only the RV owners. First year I used this had a positive experience. 2nd year I used it the owner rented us an RV that had a multitude of broken parts, due to one of which we were not able to use the water for two days. When we disputed everything including loss of use RV share said their terms don't allow them to reimburse for this. RV Share got fees for the RV, the owner made their money, we got screwed.

title: Kyle really represented your company…
body:
Kyle really represented your company extremely well. They showed concern were not in a rush, very helpful, and I was very anxious as an rv. Rental, I had since June and the trip was in about a month. Canceled on me when I told them the truth that there would be two people staying instead of one.
Kyle show concern and walked me through everything.You are very lucky to have somebody like that representing your company

author: trina stanfield
title: I rented a van through RVshare (DeVan…
body:
I rented a van through RVshare (DeVan Rentals) for a long-planned camping trip near Beaver, Utah—an area close to 10,000 feet in elevation. What I wasn’t told until after picking up the van was that it was a retired Amazon delivery van, which absolutely matters when you’re headed into high mountain terrain. 
The van didn’t even start when I went to pick it up. The owner was out of town and never met me in person. Instead, he filled out the check-in form on my behalf before I even arrived, and had to call roadside assistance just to get the van running. That should have been the first red flag.
I drove the van home and, less than 30 minutes later, it stalled again at my community gate. When I contacted the owner, he told me that was "normal" behavior for Amazon vans. That was the first time he ever mentioned what kind of van I had rented. I was shocked—and immediately concerned for my safety.
I was a 54-year-old woman traveling alone with my 11-year-old dog. If that van had stalled in the mountains, I could have been stranded at 10,000 feet or in 100+ degree temperatures going to and from. After thinking it through for about 20 minutes, I returned the van to where I picked it up from, placed the keys back in the lockbox, and informed the owner. He agreed to refund me, minus one day over text right after that. 
Despite returning it immediately and explaining the serious safety concerns, the owner denied that he had agreed to the refund, and RVshare backed him up.
UPDATE PON 09/23/2025: I had to dispute this with my bank. They took all of the information from me and RVshare along with the owner of the van denied, denied and denied. Finally it went to a 3rd party to make a decision and they decided in my favor. Don't give up. Fight for what is right and hopefully justice will prevail.

author: Customer
title: Very disappointed
body:
Very disappointed - found a rental trailer to use for guests for a week - rental about $700 - BUT THEY ADDED ON insurance I don't need to $674 

Travel trailer that the host is delivering, parking in my driveway and leaving it for a week?  Outrageous - DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE.

author: Elizabeth D S
title: This is a 3rd party site with a high…
body: This is a 3rd party site with a high insurance price tag even when the RV is delivered. Their refund policy is terrible, no contract was ever sent however when we declined to use the site we were only partially refunded. GO DIRECTLY to owners/companies instead and skip this entire site.

author: st time renter
title: BEWARE!!!!!!
body: The rv owner was a total pleasure to work with. Very responsive. Unit was exactly as advertised.  BUT, RV Share itself lacks any transparency. A, $125 a nite,  6 nite rental quickly became $1,662.46 on the credit card. As soon as you push the button agreeing to rent, you are also automatically agreeing to all ridiculous and exorbitant costs. There is no "choice". RV Share, in a separate email, was "unable to provide" a breakdown of "protection" costs. Several other hard -to -decipher and confusing texts and emails were generated.  Never, ever again. Do yourself a favor: read all the many negative reviews before choosing this company.

author: Jennifer H.
title: UNRELIABLE COMPANY!
body:
On two separate occasions we rented an RV through RVShare.  Both times, and on very short notice, we received notification that our reservation was cancelled.  No explanation was given until I was finally able to get up with the owners.  

Since renting RVs requires reservations at campsites, our entire vacation was in jeopardy.  We were lucky to find a gracious host the first time that had found themselves in that same position at one time.  They were lovely, but RVShare was of no assistance in finding a replacement RV.

The second and most recent time the host knew of mechanical issues a week before we received the cancellation and did not reach out.  

The other issue I have with RVShare is that since I am the one that plans vacations, and the RVShare account was already in my name, I was automatically the verified driver.  Nowhere did I see that the account holder was the primary driver.  

My husband was planning on doing all of the driving and when I called RVShare to find out why I had to pay an extra $13/day to have him verified as a driver the customer service representative, reluctantly waived this policy this one time.  

Within minutes, my husband was denied as a verified driver.  And while he didn't have any traffic or criminal violations he wasn't allowed to drive the RV.  I called RVShare and was told that they couldn't tell me over the phone or by email why he was denied, which makes no sense whatsoever.  They did say I would receive a notification from the company explaining the denial, which never came.  The customer service representative was anything but cordial and friendly.

Needless to say, we didn't even bother trying to find another RV through RVShare.  I found out about Outdoorsy and they have been a pleasure to work with.

I highly recommend avoiding RVShare.  There was no apology, on their part, and no offer to assist in finding another RV.  I noticed that Outdoorsy requires a vehicle maintenance check within 90 days of any rental.  And on top of that, their prices are MUCH less than RVShare.

It's not a company I would use again or recommend to anyone else.

author: Alexia Gallegos
title: Do not protect owners
body: Do not protect owners!  Rv Share allowed a renter to keep our unit for an unapproved extra night and then not collect on the funds that was owed to us. They also did not collect on any overage charges and or damages that were done as agreed upon they do not help the owners in any way shape or form They do not take any liability. They need to gather bank account information rather than credit card information that can be easily canceled before charges happen.

author: angele
title: Every time i called for help
body: Every time i called for help, everyone went above and beyond  assistance. Especially Morgan

author: Maja Mazin
title: Horrible experience scam alert
body:
Horrible experience — RVshare insurance is a scam.
We rented an RV for Burning Man, paid $500 for cleaning and $1,200 for RVshare insurance, and returned it clean. The next day the owners accused us of ridicules  petty “damages” a missing pan we never used, a tiny chip and cushion scuff, even blaming an air freshener leaking that they had installed. We had photos and video proof, and the only issue we acknowledged upon return was a small scrape from tape used to keep the fridge shut (since it wasn’t working on pickup).
The real problem came from RVshare. A week later they hit us with $2,500 in charges without explanations for what were the damages. When we finally got through to the adjuster (who never contacted us first), we discovered the insurance is a total trap: the deductible is $1,000 for every rental, but for Burning Man it secretly doubles (which is not mentioned anywhere). We were charged in total a shocking $4,500 on top of 3900$ reservation. In practice, this means RVshare avoids paying anything and renters are expected to pay the entire bill ignoring the insurance completely.
So between dishonest owners and RVshare’s hidden policies, we paid thousands on top of cleaning and insurance that gave us zero protection.
Bottom line: RVshare enables shady owners, hides critical insurance details, and profits by leaving renters exposed. Don’t rent through them it’s not worth the risk.

author: Justin
title: DO NOT RENT YOUR CAMPER OUT HERE
body:
DO NOT RENT YOUR CAMPER OUT HERE
Things were going great, I had responsible customers who took care of my RV. But then the nightmare happened. A guy brought it back in an absolute wreck. There was minor damages, but the cleaning was insane. There are optional fees on my RV (like cleaning the black and grey water tank out - the renter can do this themselves if they wish to avoid the fee) but the Renters can disregard overages!!! I did not know this until it happened to me and RVshare has no protection for these things like insane cleaning and other overages. They pretend they do, they even let you list overages (like excessive cleaning, generator time) on your listing, but there is no guarantee that you will be paid. The renter can do these overages and then just disregard them.

author: joe travers
title: How can you possibly trust a company…
body: How can you possibly trust a company that won’t let you unsubscribe from their incessant advertising?  Obviously you cannot.

author: JM INK
title: Scam
body:
First, we rented a RV that they let them bail on us the day before the trip with zero compensation
Second after we scrambled with no help from RV share to find a second one. It was a complete piece of trash. I won’t even talk about the inside of the RV versus what the pictures look like because they had a battery in it that was completely unsafe and died on us multiple times including while driving. After showing them pictures that proved that this RV was completely unsafe for anyone to use. They refunded us no money and we have a Mechanic that will be testifying in court that this thing was completely unsafe to rent. Don’t put your family and loved ones at risk. There are a bunch of other companies that rent RVs do not go through these people

author: George Nakhla
title: Jesse helped me she did great job

She done the job and completed with great success. I’m telling you Jessy is the best for the entire RV share company God bless.

title: I would not do it again!
body: The RV was in very bad condition and did not reflect the description at all. Old tires, broken ceiling window, full & clogged black water tank, lots of personal clutter, every surface was sticky... just to name a few. What was most disappointing - RV Share did not even honour us with an answer to our complaint - so they obviously do not care about customer satisfaction at all. I would definitely not rent again with RV Share.

author: A.S.
title: RVshare rips you off by adding insurance you rejected
body: While the RV owner was a pleasure to deal with, RVshare chose to rip me off with additional charges for insurance I did not ask for specifically. Literally rejected the coverage, got a confirmation email, and they charged me more anyway. They refuse to refund the overcharge.

author: LyndaS DeRemer
title: Large non-refundable service fee.  Renter BEWARE!
body: I had rented an RV from RV Share for a week in June of 2025 and paid my money.  They contact me two weeks before my rental date and state the RV was double booked, so my reservation had to be changed.  I changed it to a date in September that I wasn't sure would work and I had a conflict so had to cancel the reservation.  They charged me $166.08 for a service fee that is non-refundable.  I don't remember reading about a non-refundable service fee.  Very disappointed.  DO NOT rent from these people.

author: Matt Ziebart
title: Ruined our family vacation
body:
I paid extra for RVshare’s cancellation protection insurance to cover us if anything went wrong. Despite this, RVshare canceled our RV rental at the last minute—after we had taken time off work and planned our entire family vacation. By the time they notified us, it was impossible to find another RV rental.

RVshare claimed the owner canceled the trip. I asked for compensation as promised in the cancellation coverage policy. Instead, I spent hours on hold with their overseas customer service, was bounced around, hung up on, and called back—only to be offered a pathetic $25 promo code for my next trip.

RVshare customer service is a nightmare: an endless phone tree loop, nearly impossible to reach a real person. This feels like an online company with almost no real staff. If you’re looking for a reliable RV rental, avoid RVshare—your trip, time, and money are too important to risk on a company that doesn’t honor its own cancellation policy.

author: Joel Dyer
title: Used them for a third time in 2025
body: Used them for a third time in 2025. One time each year for a week. There are offers for delivery, pickup and dump service if you dry camp for a week like us with 3 grandkids. The trailer we rented this year was horrible. The owner had there original new rv photos on the site. The unit was a filthy mess. Had no choice but to put up with it as we traveled from Oregon to California. RVShare does not monitor or control the shape of rv is in. Will not use them again.

author: Sara
title: Never Again
body: I've rented my camper for 2 years on RV Share but I'm done. Most renters were great, left great reviews, never had problems.  However, I recently had a renter book 2 months in advance of Sturgis..which is typically booked out a year in advance but we had our camper unlisted for a while and out it back up.  He (Friday night) reported the a/c didn't work and I told him I'd have someone come out but it would probably be Monday.  Sunday evening he texts me thst the camper is absolutely filthy, theres dust on a shelf, the stove had grease on it, and the blinds are bent.  Blinds get bent, I'm not replacing them because 1 little blind is bent.  He's had it for 3 days after I cleaned it and suddenly it's filthy and it's my fault?  He demands I fix it or retrieve it so, seeing that this guy is going to be a nightmare, I opt for the latter and refund his unused days. I called RVSHARE and that was their suggestion. For a rental that should be non-refundable.  Now he's disputing having to pay at all.  For the 3 days he slept in it and got it dirty and for the (2) 4hr trips we had to make to deliver and pick it up.  I'm done.  RV Share already take most if what I'd make, I'm basically cleaning and delivering it for free and now this.  Don't rent on RV Share. It's not worth the trouble or the money you lose.

author: Tony Santillanes
title: Terrible customer service and communication
body: What a joke! The customer service and communication are terrible. Don't expect anyone at this company to help you with an rv rental. The process of reserving an RV is a joke. You pay and then sit there for 24 hours waiting for a response. After 24 hours the request just expires without any communication. I am surprised a crap company like this can stay in business. Don't waste your time to money. They also falsely advertise unlimited miles. Then tell you it is unlimited with a daily limit! lol! WTF?

author: Jessica Hammonds
title: Irresponsible and Unfair
body:
Irresponsible and Unfair – Avoid RVshare
We rented a vehicle that broke down due to poor maintenance. Our family of six was left stranded and spent thousands of dollars arranging safe travel. RVshare promised reimbursement and then denied most of it after weeks of delays. Their process is disorganized and unfair. They keep your money, give you the runaround, and leave you with the bill. Not a trustworthy company.

author: Scott Demick
title: This company is corrupt and they are…
body: This company is corrupt and they are crooks.  They scammed me of $187 fees they were supposed to pay out on my last rental.  They charged me $87.50 for a previous reservation I had to cancel several weeks in advance prior to the reservation date due to repairs that needed to be done.  The customer service is horrible.  Stay far away from this company.

author: Kris Bohème
title: Stay Away
body: I've rented from RV Share a couple times. The first was disappointing because the images absolutely did not reflect the vehicle we got. After the second time I will never use them again. After putting down the $2K deposit, the owner cancelled the ride about a month later. About a month before our trip, we wanted to get some more details about the RV, and found that the owner had cancelled the RV. He had never received any funds, and RV Share never sent us any warning other than a message in their app. No email, no text message, no call. NO REFUND (6 (SIX!) MONTHS after owner cancelled). The only warning about this was a message in their website messaging system, which most people are not going to check until closer to their trip. This just smells like extra scummy business practices to squeeze some extra cash out of their customers' funds they no doubt invest until they need to pay out. I recommend staying far away from companies like this.

author: Abby Larsen
title: good luck getting anywhere with their 'customer service'
body: As both a renter through RVshare and owner with our own RV listed, I am blown away with how terrible their customer service is. What else are they providing with their cut of the rental $$ if they can't find ANY WAY to be helpful????

author: Bill Herrera
title: A renters bad experience
body: I rented two RVs through RV share. In Northern Nevada RVs rents out for much more than the nightly rate I usually charge for Burning Man. There is special pricing on the bottom of listing for special events. I cancelled three reservations for Burning Man at the regular rate and RV Share charged me cancellation fees totaling almost a 1k. They act like they own my RVs. I told them to piss off and that I'm not paying them dookie and cancelled my listings. They are garbage if you're an owner of an RV renting on their site. Their customer service is BS. I wouldn't recommend renting through their site. Also, the customer service lady I spoke to was probably in Thailand and could barely speak English. Total non-sense.

author: Lou Smith
title: Prompt handling by Casey
body: Prompt handling of cancellation due to illness

author: Frank van der Hulst
title: Cancelled booking without notice
body:
Apparently there is some problem with information they need me to provide. I have no idea what that problem is. The FIRST I knew of this was when I received an email saying they CANCELLED MY BOOKING due to needing "information necessary to secure Insurance and Protection for your upcoming RV trip". I already signed into their site and provided that information, paid them in full for the rental, and signed up for and paid for insurance. 

So today they cancelled my booking
* Without notice. 
* Without telling me there was a problem
* Three days before I'm due to fly out to pick up the RV
* While holding $2,600 of my money.

author: Ajensen
title: First time renting an RV and I had some issues
body:
First time renting an RV and I had some issues with submitting my info with my  driver license and the insurance protection. 
First time I called , I spoke with Laura.  She was very kind and helped me out with the Drivers License issue.  
The second time I called, I spoke with Sage and he kindly explained in full detail how the insurance protection worked and how it’s already included in the price, but you have to go in and accept and pick the coverage package that fits best for you.  
Thank you to both customer service reps for your help.  You were both very knowledgeable.  
It was confusing to me, because when I reserved the RV, I paid and received my confirmation.  I thought everything was good.  Then, I receive a email from RVshare letting me know I needed to complete another step and add insurance protection.  I thought I was going to have to pay another $100 plus on top of what I already paid for the RV.  
Thanks to Sage, I understand how it works now.  Wouldn’t it be easier on the customer if you could go through this whole process before reserving and paying for the RV you rent?   It sure would have been a lot easier on this old brain of mine!  
Sincerely,
A.jensen

author: Mark
title: Cancelled rental and poor communication
body: I reviewed the RVShare platform looking for a rental unit. I messaged three different owners with simple questions about their units, and got no response from any of them. Finally, I was able to find a unit with another owner and booked a reservation that was paid in full. Within weeks of our trip, I got a two line message saying there was unforeseen circumstances, and our rental would not be available. No explanation was given. I called customer service to complain about the lack of communication with owners on their platform, as well as the canceled rental with no explanation. They gave the standard reply of understanding my frustration and inconvenience, but were unwilling to offer any kind of nominal discount for me to look for another unit on their platform.  I’ve had multiple successful and uneventful rentals with RVShare prior to this year. This is a much different experience than I’ve had before and seems to echo the frustrations that other reviewers have been saying as well. Unfortunate, but I will not be using RVShare again as they gave the impression that they were OK with this lack of response from owners and the action of canceling a booked reservation without explanation.

author: Khadijah Rigaud
title: Renters Beware – RVShare Sided with Dishonest Owner and Ignored All Proof
body:
⭐ 1 Star – Deeply disappointed and disgusted by how RVShare handled our situation.

We rented an RV through RVShare and had one of the worst customer service experiences we’ve ever encountered. Despite being honest, respectful renters and providing overwhelming proof that a damage claim was false, RVShare chose to side with the owner without a proper investigation.

The RV owner, Dana Allen, falsely accused us of damaging her RV. My husband, who drove the vehicle, is a 20-year CDL truck driver with a flawless record no accidents, no damaged loads, currently driving hazardous materials. We take extreme pride in respecting other people’s property, and we returned the RV in the exact condition we received it.

Dana claimed she took photos of the RV prior to our rental, but when asked to produce them with time and date stamps (which any smartphone captures), she never did because those photos don’t exist. We requested this proof multiple times and got nothing. Still, RVShare charged us over $500 based on her unsubstantiated claim.

We went above and beyond:
	•	Submitted aerial photos showing how Dana parks her RV in a dangerously tight space (where damage clearly could’ve occurred)
	•	Provided context showing she admitted the RV was almost stuck in the mud before our trip
	•	Explained my husband’s impeccable driving history

None of it mattered. RVShare didn’t care. They didn’t defend the renter. They didn’t conduct a fair investigation. They just took the owner’s word.

📢 This is a warning to all renters:
RVShare does NOT protect you. If an owner lies, you’re at risk of losing hundreds of dollars no matter how much evidence you provide.

And to be very clear: Do NOT rent from Dana Allen. She is dishonest and untrustworthy. If you see her listings on RVShare or elsewhere, keep scrolling.

I will never use RVShare again and strongly advise others to look for more transparent, renter-friendly platforms. I’ve learned a costly lesson don’t let this happen to you.

author: kathy jorgensen
title: We rented a camper for our trip in…September 2025 which never happened
body: We rented a camper for our trip in September.  Owner informs us she has a wonderful travel opportunity to travel during our date and then tells us to cancel our reservation.  If we did that, then we lose our Admin fee.  I contacted customer service and we did get a full refund of our reservation deposit fortunately, but that ruined our plans since we had already made RV reservations at our destination.  Don't book with RVshare.  This could happen to you and ruin your vacation.

body:
In the process of setting up a new customer rental account, identification process asked for identification.  Totally reasonable and acceptable requirement.
I went through the procedure and could not get past some hiccup, and called customer service for help.  Customer service asked that I email the same ID's which I did, and all that seemed to work.  Next day, I got an email telling me that I have been banned for RVShare.  I contacted RVShare to see if there we could resolve the discrepancy, and got a short NO.  Big pain, and quite surprising.

body: Casey is a perfect gift & help with RVshare. We have had a TERRIBLE time trying to request the use of a camper trailer thru RVshare. author: Bryan Taylor
title: Completely screwed me out of payment…
body: Completely screwed me out of payment from a rental. Customer tried to cancel within the 30 day cancellation window so I was owed the full payment (very unlikely to re-book the dates). Rep verified by phone the morning before I’d receive full payment. Then in the afternoon I received an email saying the customer “didn’t verify info for insurance” and so they were canceling the trip. Called and they’d do nothing for me despite the documentation that the customer wanted to cancel and was trying to get out of paying. Pretty obvious he exploited a loop hole. Horrible they won’t stand behind us.

author: Curt Altarac
title: Beware of Hidden Fees and Poor Customer Service
body:
I attempted to rent an RV through RVshare, but after checkout I received an email informing me of several unexpected insurance fees—none of which were clearly disclosed upfront, and there was no way to opt out. I tried contacting customer service immediately, but no one answered. Days later, I finally spoke with someone who clearly had handled this type of complaint many times before. They walked me through the cancellation process—but still kept the “service fee” despite the misleading experience.

I’ve rented RVs, homes, and more over the years, and this was by far the strangest and least transparent experience I’ve had. I ended up canceling and losing money, but I felt compelled to leave a review so others are aware. There are better, more honest local companies out there—do yourself a favor and avoid RVshare.

author: A Hewmun
title: RV Share is an absolute nightmare of a…
body:
RV Share is an absolute nightmare of a company—stay far away! I booked an RV on March 4th for a family trip, shelling out a ridiculous $1,600 for just 4 nights. I told them exactly where I was going, spent over a month studying the area and the RV, and made campground reservations to ensure everything was perfect. My family—kids included—was buzzing with excitement for what was supposed to be an unforgettable getaway.
Then, on April 8th—ONE DAY before I was set to pick up the RV on April 9th—I got a notification that flipped my world upside down: the RV I booked and paid for over 30 days ago was canceled. No warning, no reason, just a gut punch out of nowhere. I was in the middle of packing when it hit, and I couldn’t believe it. The day before pickup? Are you kidding me?
It gets worse. RV Share didn’t even have the decency to apologize. No “sorry,” no discount on another RV, no help finding an alternative—nothing. Just a smug, heartless “Screw you, too bad” vibe that left me stranded. I scrambled to contact them, but their customer service was a black hole: no answers, no support, just silence. They took my money and ran, leaving me with a canceled trip and a family to disappoint.
The emotional toll was brutal. My kids were crushed—they’d been counting down the days, and I had to look them in the eyes and explain why we weren’t going. All that planning, all that excitement—wasted. It’s not just upsetting; it’s infuriating. RV Share didn’t care one bit about screwing us over.
And here’s the real warning: this isn’t a fluke—it’s how they operate. Dig around online, and you’ll see this is common practice for RV Share. They lure you in with promises of a great trip, take your hard-earned cash, and then cancel at the last second with zero remorse. It’s predatory, plain and simple. People need to know what they’re signing up for with this sham of a company.
Do NOT book with RV Share. Save your money, your time, and your sanity. Go with a business that doesn’t treat customers like disposable trash. RV Share is a scam—they don’t deserve a single star, let alone your trust. Avoid them like the plague.

RV share says too bad you have to buy it costing us another $400…
Ironically this insurance covers the owner driving it to, and from his place to our campground, insane.Plus he charged us $200 for an owners fee… fees fees fees

author: Mindy Pedersen
title: Offering discounts and then not honoring them...
body:
We booked an RV through RVShare months ago... two weeks before our trip, the owner cancelled our reservation. RVShare sent us 4 emails offering $60 off to book a new reservation through their site. It took a few hours to find something on such short notice that would work for us, and then the $60 off code would not work. 
When I called customer service they told me that the offer had expired... I had just received the most recent email less than 3 hours before making the reservation. They offered to send me a $25 off code... a far cry from $60 but at this point my hands are tied... I NEED an RV as I had already paid for the RV spot and made other arrangements based on having an RV on site for the event we are attending. Not to mention that there aren't any pet-friendly hotel rooms available in the area due to that event. They texted me the code... that one didn't work either... I called back... she sent me another code... which also didn't work. She then checked with her team and told me that at this time it appears promo codes are not working on the site at all. So, she said she would send me a $25 refund after the fact... I asked why I wouldn't get the $60 credit since that's the offer I was originally sent, now that we know codes aren't working. She said she would "try" but couldn't guarantee anything except the $25 off. 
This has been the most stressful and upsetting experience. It shouldn't be this difficult to honor something that you promised as a company. I didn't have a choice but to use them this time... but I won't use them again.

body:
EXTREMELY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE!
I was supposed to have a camper for 7 nights.  I returned it after 2 nights of no heat because it was non-functional.  I was not able to obtain a refund for the remaining nights.  As soon as RVShare has your money, they will do nothing to support you if there is a problem with the RV owner!  Beware, Beware, Beware!

author: Michelle Neff
title: BEWARE! Terrible customer service and roadside assistance
body: We rented from RVShare and the back wheels of the RV came off while driving on the highway! We were left stranded for over 7 hours. RVShare's customer service and roadside gave us the run around, at one point said we would have to pay an additional $2k to tow it, and we were left to find a place to fix it. After having to rent a car to continue our trip, we are now left with all of the expenses and didn't even get the RV for the days we rented it for. RVShare has been terrible in trying to get some money back and has not shown care about how our family was in danger.

author: Tim DiFelice
title: LOST money using RV Share- Had no control over owner cancelation
body: in August 2023 i booked through RV Share an RV for an extended weekend for a MS Hockey Tournament. After paying for the RV parking spot and paying the RV share for the rental, the owner of the RV cancelled my reservation 1 WEEK prior to my trip. I lost all the money and had to book a hotel room and my wife, dog and I never got to experience a pre purchase RV trip all the while loosing money. Since i actually had my reservation cancelled by the RV owner, RV Share did not allow me to post a review because my trip never happened.

author: David Oddi
title: This is a very fraudulent company
body: This is a very fraudulent company. If anyone is seeking to rent an RV, do not go thru the process of renting from RVshare. This company breaks its own rules to make money. I rented a camper in OK in Sept 2024 for 4 days. First of all when I went to pick up this camper, the owner never showed up to do the out paperwork to go over any damage before I rented it. Camper was stored at a public storage facility. I had to call him to find out about how to get in the camper. I checked the lights before pulling it away and the entire right side lights didn't work. No brake lights , no turn signal. I drove it away anyway because I already paid for it and there was no time to find another one. I returned the camper on the return day 4 days later and still the owner was not there to receive the camper so no in paper work was completed like he was supposed to do per rvshare rules. 3 days later I received an email from rvshare stating that owner found nothing wrong with the camper and was releasing my security deposit of $500 back to me and that this transaction was completed. A couple of weeks later I receive an email from rvshare that the owner filed a claim for damages and I was asked by rvshare what kind of damage there was. I didn't know of any damage. The only thing was that my wife found a piece of plastic molding on the floor when we picked it up. That was it. On Oct 10th I received an email from rvshare that they were charging my credit card $398 for damages. I looked at my credit card account and there was a pending charge for that amount. I immediately called a Claims adjuster for rvshare and she explained that was for some sort of damages when i had the camper. I had to pay for insurance when I rented the camper. Company policy. She never did tell me what the $398 was for and I never did revieve any claims paperwork showing what I was paying $398 for. I told her that I was cleared by the owner on Sept 5th and my security deposit was returned. She told me that there could be more charges later. I asked for what and she couldn't tell me. Later that afternoon I received an email from her saying this account has been closed. I called my credit card company and had my card locked and closed in case anymore fraudulent transactions occurred. I will never ever deal with this company again.

title: The RV owner cancelled my rental with…
body: The RV owner cancelled my rental with Thanksgiving approaching. I am not sure I can have the same celebration as what I was planning. The company said that they would penalize the owner $350. That's good but I wasn't about to get another RV in the area knowing that it was so easy to have them cancel. The customer service fellow was very helpful. Also, the pricing is not transparent. Want to poop? That's an additional $50 per night! So it's like Spirit Airlines for the ground. Don't use it.

author: James Bolland
title: Owner made a fraudulent claim that his…
body: Owner made a fraudulent claim that his tail light was damaged. Received emails from adjuster named Tami Starkey. Despite my best efforts to explain how I could not possibly have caused the damage the owner claimed, she still approved the claim without any investigation. 100% would not recommend. If you are going to rent an RV, make sure you record videos of how the RV looked prior and after returning. If you receive an email from Tami Starkey, heaven help you, you’re doomed.

author: Gina Perez
title: Only helpful to RV Share and Renters, not owners
body: I have been an owner on RVShare for the past 8 years.  They have recently made changes that favor themselves and the renters without regard for the RV Owners.  They currently owe me more than $200 for missing hubcaps from a recent rental they refuse to pay and won't even acknowledge my numerous emails.  I have been on this website for a long time with no problems but the recent experience has caused me to pull my listing.
reply: Hello Gina, I apologize for the experience you had. Please give us a call and we would be happy to look into this for you.

author: Ja
title: Disgusting, DO NOT USE.
body: Disgusting, AVOID at all costs as a renter. RV Share have perfected being an excellent scamming tool for RV owners to be able to fix up their broken, falling apart RV's by placing them in the hand of customers and waiting for something to (inevitably) go wrong so they can rinse them for RV repairs. AVOID at all costs, they ignore customers and don't look at any evidence. The RV we rented was run down, taped and zip-tied together, the owner wasn't present and provided old zoomed out photos to RV Share, within minutes of being on the freeway after collection the awning dangerously swung off, we pulled over to find it had only been propped on with one rusted threadless screw, had a number of holes where it had been reattached previously. We spoke to the owner immediately on the platform who was disinterested and told us to just 'screw it back on'. Upon return, we were later charged our entire deposit plus another $600 that they just took from my card without informing me. We sent time stamped photos as they requested, gave the full description of what happened showing all of the issues that the owner didn't disclose (with location and time stamps from collection) as well as the evidence of what had happened when we set off showing all of the holes where it had been screwed back on again previously. They didn't even look at any of it, they will take your money without informing you. Their 'customer service' is non-existaent and Kyla is one of the rudest people I have ever encountered. They did not send a breakdown of what was being claimed for, they did not send any documentation of any of it and just took money from my card. STAY AWAY FROM THIS SCAM AGENCY.
reply: Hi, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Michael Saltzman
title: Roadside Assistance Is NOT
body: I have had renters call Roadside assistance on several different occasions to help with tire blowouts and not once has RVShare offered any assistance. I've had renters stranded without generator power and today a renter stranded with engine failure. Any call for roadside assistance takes hours upon hours of wasted time only to be told they can't help when I or a renter finally reaches a person. So I've always had to find help myself. Roadside assistance doesn't exist and is a scam. They collect huge fees and don't offer the services they market.
reply: Hello Michael, we can certainly look into this further. Please give us a call at 1-888-482-0234.

author: Jennifer Madsen
title: I booked a fully refundable trip and…
body: I booked a fully refundable trip and had to cancel. They charged me over $300 in admin and insurance fees. Don't believe it when they say it's fully refundable. Must have been some fine print I missed. Sneaky. After your response:  i called the number you provided and was treated poorly while they told me there are non refundable admin fees etc.  Just wasted more of my time with the same result. POOR SERVICE AND DONT HIDE FEES.
reply: Hello Jennifer, please give us a call and we can look into this. When a renter cancels, the cancellation policy the owner has will apply; however, coverage costs will always be refunded. Please give us a call at 1-888-482-0234.

author: Linda Rehak
title: Don’t rent from this site!
body:
Don’t rent from this site!
They did not do a thorough pre rental inspection and charged me for damage that not happen during my rental time. The owner was late for pickup and then cut time short for inspection where the awning was not opened and not inspected. We did not use during our trip and got charged for damage. They could not prove that it was damaged during my rental, but their contract states all claims are the responsibility of the renters no matter what. This gives the owner zero responsibility and all damage is charged to the renters.

author: Cassy Chauvin
title: FRAUDULENT PRACTICES
body:
DO NOT RENT FROM RV SHARE. We were VERY mislead by the RVshare listing that we rented. The tires that were on the back of the RV, were not only BALD - two of them completely blew (and I mean SHREDDED) 1 hour into our trip. We were left on the side of the highway (US 16 - which is a minimum MPH of 70 with tractor trailers blowing by every 2-3 minutes), from 10:45 p.m. until 5:45 the next morning. WE had to pay out of pocket, $1600 to have the two tires replaced that should have been replaced prior to our trip, and have not been reimbursed by either the owner or RV Share. Not only was our safety completely jeopardized, but we were not able to get ahold of the "24/7" roadside assistance that RV share claims to have. After calling the number on their website, 6 times, waiting on a hold tone line for 30 minutes EACH and every time, the call would end and hang up on us. THIS IS FALSE ADVERTISEMENT AND FRAUDULENT. They left PAYING customers in an extremely dangerous and potentially fatal situation, and they have STILL not made this right. We spent $1560 on the RV, plus $1600 on the two tires that should have been replaced PRIOR to our trip. We lost an entire day spent sitting along the side of a dangerous highway, while waiting for someone to come and fix our tire.  

Also, this RV was overall a complete disappointment. The advertisement claims it sleeps 8, however the owners screwed in the table settee that is supposed to fold down into a bed, (and FAILED TO LET US KNOW prior) rendering that space completely useless. This is false advertisement. On TOP of that, the entire side bump out motor (that had "two" sleeping areas) did not even function. We had to manually push the entire slider out, so that our space was usable for sleep. It took all members of our group, to open the slider, and all of us to close the slider from the outside in on our final day. The bathrooms were dirty and looked like they had not been cleaned in years - dust and dirt and general grime. The master bathroom overhead window/vent was completely falling apart and unable to be closed, letting in bugs constantly. The doors on the master bedroom didn't even close, because they were off the rails. Not only has RV Share not provided us with a reimbursement for the money and fees that we paid for out of pocket, they don't respond to emails, they provide you with a claims specialist that does not help either and says that they are surprised that someone from their team has not yet reached out, and then goes MIA. RV Share is a complete waste of your time. You are going to be sadly mislead on any RV that you rent. This is our second time, and LAST time renting from RVShare, as BOTH experiences were completely misleading and there is no quality assurance on the RVs they are comfortable and COMPLIANT renting to paying customer.
reply: Hello Cassy, we apologize for the issues you have faced. Please reach out to us at 1-888-482-0234.

author: Daryl Klein
title: The RV Share Insurance policy is a huge…
body: The RV Share Insurance policy is a huge scam. We paid $381 for a mandatory insurance policy that has a $1500 deductible. We had an act of nature rip a Awning cover and were charged $1000 by the insurance company. So, basically it cost nearly $1400 out of pocket.  Had I been allowed to use my own insurance company it would have cost me $230 for a 14 day policy with a $100 deductible. Never again!!  Safe travel to everyone and I hope you never have an act of nature ruin your trip! It will cost you with RV Share mandatory insurance.
reply: Hello Daryl, the renter is responsible for covering up to the deductible on damages that occur while the unit is in their possession. The security deposit goes toward the deductible as well. All renters booked through RVshare are required to go through the insurance and protection flow in the dashboard, whether they are paying for the protection plan through RVshare, or an owner's commercial policy. Please reach out to us for further clarification 1-888-482-0234.

title: I am SO frustrated with RVshare
body: I am SO frustrated with RVshare! Last night (Friday) we spontaneously decided to go camping with friends on Sunday. We successfully booked an RV for 112$/night for 2 nights. We called immediately to cancel and was told that there was only one hour to cancel within 7 days. I AM FURIOUS WITH THEM (and with myself for not paying close attention to all the fees they were adding in). SEVEN HUNDRED DOLLARS FOR 2 NIGHTS THAT WERE ADVERTISED FOR 112$ A NIGHT
reply: Hello Robyn, thanks for reaching out to us. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

title: RV Share is greedy and not at all Owner…

I have removed both of my trailers from their service and will be using Outdoorsy exclusively!
reply: Hello Matthew, I apologize for the experience you had. We can certainly look into this for you. Please give us a call at 1-888-482-0234. As for the cancellation fee, this is for cancellations that do not fall under extenuating circumstances. Just as we protect our owners with the Renter Cancellation Policy, we want our renters to feel they have reliable plans in place. For further clarification, please reach out to us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234. To learn more about the Owner Cancellation Policy, please visit https://rvshare.com/owner-cancellation-policy.

author: Carlos Crestana
title: We rented a broken RV and were charged to fix what was broken
body:
We rented an RV and faced numerous issues, yet were unfairly charged $1,387 for damages we did not cause even after paying $347 in insurance.
	
	Booking & Pickup: On April 15, 2024, we contacted Nedra Woods, the RV owner, to rent the RV for 3 days. We negotiated early pickup and late drop-off for $100 each, and finalized the booking on April 19. On May 1, the owner informed us that the RV had a damaged door that couldn't be fixed in time. The door could NOT be closed from the INSIDE. She offered a $200 discount, which we accepted due to our travel plans with friends. Pics attached.
	
	Condition of RV: Upon pickup, the RV was dirty and had several damages. We documented the dirt in the bedroom and on the couch, and the exterior damages. At the campground, we found the awning inoperable due to duct tape. Despite owner's assurance that the awning was functional (!), the tape prevented it from moving and removing the tape revealed the broken frame, making it unsafe in the wind. We never left it unattended or open overnight contrary to the baseless claims from the owner. Pics attached.
	
	Safety Hazards: The rear left luggage compartment couldn't be locked and opened while we were on the road, requiring us to tape it shut. The exterior light and some power sockets were non-functional, leaving us reliant on the awning's LED lights. Pics attached.
	
	Incident with Rear Window: While parked, my sister tried to open the rear window, which immediately shattered without force. We cleaned the area and taped the shades to secure the RV, storing the window frames in a luggage compartment. Pics and video attached show that it couldn't have been hit by a tree, as owner baselessly claimed.
	
	Despite comprehensive evidence (photos, videos, and comments), RVShare unfairly charged us $1,387. This charge is unjustified as we did not cause the damages listed. We demand a fair review and resolution of this matter with full refund for this upsetting, aggravating and frustrating experience.
reply: Carlos, you should be receiving an email shortly regarding the further investigation of this issue. Thank you for your patience.

title: This was the worst RV experience I've…

The RV was delivered with damages, and they tried to blame it on me despite my clear evidence to the contrary. Their customer service was unhelpful and dismissive. This company is not trustworthy, and their business practices are deceptive. Save yourself the headache and steer clear of this rental service. Absolutely not worth it.

author: R Machin
title: Disappointed with RVshare's Refund Policy
body:
I recently rented an RV through RVshare for a family trip to the Grand Canyon scheduled for June 24 and 25. The total cost was $673.68, which I paid on May 24. Unfortunately, due to the high cost, I decided to cancel the reservation on June 4.

To my surprise, I only received a refund of $152.64. I called the company on June 10 to inquire about the remainder of my refund after checking my credit card statement, only to be told that I would not be receiving any additional money back. This experience has been extremely disappointing.

Their refusal to provide a full refund feels like a scam. I urge others to be cautious when dealing with RVshare and to thoroughly understand their cancellation policies. I sincerely hope the company reconsiders its policies and practices, as this experience has left a very negative impression.
reply: Hello, on each listing page, the Cancellation Policy is shown and chosen by the owner out of the three options we offer; strict, standard, or flexible. This is agreed upon at the time of booking the reservation. The cancellation policy is upheld when the renter initiates the cancellation. If the owner needs to cancel the reservation for any reason, then the renter will automatically receive a full refund.

author: Robert A Gorman
title: RVshare scam and reply
body: Buyer be aware.  The insurance that RVshare forces you to purchase is a scam and in some states illegal.  You give your credit card and pay the fees to only find out that you cannot check no on insurance.  The problem is I had the trailer delivered by the owner.  I do not need road side which they make you purchase. To top that they cover windshield repair along other things that a trailer does not even have.  It is very clear that after you have already paid the fee that you cannot get out of the contract now without loosing the fee amount.  I total scam.  Other rental company’s do not do this and the rentals are less money.  It is strongly suggested to not use RVshare unless you enjoy being forced to paid insurance for something that does not exist. A good example is a broken windshield.  FYI a trailer does not have a windshield and if the trailer is delivered why would RVshare require me to have roadside insurance.
reply:
Hi Robert, we're sorry to hear about your experience. Every RV rental is required to secure RVshare Insurance and Protection in case anything accidental happens while the RV is on a trip, there are exceptions to this rule in some cases. This also includes trips where the RV might be delivered or stationary for the duration of the trip. Even if the RV is delivered or stationary, damages may still occur, examples include fire, acts of nature like storms and falling trees, etc. 

Coverage during the rental period will vary depending on the package is selected during checkout. For more information on what the package includes visit the Insurance and Protection page at https://rvshare.com/insurance
