author: Dominic Brito
title: I have rented my RV on RVshare for…
body: I have rented my RV on RVshare for years.  Not happy with changes that the company is making because they don’t protect the renter.  Renting an RV is a lot of work and takes a lot of time keeping your rig road ready.  They have a very strict cancelation policy and they have loop holes that allow renters to bypass the cancelation policy and that puts RV owners in a bad spot. I live in the mountains and have to setup my RV prior to renter and tear it down and not store anything in it during downtime. With this policy loop hole I have lost out on lots of my time when they fail to verify insurance till the last minute. This loop hole should be closed so that Rv owners time are protected. I have delisted by RV with them and will be using other sites.  I enjoy renting my RV allowing others to experience RV life and meeting new people. It’s unfortunate that it has come to this.
reply: Hi Dominic, we're sorry to hear about your experience. The information necessary was not completed to secure The RVshare Protection Plan, so your reservation was canceled. We understand that the timing was not ideal but to protect you, we will not let any reservation go out that has not properly secured insurance. You can contact our Customer Experience team 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234 if you wish to discuss this reservation further.

reply:
Hi Brian, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. We make the administrative fee on post-trip charges available to all renters at several steps in the booking process. 

At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

This was our worst fear using RvShare.  Good luck if you use this service. You will need it.

In the event of a cancellation, refunds are processed automatically and typically take 2-5 business days depending on your bank. A full refund has been issued for your reservation. If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

author: Melissa Tucker
title: Rv share is most unhelpful
body: We had our rv rented out and it ended up in the shop. It did it get repaired so we had to change the rv to another one we have. I called RV Share and told them what was going on. They told me to tell the renters to call and have it changed. I did as he sat in our driveway. He did not do it and I was unaware of that fact. They brought back our rv with damage. I got an estimate which was $5400. They would not pay it because he never called. RV Share sent the insurance money back to them ($1200). I am extremely upset with rv share about this entire situation. I now have a 2023 that was the second time it had been rented with the damage. I personally can not afford $5400. It is so sad as they knew it had to be changed and they have the 2023 on their platform as well. I currently have 4 rentals through them and I am considering changing to another platform. I had to change my other rv today because I accidently double booked it and all I had to do was tell them I needed to change it and they took care of everything. I realize I ama small fish to them but it is extremely frustrating that they will not help at all.
reply: Hi Melissa, I apologize for your experience. Please reach out to us at 1-888-482-0234 and we can certainly look into this further.

I'll update as I see how well the site works at renting my Travel trailer.

Thank you Joel! You made my day!

title: My experience with Morgan was very helpful!
body:
My experience with Morgan was very helpful. Before I connected with him I was going to leave a bad review due to several calls to customer service at RVshare without getting a response that was helpful at all. the other customer service employees were rude as well.
Morgan took a long time to understand my issue and came up with solutions. He was very understanding to my frustration with trying to get an answer previously and could not. RVshare is lucky to have him in customer service.

title: Jason B
body: Jason B was the agent who helped me with the issues I was having.  He was thorough, efficient, and he has a sense of humor, which I really appreciate.

author: Alexis Singleton
title: Dedicated & Efficient Customer Service
body: I had another fantastic experience with RVshare's customer support. As an owner, I encountered an issue that had the potential to impact a future reservation. However, Jesse S. went the extra mile to address the problem promptly. Within an hour, she not only resolved the issue but also personally reached out to me to provide an update. Their dedication and efficient service are truly commendable.

author: Reba
title: Ross was very helpful
body: Ross was very helpful! He explained everything clearly and thoroughly and resolved my issue in an efficient manner. Glad I had that type of experience with RV share and I am also very glad they have a phone number to cal with customer service like Ross had!
reply: Thank you for the kind words!

author: Ja'Miah Striverson
title: Lack of Communication
body: My booking got canceled due to lack of insurance information. However I was notified of the information needed when they informed me that my reservation was canceled. I give it a 1 star for the lack of communication with their third party insurance handler and for the miscommunication from Alexander H who is apart of their Customer Experience team. Alexander failed to inform me of the information needed for insurance purposes and failed to email me back. Had their Customer Experience rep emailed me back that morning, my reservation and trip wouldn't have been canceled.
reply: Hi, we apologize for this experience. We do try out best to reach out to obtain the necessary information as quickly as possible. We cannot let reservations go out without the RVshare protection activated. This is all a part of the booking flow that is a vital step. Please reach out and we can look into this further, 1-888-482-0234.

author: Camille Attell
title: Jason was responsive & very helpful
body: Jason was responsive & very helpful. He was also empathetic towards an issue we encountered with a renter.

author: Dan Hitchcock
title: Very quick responses!
body: I have used online chat help desks with many companies before, but none compare to what I just experienced with Jason B at RVShare.com. He was very knowledgeable, and incredibly quick. The last thing I want is to wait around for responses and have a chat take 10 times as long as it should. That wasn't a problem here. Awesome experience!

author: Thomas McNamara
title: terrific cust support
body: terrific cust support

author: Peter

body: Thank you Ross for your help! Great customer service!!

author: Consumer

The live chat what great with Arthur. He helped me so much with my booking. I had a problem with the insurance and he checked my reservation, explained everything to me and fixed it.
He was very polite and friendly and I would always do it again. Great help, thank you so much.

author: Truly Blessed

body:
Rvshare is one if not the best RV rentals platforms available, as a Host I am grateful for the customer support I've received whenever I have an issue and today Ross was my shining star of support, he was a great listener, very efficient and addressed my issues with professionalism. 
Thank you so much Ross.

author: Daniel Christ
title: NEIL (B) with the CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE…
body: NEIL (B) with the CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE TEAM went OVER & BEYOND in a situation that had us in a bind with our damaged rv. There's still GREAT GENUINELY caring people out here and we are glad to partner with a company like RVSHARE that employs gentlemen like NEIL (B). GREAT JOB & THANK YOU SO MUCH.

author: Shawn Murphy
title: This was a horrible experience
body: This was a horrible experience. The door broke early on during the trip (not our fault). The RV Share representative had mentioned that there was a problem with the door getting stuck when we went to rent it, but we did not realize exactly what he meant. We thought that he meant that it was just a little hard to open. However, we soon realized that when that door got stuck, it became IMPOSSIBLE to open! We had to climb in and out of the passenger door the entire time, which was very difficult. Roadside Assistance refused to help us, declaring that it was not an emergency. Second, it was supposed to sleep 10, but it truly only slept about 6, and we had rented it precisely because it slept 10. We NEEDED it to sleep 10 people, so that was extremely deceptive. When we confronted the RV share representative with this fact, he claimed that these upright recliners could sleep 2. These recliners only reclined partially rather than fully flat, so there is no way in which most people could actually sleep in such an upright position. Also, there is no way that it even comfortably slept even 8 adults. Maybe it could comfortably sleep 6 adults, but even that would be stretching it. The first night, we couldn't even get the table/bed to work properly, so it only slept about 5. Third, the insurance and fees were a total racket - way expensive! Fourth, the bathroom stunk. The backup camera is not to be trusted when backing these things up. My husband accidentally hit something, and it made a very small dent in the back, and so we lost our entire damage deposit.  We will never rent from them again!
reply: Hi Shawn, we apologize for this experience you had with the RVshare owner. We can certainly look into this further. Please reach out to us at 1-888-482-0234.

author: Scot Anderson
title: First night in a 12 day rental and we…
body: First night in a 12 day rental and we discovered the RV had an irreparable water leak that kept us from using sink or toilet.  Returned the RV the next day as the owner had no solution for the issue.  Started a dispute with RVShare to get a full refund, but they only gave us a partial refund...and the owner would not respond to RVShare to comment.  Horrible customer service.  The whole experience ruined a two-week holiday that we had planned!
reply: Hello Scot, we are sorry to hear about your experience with the RV. If you wish to discuss this reservation or dispute further, please feel free to contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234. However, please note that this dispute will not be reopened and the decision is final.

author: Anna
title: Cancellation 18 hours before my RV rental!!!
body: I rented an RV 1 month prior to our trip. The rental was cancelled less than 24 hours. RVshare was not very helpful in rebooking or compensating for the cancellation.
reply: Hello Anna, we're so sorry to hear about this unfortunate experience. In the event of an owner cancellation, you do receive a full refund which typically take 2-5 business days. If needed, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234 or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Alex
title: Paid and Website is unable to complete the Reservation
body:
I tried to rent a RV from them, filled out all the info, and paid the money via credit card.  I got back a few minutes latter an email that my ID check failed.  I emailed and called for help, but all they could say is keep trying.  Well the webpage and links they sent to verify kept showing up as a blank screen.  I tried multiple browsers and PC's with no luck.  I used Chrome to see there were HTTP 500 Internal Server errors and sent screen shots, but no one helped to seemed to contact their own IT department.

It makes me feel they created this website to take your money and make you so frustrated that you cancel the reservation yourself and then loose a portion of payment.

Update: 30-Dec-2023.  After conversations with a manager on the phone, it was clear there was no simple solution on getting my data uploaded for verification.  I asked for a 100% refund and the team responsible for the reservation was kind enough to refund to my CC 100% of the charges within a business day.  Thank you for helping to fix the situation.

author: Chantal Clement
title: DO NOT rent from RV Share!
body:
DO NOT rent from RV Share!! Save yourself unnecessary stress and heartache - 5 days before my trip and amid the holidays they allowed the owner to cancel my booking and then penalize me, the customer whose money has been committed for months. Now days before I have purchased flights and activities I have to break the news to my kids that we are not able to have the vacation we planned for months during the holiday season!
The rep told me to contact potential owners to rebook. What utter nonsense, what is the point of their third-party fees if they do nothing to support their impacted customers.
The staff lacks empathy, they are unreliable, unempathetic and have deplorable customer service. 
I highly suggest working with independent companies who care about their customers than this useless service.
After days of stress and escalation the day before my scheduled trip, a rep finally contacted me to acknowledge my complaint and offer me 5% off a future booking. After such a negative experience why would I ever give them another chance?

I just finished my trip and successfully acquired another RV from TxRVAdventures I concur that RV Share has the most deplorable customer service and is not worth the stress, they offer nothing and you can get so much more from direct RV companies. I highly recommend you do your research and if you have to pay a little more to get value for your money and have a stress-free booking explore booking with direct companies, it is a night and day experience.
reply: Hi Chantal, we apologize for the cancellation. We want you to know that you won't be penalized for it. If you need help with re-booking, please don't hesitate to contact us at 1-888-482-0234 or chat with us on our website, rvshare.com. You can also email us at https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Timothy Urrey
title: Rented a Rv for a weekend (2 nights)…
body: Rented a Rv for a weekend (2 nights) with RV owner towing and setting up. Owner did awesome RV itself was fine. The problem was "RV share" management.  I have received over 30 emails for a 2 day stay. Needed insurance although owner towed. Filled out enough paperwork to buy twice and website difficult to navigate. All said and done $300 a night not including lot rent for a used 30 foot Rv in Arkansas is high.
reply: Hey there, thanks for your feedback regarding your experience. If you need any further assistance, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: David Ritter
title: worry free rental is a scam
body:
RVshare connects millions of renters to the perfect RV rental and provides a safe, secure booking experience. When you book and pay on RVshare, our Worry-Free Rental Guarantee protects your payment, security deposit, and more should anything go wrong.

This is a flat out lie, I called within 12 hrs. of  making a 69 charge which turn into 1700.00 and got nowhere with customer support  I couldn't even talk to a manager  so I out 1700.00 that I was gonna spend visiting my children for Christmas
reply: Hello David, we're so sorry to hear about this experience. Please contact our Customer Experience team for further assistance with this matter. You can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Chantell Martin
title: Easy and reliable
body: Easy and reliable! Thank you RV share for helping us find the perfect RV.

author: WIL
title: I had the most wonderful experience…
body:
I had the most wonderful experience with the RVShare representative, Bryan.

We had a problem with our listing and we needed to setup a new one. I had been working on this for a little bit and had spoken with  representative who had said that another department would email me and to take screenshots of the current listing, then re-enter the information once the other department notified me.  Well, we had a person that wanted to rent the RV, but couldn't because of this dilemma. I called again today and got Bryan. I explained my situation and he took care of the issue while I waited on the phone.  I'm so happy that Bryan stepped in!!!  And, we didn't lose our renter!  Thank you again Bryan!!

author: Courtney Johnson
title: Don't trust RVShare and their insurance.
body: A renter destroyed so much of my trailer and RVShare did nothing to help me recoup the repair costs.  We kept the deductible, but they did nothing to help recoup the other $1000 in damages.  They told us tough luck.  The insurance they sold to the renters didn't cover the first $1500 (deductible), but informed me that it was fully covered.  Now I am out $1000 because the renter backed my trailer into some trees, had dogs tear up the inside, and busted up the outside bumper grill.
reply: Hello Courtney, we're so sorry to hear about the damage to your RV. We recommend reaching out to our Damages team for further information on your insurance claim. You can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

Completely unnecessary, yet you have to wade through all the nonsense because there might be yet another costly surprise in there somewhere (see my first point). Don’t know if this describes the whole industry, but I will definitely try another provider before I go to RVshare again.

 So the trailer was just an exspensive sleeping vessel. The owner tried to make the problems our fault and didnt even offer to compensate us.

body: Sometimes it is frustration waiting for your refund when charged wrong and waiting for your deposit to be refunded.  It is a large amount of money.

author: ROBINSON, LLOYD

body: Using RV Share was convenient and easy to find a RV to rent.  I was trying it on my own when I came across the RV Share website and then it became so much easier in selecting the RV I required and securing it for my trip.  RV Share handles everything in your preparation for renting a RV.  I will use it again without fail.

author: EdF
title: RVShare is Great
body:
Was able to find an RV - i think we would be more comfortable if RVShare included a safety checklist for owners prior to releasing a vehicle to a renter.  We had a good experience but there were a few items (such as no taillights) that could have caused an issue for us and damage to the owners vehicle.
Also some additional resources for first time renters would be great - things like not being able to use certain appliances when the vehicle is in motion, local dump stations, and the type of power connections needed to hook away from a RV park would all have been helpful - our owner didn't cover these items but I couldn't expect him to know our level of expertise so not his fault!

author: Lina
title: The owner of the RV was close by and…
body: The owner of the RV was close by and was able to deliver promptly. Correspondence was easy to access as well as payments. The company provided all the information and step by step of the process. I’m very pleased and my guests enjoyed the privacy of their own space in my property. Thank you!!

author: Cheri Creecy
title: Bill and Brenda were the best and it…
body: Bill and Brenda were the best and it was wonderful to have our first RV share experience with them. It was great as they took the time to share features and how they worked, and the were patient when guiding us through the process.

author: ron
title: terrible concept
body: terrible concept. been cheated insurance money. Never again

author: Jonathan Scott
title: The owners of the RV were great
body: The owners of the RV were great! They were responsive, kind and professional. They made  the rental very easy, and my family enjoyed the unit. Would recommend them and RV Share to anyone looking to rent a unit.

author: Stephanie Falcon
title: Unacceptable Insurance Clause
body:
I added similar comments in the owners review, not realizing I could provide a separate review for my experience with RVshare. I think if you look at the correspondence I had with your associates in an effort to try and obtain a refund of the money I had paid before learning the RV was not suitable to take on the road, you will have all the information you need. The bottom line is, your insurance clause is unacceptable for someone who is not able to use the RV for what it was intended for. Just because it sat in my driveway for a couple of hours before we noticed carbon monoxide was leaking in, should not constitute me not receiving a full refund of the insurance amount. 
I will not use, nor refer anyone to RVshare.

Edited on 12/15/2023: 
After posting the review above, RVshare contacted me and conducted another review of my case and completed the reimbursement of the remaining amount. I am thankful they chose to reconsider the events impacting my reimbursement request. For this, I have changed my overall rating to 3 stars.

body:
The owners was phenomenal and they made sure everything was perfect for us .

author: Steve Fenn
title: I really enjoyed my first rental but…
body: I really enjoyed my first rental experience. I did have one issue with the RV share group and that is the "fees and costs". I had to pay 2 times the cost of renting the R.V. to insure it and to have RVshare be the intermediary for the rental. When I returned the rental there was an expected over mileage charge that I was expecting to pay. The biggest thing that bothered me was RVshare charged me a fee for my excessive daily mileage as if RVshare did anything that the over mileage fee needs to cover such as maintenance and repairs. I guess what my issue is the person renting the rig to me got about 39% of the bill while RVshare group made 61%. The rig owner  took all of the risk and had to pay for the extra maintenance the mileage created.

author: Mike Le
title: Great experience
body: Great experience. Love David because he always responded when I needed him.

author: JM
title: RV was not ready and unfair relation
body:
RV was not ready at administrative level (RV Share) when I came and I d to insist to get everything, this event delaying my pick up
The insurance company has been inefficient on the claim process I get
Basically everything is against the customer, there is no balanced relation between platform, renters and customers, this platform is made to proctect the guys renting their RV whatever the condition and make money, the coverage of the insurance is ridiculous taking into account the cost, the platform is taking 15% in any financial flow on top of the initial rental, I felt really abused by the conditions

author: Thomas D.

author: Louise B
title: The RV we rented was unsafe and not…
body: The RV we rented was unsafe and not equipped as advertised. The owner was unresponsive once we returned the camper (he didn’t even meet us at the drop off and instead sent a last minute text telling us to leave the keys in the camper). We filed a dispute with RV Share and again the owner was unresponsive. RV Share refunded us their small service portion of the charges which was appreciated. We made the mistake of trusting the owner when he said the camper was ready to go and trusting that RV Share insures their rentals are safe and equipped as advertised. We won’t make the mistake of renting an RV again.
reply: Hello Louise, we're so sorry to hear about your experience with this RV and RV owner. If you need further assistance, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

Awesome experience!! Smooth transaction, especially for first time RV users. Will definitely be using again. 
Thank you!

author: Houseboating on the lake!

When I had to call for help, the personnel were not professional at times (one guy hung up on me) and one of the other people I worked with sounded as if I was inconveniencing her with my questions.   Also, the first RV I rented was cancelled by the owner less than a week  out from my rental.  This sent me to scramble and had to go through the laborious process all over again.  However, this time I called and had one of your representatives help me and she was very professional.

author: Michael/Kathie Anderson
title: RVshare nice to do business with

I liked that RVshare had several trailers to choose from & a few were in our price range.  
A lot of the trailers cost more than a nice motel room & that seemed expensive but maybe the owners set the prices not RVshare.
Some of the website information links were hard to find or confusing but when I called the help number the people were very patient & helpful.

author: Bryan Poppenhusen
title: Using RV Share was an easy to use…
body: Using RV Share was an easy to use alternative from a physical rental company. Renting from an individual resulted in a well kept RV, and great person to person experience. The owner of our RV Joel had great communication, and was great to work with. I cannot wait until my next trip.

author: Lori
title: It was very easy to find and reserve an…
body: It was very easy to find and reserve an RV. The website was easy to use. I felt the pictures of the RV’s were good as well. The pricing was very transparent and I knew exactly what I was paying. The only reason I gave 4 Stars, is I felt the fees were a little high. I understand insurance of course, but the service fees added up fast, the $78 per night RV added up to over $500 for 2 nights, by the time I paid the service fee, taxes and insurance. Overall it was a good experience though.

author: CJ
title: The rentals were good but the fees were…
body: The rentals were good but the fees were silly when we had the RV delivered vs towing it. Should not have to pay for insurance when we are not towing the RV.

author: Tinks.
title: Choosing the RV was the easy bit
body: Choosing the RV was the easy bit. Paying for the rental and insurance with my UK international credit card was more difficult. Several attempts were made. Initially successful, the CC company AI decided that there was fraudulent activity and cancelled my card. I was informed by a phone call at 02.30 am in the middle of the dessert! Luckily the insurance payment had been accepted by another card- but we didn’t know that at that point. Not the best start! We had a fantastic time touring in Nevada- Lake Mead, Valley of Fire, Red Rocks and in Arizona- Willow Beach, Lake Havasu, Kingman, Grand Canyon, Lake Havasu Cattail Park and Route 66. The support from the RV staff and the owners was great. We were able to take our time and really get the feeling of the places we visited. We never rushed. Places we missed are on a list for the next time we visit the USA. We have lifetime memories!

author: Lucretia Gray
title: My experience was very good
body: My experience was very good. I WISH IT WAS EASIER to locate someone locally..I had a problem with the RV's that I really liked being a couple of states away! I will use  you again and I am recommending you to my friends!

author: ArtJones
title: The RV was set up and ready to go when…
body: The RV was set up and ready to go when we got there. Newer 2022 model and everything  worked even had a Kruric coffee maker and coffee. Just like home

author: Simone Cichocki
title: Great surprise
body: I'm surprised how the company  works. Easy, quickly, and so safe!! Was the first time that we rented an RV  by RVshare and highly recommend.

author: THOMAS R Watkins Sr
title: Poorly maintained.
body: RV was poorly taken care of Maintenace   wise. Needed shocks & exhaust system

author: Allyson
title: Good variety of campers
body: Good variety of campers. Multiple options with all kinds of features, from basic campers to luxury liners. We’ve always been pleased when using RV Share. Delivery or pull it yourself, they’re everywhere you want to go. This time, as is every time we’ve rented, our host was great. Delivered and set us up for a great weekend in his roomy camper. It was clean, had a lot of living and sleeping space. Will definitely use RV Share in he future!

author: Todd
title: RVShare Service Fee - Ripoff.
body: I booked an RV through RVShare because I needed it for an event we were going to sponsor in six months.  After two days, I realized that I was going to need to sponsor the same even in a different state as opposed to the one I initially planned for.  I was going to cancel the reservation and re-book for another location; however, RVShare charged me a $69 "RVShare service fee."  I know how much it cost to acquire a new customer like me, and I know how much it cost to keep a potential good customer.  Hint: It's cheaper to keep a new customer, something that RVShare is oblivious to.  They got my $69, but I estimated my need for this kind of service over the next twenty years and I'm probably one of many who will blacklist this company forever.

author: Donna
title: Wonderful experience!
body: Everything about renting the RV was wonderful.  The owners were great to work with.  They brought the RV and set it up. It was fully stocked with what we needed. We had a wonderful time at Lake Lurleen with family.

author: Kathryn

body: Fabulous experience. Rented for NASCAR race and it could not have gone any better.

author: Randy Langlitz

body:
I've used campers before and have years of experience pulling trailers. However I'm a first time RVShare user. Overall excellent process.
RVShare website was easy for me to navigate and the mobile app worked very well for me which was very important while coordinating the pick up and drop off with the owner.
Camper trailer owner was very accommodating and communicated very well with me. Everything was as advertised, no surprises. 
It would be nice to see the security deposit returned faster but thats not realy a show stopper. I've already booked my next trip through RVShare.
Thanks

author: Charlotte reed
title: DO NOT TTRUST THIS PLACE!
body:
We rented this RV in good faith that it was in working condition.  We drove 98 miles to our first destination.  Once we parked the carbon monoxide monitor went off immediately.  Also, the water hose was bent at the connector and water went everywhere on the outside.  We would have had to purchase a new water hose in order to use for the three days we had rented.  Also, the sewer connector did not have fitting to connect to the outside tank.  
The ac in the front part of the unit does not work on the passenger side well.
We could have dealt with other items, however, we did not feel comfortable sleeping with the carbon monoxide possible problem, so we turned around and drove home with windows down on unit to ensure our wellbeing.
We returned the unit the next morning.  No one was at the location, so we were informed to leave keys in box and park which we did.  I have video and pictures of all.
We requested at least a partial refund because I had paid for nights in RV parks we didn't get to use.  Instead Scott charged against yhe cc he had on file an additional charge because we did not file up with gas.
I have talked with an attorney and plan to take this to small claims court.
Sincerely
Charlotte Reed

author: John Kirkcaldie
title: No security deposit back....its been…
body: No security deposit back....its been months even with emails sent to with my clean return sheet attached. Do not use RV Share.
reply: Hi John, the way we handle security deposits is by placing a "hold" on your account for the amount of the security deposit. When that happens, your bank creates a pending transaction but never actually processes that payment. Because of that you were never actually charged that money, you won’t see a refund in your account. Instead, that original pending transaction will either say that it was voided, or it will have disappeared altogether. If you have any further questions, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Arla
title: I rented a motorhome from RVShare
body: I rented a motorhome from RVShare.  Price was decent, but the insurance was really high even for the highest deductible price.  Insuring it with my own insurance company wasn't an option.  My insurance company would have added the week's rental to my policy for less than $20 rather than the almost $500 insurance through RVshare.  I also paid a $500 security deposit.  The motorhome owner seemed very nice.  The motorhome was pretty nice.  My trip was fine except when I got to my destination, my brother noticed there was a flat tire on the inside dual tire.  He also said the other tires were in horrible condition from sun and weather exposure.  He was upset at the owner for sending me with them in that condition.  I wasn't used to driving something tall and I brushed up against tree branches.  That damaged the awning and paint finish.  My brother said for the awning to fall apart the way it did, it like the tires had to have been already in horrible condition due to sun and weather damage.  The 24 hour roadside service person agreed with his conclusion.  I spent a half a day in the tire shop.  I returned the RV.  The seemingly nice owner fraudulently charged me with generator hour overage which I absolutely did not use it more than a couple hours.  I disputed the overages.  RVShare withdrew the $1000 (which added to the $500 security deposit is the $1500 deductible) plus the $200+ overages from my bank account without notice as soon as damage and overages we're reported and before the dispute or insurance adjustment even had a chance to get started.  It is impossible to talk to anyone at RV share who is located in the USA.  Their international customer service people are helpful before the trip, but can't do anything for you afterward.  I contested the withdrawals and the overdraft charges with VISA.  VISA ruled in my favor and refunded my money, but now I can't get a hold of anyone to solve payment of the damages.  RVShare has non-existent customer service after the sale, bad business practices of withdrawing money from bank accounts prematurely, and absolutely no communication.

author: John Doe
title: Rig equipment
body: The one suggestion I would make is to require owners to accurately list, in detail, what equipment is included on the rig.  General comments such as "outfitted kitchen" or "outdoor gear" are not helpful.  One of our recent rentals had a few pots and pans, no plates cups or cutlery, though the description implied the inclusion.  We had to get paper plates, plastic forks, etc.  Our most recent rental included a fully itemized list organized by on board cupboards and basement storage bins.  We referred to this list several times in search of basement items; in one case a first aid kit.  We looked at the on board itemization and found the kit immediately. A huge help in prepping for the trip.

author: Paolo
title: RVShare and Digusure are scammer companies
body:
Dear RVShare and Digisure, with reference to my last review «Let's face the truth» to which you didn't even bother to give a response. I would like to evidence to everyone what has happened in the last two weeks …

Digisure representative, showed us (via email) a photo of a damaged roof of one RV which we don't know whether or not it portrayed the RV rented by us, without even a date stamped on the photo to at least understand if the photo had been taken in the period of our rental. And with this “fake photo” he took 1,500 USD from our credit card and ran away…. Together with RVShare. Company of clowns !!! 
 
To ensure that everyone knows about it, I am publishing the article 11 of the RVShare contract with which they steal money from renters .

According to the Terms of Service, Paragraph 11, Damage to RVs; Renter Responsibilities.  https: rvshare.com ... terms-of-service
 
  • Renters will be held liable for any damage that cannot be proven to have existed prior to  the rental period.  

I don't know if you have understood!!! With the article above, RVShare and Digisure can charge anything to the renters... as they did to us !!! So dear gentlemen, future customers, if you want to use RVShare, when you pick up the RV plese photograph everything (front, back, roof , wheels, underbody, internal and external punctures, BBQ, generator, all the staff .... everything !!!) and when you return the RV, ask to the Owner to check the overall condition of the RV in front of you and pretend he sign a declaration. Otherwise, you’ll go home and few hours later RVShare and Digisure will charge on your card an amount to cover a «fake damage» that you never caused!!! 

RVShare, Digisure: you are clowns and scammer !!! 

Give us back the money you stole from us (ref. Accident RVSUS3233914). We sent you all the evidence including photos with stamped dates showing that the roof of the RV was not damaged!!!
reply: Hello Paolo, we are so sorry to hear about this experience. If you would like to dispute the damages charges associated with this reservation, please contact our Customer Experience team to open a formal dispute. You can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Jim Herring
title: Very good first rental, host very nice, easy pickup and dropoff
body: Overall good experience with website, transaction and rental unit.  Not crazy about the asynchronous nature of the messaging system.  I wasn’t always notified by text when I got a new message, and when I did I had to pull off the road and open up the app to read.  I understand the need to communicate through the app but would be nice to allow the app to pass through the message via text (so I could listen to on Apple CarPlay) or provide a CarPlay app.

author: Greg Cooper
title: My host was great and the RV was…
body: My host was great and the RV was perfect !!!

author: Carrie
title: Skeptics, Now Love RV Share
body:
We were skeptical at first, being first time RV, share users. However, the app was very easy to use, and communication was open, clear and frequent response from the owners of the RV.  If this is your first time, using RV share, I would think of it as one of those Bnb apps but for RVs. You are renting a private owners of the RV, with RVshare as the intermediary (software service). 
You can pick up your RV/trailer or pay for delivery.
We opted to have ours delivered to our campsite.
If this is your first time, renting you sure to choose your spot and rent it for the whole time that you want it. Be sure you are there when the owner arrives to set up. They will explain to you all the ins and outs of operating the RV especially if you have a delivery. Take full advantage of this time to ask all your questions to the owner.
The whole process was delightful and the owner showed up right on time. They set up the RV just what we wanted it. we definitely had the best time and will definitely be using RV share again.

author: Alex Schmitz
title: Great experience, just one flaw
body:
Great experience, just one flaw:
Overall I was very happy with my experience.  The whole process went smoothly.
One thing I did not understand was the pricing-policy:
After a 10 nights rental period the owner of the trailer and me tried to extend the drop-off-time from 09:00 AM to 03:00 PM for my convenience. Unluckily this resulted in an additional fee of more than 200 USD!! This is absolutely ridiculous! The rental period was about to end at the same day, literally. So how does RVShare dare to assume that it would be another night? 
Last but not least it turned out that extending from 09:00 AM to noon results in 1 Cent additional fee. This is absolutely not understandable to me. I paid a lot of money and I'm sure that RVshare gets a generous cut on every rental agreement, so this pricing policy is not customer-friendly at all!

author: Greg C
title: Late cancellation without notice
body: My first rental was cancelled less than three weeks before my trip due to a mechanical issue with the RV.  The problem was not that it was cancelled, it is that the owner said they notified RVShare more than a month before that (at least seven weeks before my trip!), but I was never notified that it was cancelled.  I had to reach out to the owner with some questions about my trip, and they said it was cancelled over a month before.  This was very frustrating, and could have been easily prevented had RVShare notified me in a timely manner.  As a result, I ended up spending a lot more to get an RV at the last minute.  The one I got was very nice and I had a great experience with the owner.  I left that owner a review and would happily recommend her to anyone

title: Great Experience

author: Last RV Share Experience
title: BAIT & SWITCH
body:
1) I felt that it was kind of a bait and switch with the quality of RV in the photo and what was received.

2) The daily insurance garbage is COMPLETE BS!  A) the owner has an 'insurance charge on their breakdown of fees' & B) RV share makes you get ADDITIONAL COVERAGE MANDATORY for like an additional $250+ to the trip!   AND we were told by the RV owner the one we rented wasn't as new as this other one - 2019 to 2020... So we get the RV and pay for insurance on an RV we're not even driving around on!  The insurance stuff is such a money making scam!

3) There's way too many fees that breakdown the cost - the $170 a day turned into about $500 a day once it was all said & done...

4) PLUS THE RV WAS BEAT TO CRAP... for 2020 build it felt like it has been used for 20 years!  They need to cap owners from renting them after 75,000 miles or so - or at least have a third party review the RV...
reply: Hi Nick, we apologize for the unpleasant experience you had with the RV. We strive to maintain complete transparency with all our platform users about the service or administrative fees they might be charged. When you book with us, we provide a detailed breakdown of the rental price, owner's fees, and all taxes or RVshare fees. Please feel free to contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234, which is available 24/7, if you have any questions related to these fees or if you would like to discuss your experience further.

author: Alyssa S.
title: Never go through RV share...
body: The camper we rented ended up breaking down. We called RV share and they gave us the insurance number. The insurance carrier was a joke. We sat on the side of the highway ( very busy and very dangerous) for three hours all while on the phone with an agent that had no idea what the hell she was doing. Left me on hold upwards of 20 to 30 minutes at a time. She almost towed the camper to a place that restores campers not fix the engine. I had to do my own research to find the correct place that would service the camper. Then I called RV share to find out how they were going to help. Your company was no help. Why the hell would we pay for another camper to rent when we just spent all our money on the one that broke down. Any time I spoke to a representative it just irritated me even more. We ended up having to rent an SUV to get us home. The owner of the camper Daniel was amazing. He communicated with us through the whole ordeal and told us not to worry about anything.
reply: Hi Alyssa, we're sorry to hear about the troubles you faced during your RV rental. We suggest that you contact the RV owner to discuss the possibility of getting a refund for the issues you encountered. If you need any help with this, please feel free to get in touch with us anytime, as we are available 24/7. You can reach us at 1-888-482-0234, or you can chat with us on rvshare.com. Additionally, you can also email us at https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: customer

author: Sue
title: The Air B n B of RVs
body: Our first RV Share experience went very smooth. We choose RV share because we want to eventually buy our own, but wanted to try different sizes and options to see what would work best for us.    We picked out our RV, contacted the owner, (who was awesome, friendly, kind, informative, besides that the RV was spotless and had everything you needed to tow)  we got approval to rent.  Next we got the insurance coverage for the RV through RV Share.  Everything is very simple. We feel the prices were fair, for the rental and the insurance.   The only part I did not like was after we booked the RV I was getting emails from other RV owners trying to get me to rent theirs.  I understand what they are doing, but after our trip was booked and we made a commitment. I did not feel it was necessary to be bothered by other owners.  I think that during the process of renting, having owners with similar RVs would be quite helpful.   We can't wait to go again.  Thank you!!  I tell everyone this company is the Air B n B of Rv's!!

author: James Stevenson
title: My experience with Carson was horrible
body: My experience with Carson was horrible. The rv was not checked out prior which resulted in a generator that kept shutting off, the fresh water want filled so I had to use my drinking water to bathe/use bathroom since there was no water source at camp site, the fridge was moldy and Carson said I would be compensated by being credited money back some money along with my security deposit and he lied. I have not seen a penny and have been back almost 1 month after the 7 to 10 business day I was told via email. I will never use this again or recommend anyone to Carson.

If you would like to dispute the charges associated with this reservation, please contact our Customer Experience team to open a formal dispute. author: Dclare
title: The unit that we wasted our money on…
body:
The unit that we wasted our money on was not ready to rent. The awning was broken, which we were not told about until we got the unit, it was very hot and we were parked in the sun, so the awning was needed very much. The heater/ac switch was broken.  The unit was very dirty, cookware was dirty with food stuck on it,black grease on the pans, the silverware drawer has dirt and what may have been food in it.  The coffee pot, a Keurig, had the descale light on from the day we first used it, halfway through our week, it quit working.  I had to go to town and buy another coffee pot to get us through the week.
The owner charges $100 for a "deep" clean.  Whoever is paying for this service is getting ripped off.
The owner was very good to communicate and actually delivered our unit to us early, set it up, and had the air conditioner going for us when we arrived.
reply: Hello, we're so sorry to hear about your experience with this RV. We recommend reaching out to the RV owner to discuss a potential refund for the issues. If you need help with this, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

reply: We appreciate your feedback regarding the rental pricing. However, it is important to keep in mind that the RV owners themselves are responsible for setting the rental rates and security deposits. If you require further clarification on insurance or would like to discuss this in more detail, please do not hesitate to contact us at any time. I checked on my coverage and unowned towed trailers are covered by my policy, so I was disappointed that I was forced to buy a redundant policy. I would use RV Share more if I could use my insurance.

author: Lisa Dornbusch
title: I needed more places for people to sleep!!!!
body: I had more family members coming to stay with me than I had bedrooms for.  We live in a smaller, more rural area where nice hotel rooms are a 20 minute drive away.  RV Share took care of this problem for me by hooking me up with multiple RV owners that not only rent their RVs out to others, but set them up as well.  The people we chose brought their RV to us, set it up with beds fully made as well as providing other staples like towels, dishes, coffee maker, etc.  Then, they came and packed it up and hauled it away when our company left.  Nothing could have been easier.  What a great service!!!

body: The additional fees are bull... after he picked up the rv from the site (delivery only) he charged me $20 for the disposal fee (which is free at the campground) and RVShare tacked on $3 to that for processing fee.... Really?  This was the final straw of the money charged on top of the owner's fee.  We will not be renting again from RVShare
reply: Hello Donna, thanks for reaching out to us. We make every effort to be as transparent as possible with all users of our platform regarding any administrative or service fees they may be subject to. We make the administrative fee on post-trip charges available to all renters at several steps in the booking process. At the time of booking, there is a breakdown of the rental price, any owner’s fees, and any taxes or RVshare fees. If you have any questions regarding these fees, please contact our Customer Experience team at 1-888-482-0234 open 24/7.

title: Ray F. from Customer Servivce
body: Ray F. from the customer service team went over and beyond for us in fixing our account in an effort to avoid us having to cancel and start all over again. Ray understood our dilemma and concerns and accepted the feedback we gave him regarding how the problem occurred in the first place and hopefully some changes will be made (to the account creation process) as a result.  He took ownership for our issue and even followed up the next day.  He was professional and has great communication skills. It's rare to find an employee who "thinks outside the box" in order to help a customer. He is an asset to your team and because of Ray, we will likely use your website in the future (we were not going to before our phone call to him).  So, he retained a customer!

author: Joseph S
title: Poor
body:
here is a copy of my dispute form, trip was horrible, RVShare did not help much. We will use another service moving forward.
RV rental condition was not "as advertised" in many ways, plus a few other unacceptable issues.
1. Expired Inspection, which is both unsafe and illegal. We noticed this after departure. If we were to be pulled over, or worse in an accident caused by neglected repairs this could have been very bad. (Unacceptable, see attached pic "Expired Inspect.jpg")
2. Upon flushing the toilet and/or using the sink water the stench that filled the RV was unbearable. This made the bathroom toilet, shower, sink and kitchen sink unusable for the duration of the trip. (Unacceptable)
3. We had rain forecasted during our days at camp so the awning feature advertised was very very important in our decision to choose this particular RV. Awning was broken off so we were left with out the option for shelter to sit outside during the rain. (Not "as advertised", see attached pics "No_Awning1.jpg", "No_Awning2.jpg" and "No_Awning3.jpg")
4. We chose not to sign-off on the Departure Form, per the advise given to us by customer service upon calling RVShare to discuss our situation after we headed out on our trip. As you will see in attached pics, the images sent by owner on the departure form do not accurately represent the RV we drove away with (Awning is shown intact, as well as some of the pics were direct copies from the initial rental posting, they were not current or taken the day of departure, as it should have been). See attached screenshots from the departure form, "DepartureForm1.jpg" and "DepartureForm2.jpg".
5. Lastly in general we feel the RV advertisement was very misleading and we did not receive what we expected we were going to get. You can see in the advertisement screenshots "Misleading1.jpg" (there was no backup camera and radio was not functioning). Plus other examples in "Misleading2.jpg", "Misleading3.jpg" and "Misleading4.jpg". 
6. The pics "Busted1.jpg", "Busted2.jpg" and "Busted3.jpg" are some additional shots taken by us of the actual RV and its current condition.

author: Ivana Rizzi
title: Absolutely to avoid…
body:
Absolutely to avoid…

We rented with RVShare for a 8 days trip in Arizona.
2 days before the rental, while we were already on holiday, unfortunately the owner tells us that the RV rented is not available!!!
RVshare did not help us at all in this emergency situation since communication with them was extremely hard and multiple people were involved and never was there appropriate follow-up.
So we had to find another RV through the RVshare circuit on our own and since it was July 28th it wasn't easy at all!!!! We have spent the whole two days in Las Vegas looking for a new RV without any support by RVshare!!! 
 The cost of the new RV, although equivalent to the previous one, was 50% higher!!!

Once the RV was returned, the Owner of the RV was very ambiguous and did not want in any way, despite our pressing requests, to check the condition of the RV. After 2 days RVShare and Digisure (the insurance company linked to RVShare) without any notice withdrew $1,500 from our credit card notifying us of a claim to the roof of the RV!!!. We have had absolutely NO ACCIDENTS!!!!
Unfortunately, we could not prove it because we did not shoot any pictures to the roof of the RV when we rented it. The roof of the RV is 13.5 feet above the ground!!!
We asked RVshare to send us photos of the roof to prove that it was intact before the rental but they were never provided.
Despite numerous calls and e-mails to the references above, nothing has happened to solve the said issue.
This is clearly a fraud!!! 

Referring back to the claim RVSUS3233914, we demand reimbursement for the amount they charged us without referring to us first.

I would recommend Camping World, even though I've never used them!
reply: Hi Eileen, the way we handle security deposits is by placing a "hold" on your account for the amount of the security deposit. When that happens, your bank creates a pending transaction but never actually processes that payment. Because of that you were never actually charged that money, you won’t see a refund in your account. Instead, that original pending transaction will either say that it was voided, or it will have disappeared altogether. If you have any further questions, you can reach us 24/7 at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

title: stop

There is also an “Unsubscribe” link at the bottom of any marketing emails. If you need any assistance with this, you can reach us by phone at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us through our website rvshare.com, or send us an email at https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport. author: Candice Corbin
title: A company that cares about you
body:
What could have turned into a complete disaster became the reason I trust LensRentals more, because their team stepped in quickly and took responsibility when it mattered.

My large rental order was not delivered on time as a fedex driver didnt knock on my door and just left a notice saying they missed me, it was captured on camera, not having it arrive on time caused real stress, lost preparation time, and financial loss before a professional job. Because the gear arrived late, I did not have the proper time I normally need to inspect everything, test it, organize it, and prepare calmly.

That said, LensRentals’ team took the situation seriously. They jumped in, the same day,  took responsibility for the loss, and overnighted what was needed so I did not miss the entire rehearsal day. That mattered a lot.

Even though the delay created real problems for me, the way their team responded built trust. They put the customer first in the best way they could under the circumstances, and took care of what was needed to cover what i lost financially and  because of that, I would feel comfortable using them again.

author: Christian von Delius
title: I was looking for a DJI Avata drone
body: I was looking for a DJI Avata drone, and used was acceptable, if it came with all the parts (battery, props and was unbound) for a reasonable price. Going blind to a website, I have seen a number of well done scam sites, but after evaluating Lens Rentals website, because they offered rentals and used items, I decided to take a chance. The drone was competitively priced, included spare props, and a battery, I was shocked when it arrived, WELL packaged, quickly, in the original RETAIL box, with the accessories package (screws, allen wrench, a L and R prop, charge cradle, cable and original charger, missing only the user manual. I already had DJI FPV Goggles V2 and an original Motion Controller, so I specifically said, 'if this is 'unbound', do not ship it'; I never heard anything, back, so when it arrived quickly, I set about to bind it to the Goggles, which it showed bound, but gave me the option to 'unbind' (from the previous owner', which it did, bound immediately to the Goggles, and then the Controller, so I was able to immediately go out and teach myself to fly it successfully. I want to say I am VERY happy with Len's Rentals and will certainly go there for future drone and photography needs. 20 Stars. You can rest assured these guys are a quality operation. Love that I received the retail box. Third flight  video here in NW Montana: youtube v=vklkWEpNP_s Insanely good 4K footage.

author: Aypawn Media
title: Customer of 3 years
body: I've used Lens Rentals for over 3 years and they have treated me like family. I have actually never rented from them, but I've purchased everything from cinema cameras to cheap SD cards. I do need to call attention to why people do not agree with their grading. They only have 4 tiers, and I've purchased items that are "working" but look absolutely terrible. Reach out to them if you have issues they've gone above and beyond for me for my own mistakes when I contacted them. When I'm in need of professional used gear I always go to Lens Rentals first.

author: Kirigia
title: Absolutely horrible!
body:
Absolutely horrible!

I've seen mostly good reviews about this company, and they have some really competitive pricing. However, if you're a first-time buyer, forget it!

They wait 30 minutes after an order is placed, then ask you for the most random information, like a website. Then they come back an hour later and say my information is fake, and they want a $4,000 deposit!!!

I mean, at that point, you may as well just buy the camera outright, brand new! Also, if you're renting a camera, it's obvious you won't have the money to pay that deposit. What a scam this company is. I definitely will not be working with them.

I’m absolutely pissed save your self mental energy this company IS TERRIBLE. I’m in deep regret now because I just purchased a used camera from them a few days prior now I’m afraid something is wrong with it because of how terrible there quality control is.

author: AJ
title: Charged for a cable that was never in the kit
body: I rented a gimbal, it didn’t have the cord I needed to connect the accessories to my camera (no problem I bought one of my own). However they ended up charging me $20 for some cable they said was in the kit that I didn’t send back which is absolute nonsense because I meticulously kept track of everything that was in that case for that specific reason, that I didn’t want to get charged for something. Go with another rental company!

author: Chad Palmer
title: Disappointed After Past Good Experience
body:
I’ve had solid experiences with Lensrentals in the past, but this latest one was a letdown.

I ordered a flash with a one-day buffer before my shoot—still arrived late. Frustrating, but I decided to keep the flash and went through their “keeper” purchase process. Got the quote, paid it, tossed the return box, and moved on.

Weeks later, I get an email saying I’m being charged $75 for not returning the generic hard case (Pelican-style). That case was never mentioned as part of the keeper quote. No warning, no heads-up—just a surprise charge.

I reached out and offered to return the case if they sent me a box. Instead, I got a response from Kelsey in customer service citing the rental contract and threatening additional charges. I said I’d be contacting my credit card company to dispute the charge. Thankfully, another rep stepped in and agreed to remove the fee while waiting for the case return.

Bottom line: if you’re buying gear through their keeper program, be warned—the case isn’t included, and they won’t tell you upfront. It’s a hidden charge that feels like a trap. If you’re just renting, this won’t affect you. But if you plan to buy, make sure you clarify what’s actually yours.

Disappointed to see this kind of shady practice from a company I used to trust. Do better, Lensrentals.

author: Justin McLaughlin
title: They are so invasive -- they demand an…
body: They are so invasive -- they demand an explanation for your address (did you move recently, is this where you're working), information about unrelated work and business accounts, all of your social media, everything. It's like they're an invasive data broker not a rental company. There are better alternatives out there.

author: yz
title: They ask for your work information
body: They ask for your work information, where you work and your title, work phone number etc. Isn't ID sufficient and you want to contact our workplace for a personal rental? They ask for social media and other info as well. It's getting a little personal and uncomfortable, especially I received several calls after filling out their online forms and entered card information--everything should be settled at that point. The fact that they called so many times is annoy.

author: Matt Gelfman
title: From a Professional Wedding Photographer Who Uses LensRentals Almost Every Week
body: As a professional wedding photographer, I've used LensRentals as my main source to rent lenses and cameras on a regular basis. I've never once had a negative experience with them—on the contrary, they've always been extremely efficient, the equipment has always been delivered in terrific conditions, and on occasions in which I had to make any changes to an order, their customer service has always been perhaps the most prompt and friendly I have ever communicated with. I can't recommend this company and their services enough!

author: Jena Parram
title: **BEST** customer service
body:
I’ve used Lensrentals multiple times and it’s always an easy experience. Their equipment is always in great condition, but what truly sets them apart is their customer support.

My package is currently lost in transit a day before an important photoshoot. I have been STRESSING OUT all day and their team immediately stepped in and offered to ship a replacement overnight so I wouldn't miss the shoot. Jose and Jay were incredibly helpful and kind! It’s rare to find a company that cares this much to keep their customers happy!!

I can’t recommend them enough. This type of support is exactly why I’ll keep coming back!! Thank you so much again. Really appreciate it.

author: Terence Donnelly
title: Sometimes it's better to rent than to buy something to find out later you don't need it!
body: I've rented multiple time from this company and I have never had any issues with them.  The equipment is in working order, customer service is always easy to reach and mailing has never been an issue at all.  I plan on using them especially if I want to try out a piece of gear that I am not sure if I want or need.  I just rented the Sony 300mm F 2.8 G lens and man I just wanted to use it not want to go out and buy one now.  I wish I had the money for one of them because I would.

author: e stanton
title: Worst customer service ever
body: I have made several contacts to them for a quote on a camera /lens rental. Besides endless phone waiting time, and uninformed associates who do nothing but chat about nonsense, I finally got Letitia who would not answer my equipment questions, and wanted to chat in a "woke" manner and did not understand that the reason for my call was business, not to chat aimlessly,  and,  that I needed info to make a plan about a possible rental. When she took the call after a long hold time, and I asked about a camera body, she then complained that I did this. What?   Isn't that why I was calling?  She refused to give me a manager, continued to argue and make rude and off base comments and hung up on me. In calling back, to hopefully get a better contact, I got a continued runaround. Not sure what their issue is but they were able to discuss business in the past, unlike the current contact events.  If they cannot discuss info in a business like manner, I would think that renting from them would be unwise.

author: Kat Alamed
title: Do not pay extra for the insurance
body: Do not pay extra for the insurance they try to sell you. If your item is lost, stolen, or damaged. Lens rentals will not honor the insurance you paid for. They will immediately charge your card for the full cost of the item.

author: Kara
title: The only rental co worth using
body: I’ve rented from this company 10x now and can’t recommend them enough. They have a seamless process through FedEx and unparalleled customer service. They even were able to mail my rental lens to Shutters on the Beach when it was clear it wouldn’t make it to Michigan in time. They are super flexible, friendly, and always have everything in stock. This is the only place to go!

author: Arielle Jackson
title: I rented some portable microphones for…
body: I rented some portable microphones for a conference I was going to. However, when leaving the conference, I misplaced a bag and lost their item as well as my camera and other things. I reached out to them to let them know that I had lost the item and they told me that if I didn’t continue paying for the rental, they would send it to collections. I asked if I could just pay for it in some sort of payment plan as the item is expensive and I cannot afford to pay for it out right or I would have just purchased my own. They then began to threaten to call the police and report a theft when I clearly stated to them that I lost the item. They then told me to pay them more than double of what the item was worth for them to replace a brand new item. Which I understand business so if they wanted to upcharge that was fine again, I asked if I can make the payment and some type of arrangement over the next couple of weeks and they declined. For weeks has been a constant back-and-forth with them continuously, threatening to make a police report against me when they could have already taken several payments to clear this matter. The customer service has been horrible with consistent threats and no resolution when I have offered several options to pay for the item. If I intended to steal their item I wouldn’t be offering to pay for it. They also told me to go get a personal loan to pay for the item and was upset that I declined doing so they refuse to take payments and keep threatening me with the police.

author: Gil Moonzo
title: My $ 100 PLC got stuck on a $15…
body:
My $ 100 PLC got stuck on a $15 protective UV filter. I called to ask how much to just pay for the protective filter so I can keep using my PLC. They told me, oh don't worry about it we have the tools to separate them, and we will return your PLC . So upon Lenrentals advice I returned it... again, NOT what I was originally asking for them to do. Lenrentals idea... so I get and "good news" email. So glad I didn't have to pay for your gear that I basically asked to buy so my filter was still mine. Give me a break!

Hey,

The good news is that there was no damage to our gear once they got that filter off, so no repairs cost here!

Unfortunately, it does not look like the repairs team was able to remove that filter in good enough condition to send back.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

author: C K
title: They are interest on you
body:
Once you submit the order, they will ask the following questions.

--------------------------------------------------
1. Current employment information (employer, job title, and perhaps a work phone number)
2. Official work or school email address (Don’t worry! We’ll only email you there once to confirm your order.)
3. Links to your personal or business websites (If it's photo/video-related, great! If not, that's fine too!)
4. Links to any social media pages where you might share photo/video (Instagram, YouTube, Facebook, Vimeo, etc.)
5. Our address verification was unable to link you to the SHIPPING address provided. Would you provide a brief explanation for that address? (e.g. moved recently, business address, shoot location, etc.)
--------------------------------------------------

Not sure why they need this information for the rental. It is more likely a personal data collecting agency with gear rental project as a side job.

author: Mikey
title: Customer service was persistent to make…
body: Customer service was persistent to make things right. Some of the best customer service I ever received anywhere, ever. They have a huge range of gear, which is both the benefit and drawback of their services. Quality and condition widely varies, the customer service, pricing, timeliness are top in the industry and one of the only good choices for online rentals and used equipment.

author: Courtney Kerns
title: Always a great experience
body: I have rented from LensRentals many, many times and always had the best experience. The order is always on time and correct, and it’s a piece of cake to return the gear. The customer service is fantastic! It’s very easy to talk to someone on the phone or get email support from an actual person. I just had a major problem which was the fault of UPS, and they communicated extremely well as we resolved it. Don’t ever change that top-notch customer service! Highly recommend.

author: B
title: Predatory business model
body: If you value your privacy or personal information, please look elsewhere for your rentals. Shortly after submitting your order, you will receive an email saying that your order will only be fulfilled if you provide an exhaustive list of personal information. Specifically; employment info, work or school email, links to personal or business websites, and links to any/all of your social media pages. There is no request for deposit or anything like that. So it was not a question of a financial guarantee for the safe return of their equipment. But a deliberate effort to extort personal information from their customers.  Your order is then withheld until you provide this personal info.

author: Kayla Davis
title: Despicable company, sends out multiple defective units and then hesitates on your right to a refund.
body: If they had it their way, I would be paying for absolutely nothing besides an exorbitant amount of hassle and emailing back and forth ON MY VACATION, due to them sending faulty GoPro, after faulty GoPro. I will NEVER use this company again and I will go out of my way to bring them up in conversations simply to steer any one else clear of using them as well. They are clearly profit driven and could not care less about providing a quality rental product to their “customers”.

author: Caleb
title: Best place to rent camera gear!
body: Literally the best place to rent equipment that is in great condition! They ship so fast and make the process so easy. I needed to rent equipment for photographing my first wedding, and I'm still blown away by how awesome lensrentals.com is!

author: MaggieLeft
title: Best rental for photo and video gear I know
body: ALWAYS ON TIME, ALWAYS HELPFUL, ALWAYS GOOD GEAR. Can't recommend this company enough!!

author: CR
title: Save your time and don't bother
body: I just tried to place a rental with them. They do not want people new to the field. They demand a portfolio of your past work before they rent to you. What a waste of time.

author: Miller
title: Horrible company
body:
Horrible company. 

They take your credit card information and THEN email you asking for even MORE information. Enough to steal your identity. Stay away!

author: Steve S
title: Literally the best ever camera rental company.
body:
I've been renting camera gear since the days of film. There has never been a better option than Lensrentals. Not only do they consistently have the best gear available, they make the rental process completely pain-free. Ah-mazing. And on top of that they are super helpful and customer-service oriented. These folks are the ones you want in your corner when you desperately need a piece of gear quick.

My tip: Don't rent from anyone else.
My pro tip: Get the longer rental, they make it affordable and you won't have to stress about shipping snafus (omg, so critical) plus you'll have some extra time to play.

author: Webe Mouvin
title: My go to for high-end camera equipment
body: Renting camera equipment is an essential part of a photography business, and Lensrentals.com has become my go-to company. The ordering process is really efficient, even for renting high value equipment to be shipped to an assignment location, and they are partnered with FedEx Office for both picking up and returning equipment if you are on the road. I've never had a problem with inoperative, dirty or damaged equipment.

author: Kenny On the Gulf Coast
title: In LA in June 2023
body:
In LA in June 2023.  Tried to rent from two separate people and could not get anyone to respond. 
Ended going to cinema camera rentals in LA.  Great place. Will probably not use kitsplit again. 
I don’t have time for that. 
I’m responsive to clients and I expect rental folks to be responsive.

author: Agape Lataillade
title: I've been having major problems with…
body:
I've been having major problems with KitSplit ... a year ago I had a rental go bad, resulting a $10k+ claim, $8k+ of which they still owe me. It is impossible to reach anyone at the "company". No phone numbers exist. I email the customer service platform and might finally get a brief response after sending multiple messages for months, and my questions are always ignored.

I have never experienced such a lack of communication and information from a company before. 

It’s been 1 year now since the rental took place. I was told LAST MAY by Andi Avery & Stephanie Morris that I would received MONTHLY payments of $1k until paid in full. I received 1 payment, and then NOTHING until November. After reaching out dozens of more times, the co-founder, Ken Amarit, messaged me an sent 1 more "monthly" payment to me via Zelle in November. NOTHING in December, NOTHING in January, and no one will respond again. I HAVE ONLY RECEIVED 2 “monthly” payments since May. 

Kitsplit has been frustrating & unethical to say the very least. I won't stop reaching to them and calling out their illicit and crooked business practices until I'm paid what I am owed in full.

author: default
title: Your camera will never be returned they…
body: Your camera will never be returned they will never reimburse you. No point in letting steal your equipment. They call it voluntary separation.

author: Brandon Osterman
title: Owners beware of Kitsplit and Thimble…
body: Owners beware of Kitsplit and Thimble Insurance! Renting on Kitsplit cost me a $4000 camera kit when their renter lost my camera's viewfinder, Thimble denied the renter's claim and Kitsplit failed to intervene. Rent elsewhere. There are much safer options for equipment sharing.

body: My equipment was stolen from a KitSplit user and they are refusing to reimburse me. Very shady company.

author: client
title: Reasonable price/quality
body: The car got us where we wanted, it is super convenient to have a designated parking space and not have to look around for a place when returning. It was our first time with a baby and we had enough place for the stroller (using the baby car seat) and our things. I had absolutely not anticipated that have 4 doors would be more convenient. Also, I think car might have done some noises but I have no idea. Having everything manual was great because you don’t need to look for the boutons or how to do stuff. The usb connection on the lighter unfortunately didn’t work and the phone fell from the support most of the times. These of course, were “extras” so I wouldn’t complain. It could have used some cleaning before the rental both on the inside and outside.

author: Ulla Törnroos
title: No substantive response to damage case since January
body:
I have been trying since January 2026 to obtain a substantive response from Getaround regarding a €3,300+ clutch and dual-mass flywheel repair after repeated rentals of my vehicle through the platform.
Despite multiple contacts via the app, phone support and email — including to the legal department — I have not received any response beyond automated replies and references to the standard damage compensation procedure, which does not apply in this case.
I have been renting out my car through Getaround for several years and therefore find this lack of response particularly disappointing, especially in light of the recent announcements regarding Getaround’s European expansion and strengthening of its user community.
I still hope the matter can be reviewed properly by a responsible person.
